questions.question: A number which can be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is called a ________.
questions.explanation: Every rational number is in the form of {tex}p\over q{/tex}where q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0

questions.question: ________ = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} a = a for any rational number a.
questions.explanation: Any number multiplied with 1 is the number itself

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{7} + \left( { - \frac{6}{{11}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{{21}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{22}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}3\over7{/tex} + (-{tex}6\over11{/tex}) + (-{tex}8\over21{/tex}) + {tex}5\over22{/tex}
=[({tex}3\over7{/tex})+(-{tex}8\over21{/tex})] + [(-{tex}6\over11{/tex}) + {tex}5\over22{/tex}]
=[{tex}3\times3-1\times8\over21{/tex}] +[{tex}-6\times2+1 \times5\over22{/tex}]
=[{tex}9-8\over21{/tex}  ]+ [{tex}-12+5\over22{/tex}]
={tex}1\over21{/tex}-{tex}7\over22{/tex}
=[{tex}1\times22-7\times21\over462{/tex}]
=[{tex}22-147\over462{/tex}]
= {tex}-125\over462{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation: Both 1st part i.e = {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 and 2nd part i.e = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} the number( {tex}—1\over5{/tex})  is multiplied by 1 and 1 is said to be the multiplicative identity of rational numbers.

questions.question: The product of two rational numbers is always a _______.
questions.explanation: The product of two rational numbers is always a rational number as, if we multiply any two rational numbers the product is a rational number ( with the exception of 0)

questions.question: Find: {tex}\frac{2}{5} \times \frac{{ - 3}}{7} - \frac{1}{{14}} - \frac{3}{7} \times \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{2\over5}\times{/tex}{tex}-3\over7{/tex}—{tex}1\over14{/tex}—{tex}{3\over7}\times{/tex}{tex}3\over5{/tex}
= {tex}3\over7{/tex}[{tex}-2\over5{/tex}—{tex}3\over5{/tex}]—{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}3\over7{/tex}[{tex}-2-3\over5{/tex}] —{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}3\over7{/tex}[{tex}-5\over5{/tex}]—{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-3\over7{/tex}—{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-3\times2-1\times1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-6-1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-7\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-1\over2{/tex}

questions.question: Tell what property allows you to compute {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \left( {6 \times \frac{4}{3}} \right)=\left( {\frac{1}{3} \times 6} \right) \times \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation: The answer is associative property of multiplication  as the product follows the associative property of multiplication rule which is  {tex}a\times (b\times c){/tex}= {tex}(a\times b)\times c{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3} \times ( - 3) = (- 3) \times \frac{{ - 1}}{3} = 1{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The property used here is both reciprocal and commutativity under multiplication. Reciprocal because both (-3) and its reciprocal {tex}—1\over 3{/tex}are multiplied. Commutative under multiplication because it follows the rule: a {tex}\times{/tex} b = b {tex}\times{/tex} a

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3} \times ( - 3) = 1{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The answer is reciprocal. Because (-3) and its reciprocal {tex}—1\over 3{/tex}are multiplied to get the answer.

questions.question: For any three rational numbers x, y and z, x + (y + z) = ________.
questions.explanation: For any three rational numbers x , y and z,  x + (y + z) = (x + y) + z, as rational numbers are associative under addition.

questions.question: ________ = a for any rational number a.
questions.explanation: The answer is a {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = a, as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers that is, any number multiplied by 1 gives the same number as a product.

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{7} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{14}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{{21}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{21}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}3\over 7{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over 14{/tex})] + [( {tex}-8\over 21{/tex}) + {tex}5 \over 21{/tex}]
= [{tex}3 \times2 + 1 \times (-5)\over 14{/tex}] + [{tex}-8 + 5 \over 21{/tex}]
= [{tex}1\over 14{/tex}] + [{tex}-3\over21{/tex}]
= {tex}3 \times1 + (-3) \times2\over 42{/tex}
= {tex}3 -6\over42{/tex}
= {tex}-3\over42{/tex}
= {tex}-1\over14{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{4} + \left( { - \frac{5}{2}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{3}} \right) + \frac{5}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}3\over4{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over2{/tex}) + ({tex}-8\over3{/tex})] + {tex}5\over5{/tex}
= [{tex}3 \times3 + ( -5) \times6 + (-8 ) \times 4\over12{/tex}] + 1
= [{tex}9 -30-32\over 12{/tex}] + 1
= {tex}-53\over12{/tex} + 1
= {tex}-53 + 12 \over12{/tex}
= {tex}-41\over12{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{5}{7} + \left( { - \frac{6}{7}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{{35}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{21}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}5\over7{/tex} + {tex}-6\over7{/tex})] + [({tex}8\over 35{/tex}) + {tex}5\over21{/tex}]
= [{tex}5-6\over7{/tex}] + [{tex}-8\times3 + 5\times5\over105{/tex}]
={tex}-1\over7{/tex} + {tex}1\over 105{/tex}
= {tex}-15+1\over105{/tex}
= {tex}14\over105{/tex}
= {tex}-2\over15{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{5} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{12}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{7}{{15}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{20}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\frac{3}{5}+\left(\frac{-5}{12}\right)\right]+\left[\left(\frac{-7}{15}\right)+\frac{5}{20}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac{3 \times 12+(-5) \times 5}{60}\right]+\left[\frac{-7 \times 4+3 \times 5}{60}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac{36-25}{60}\right]+\left[\frac{-28+15}{60}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{11}{60}+\left(\frac{-13}{60}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{11-13}{60}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-2}{60}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-1}{30}{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{7}{8} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{16}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{3}{{16}}} \right) + \frac{5}{8}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}7\over8{/tex} + ({tex}—5\over16{/tex})] + [({tex}—3\over16{/tex}) + {tex}5\over8{/tex}]
= [{tex}7 \times2 + ( —5) \times1\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}—3\times1 + 2 \times5\over16{/tex}]
= [{tex}14—5\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}—3+10\over16{/tex}]
= {tex}9\over16{/tex} + {tex}7\over16{/tex}
= {tex}16\over16{/tex}
= 1

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{5}{9} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{18}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{7}{{18}}} \right) + \frac{7}{9}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}5\over9{/tex} + ({tex}—5\over18{/tex})] + [({tex}—7\over18{/tex}) + {tex}7\over9{/tex}]
= [{tex}5\times2 + (—5) \times1\over18{/tex}] + [{tex}—7\times1 + 2 \times7 \over18{/tex}]
= [{tex}10—5\over18{/tex}] + [{tex}—7+14\over18{/tex}]
= {tex}5\over18{/tex} + {tex}7\over18{/tex}
= {tex}12\over18{/tex}
= {tex}2\over3{/tex}

questions.question: 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is {tex}1\over2{/tex} as any number multiplied by 1 is the same number as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers

questions.question: 0 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is 0 as any number multiplied by 0 gives product 0

questions.question: 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{12}}{{13}}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is {tex}12\over13{/tex} as any number multiplied by 1 gives the same number as a product as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers.

questions.question: 0 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{15}}{{17}}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is 0 as any number multiplied by 0 gives 0 as the product.

questions.question: 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{13}}{{14}}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is {tex}13\over14{/tex} as any number multiplied by 1 gives the same number as a product as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers.

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{15}}{{17}} + \left( { - \frac{{15}}{{17}}} \right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}15\over17{/tex} + ({tex}—15\over17{/tex})
={tex}15\over17{/tex} - {tex}15\over17{/tex}
= 0

questions.question: {tex}\left( { - \frac{{16}}{{19}}} \right) + \frac{{16}}{{19}}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
({tex} -16\over19{/tex}) + {tex}16\over19{/tex}
= {tex}16\over19{/tex} - {tex}16\over19{/tex}
= 0

questions.question: {tex} - \frac{2}{5} \times \left( { - \frac{5}{2}} \right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}-2\over5{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}—5\over 2{/tex})
= {tex}10\over10{/tex}
= 1

questions.question: {tex} - \frac{{17}}{{18}} \times \left( { - \frac{{18}}{{17}}} \right){/tex} = _________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}-17\over18{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}-18 \over17{/tex})
= {tex}306\over306{/tex}
= 1

questions.question: {tex} - \frac{{19}}{{21}} \times \left( { - \frac{{21}}{{19}}} \right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}-19\over21{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}-21\over19{/tex})
= {tex}399\over399{/tex}
= 1

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{18}}{{23}} + \left( { - \frac{{18}}{{23}}} \right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}18\over23{/tex} + ({tex}-18\over23{/tex})
= {tex}18\over23{/tex} - {tex}18\over23{/tex}
= 0

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{13}}{{19}} + \left( { - \frac{{13}}{{19}}} \right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}13\over19{/tex} + ({tex}-13\over19{/tex})
= {tex}13\over19{/tex} - {tex}13\over19{/tex}
= 0

questions.question: Find {tex} - \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{3}{5} + \frac{5}{2} - \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{1}{6}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}-2\over3{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}3\over5{/tex} {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}-{tex}3\over5{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}1\over6{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex} [{tex}—2\over3{/tex}-{tex}1\over6{/tex}] {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex} [{tex}—2 \times2 —1 \times1\over6{/tex}] {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}[{tex}—4—1\over6{/tex}] {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}(-{tex}5\over6{/tex}) {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}—1\over2{/tex} {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}—1+5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}4\over2{/tex}
= 2

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{2}{5} \times \left( { - \frac{3}{7}} \right) - \frac{1}{6} \times \frac{3}{2} + \frac{1}{{14}} \times \frac{2}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}2\over5{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}(—{tex}3\over7{/tex}) —{tex}1\over6{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}3\over2{/tex} {tex}+{/tex} {tex}1\over14{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}2\over5{/tex}

{tex}\frac{-6}{35}-\frac3{12}+\frac2{70}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{-72-105+12}{420}{/tex}

={tex}—11\over28{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{7} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{14}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{9}{{14}}} \right) + \frac{5}{7}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}3\over7{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over14{/tex})] + [({tex}-9\over14{/tex}) + {tex}5\over7{/tex}]
= [{tex}3\times2+ (—5) \times1\over14{/tex}] + [{tex}—9\times1 + 5\times2\over14{/tex}]
= [{tex}6—5\over14{/tex}] + [ {tex}—9+10\over14{/tex}]
= {tex}1\over14{/tex} + {tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}2\over14{/tex}
= {tex}1\over7{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{{11}}{{12}} + \left( { - \frac{5}{6}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{7}{6}} \right) + \frac{7}{{12}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}11\over12{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over6{/tex})] + [({tex}-7\over6{/tex}) + {tex}7\over12{/tex}]
= [{tex}11 \times1 + (—5) \times2\over12{/tex}] + [{tex}(—7) \times 2 +7\times1\over12{/tex}]
= [{tex}11—10\over12{/tex}] + [ {tex}—14+7\over12{/tex}]
= {tex}1\over12{/tex} + {tex}(—7)\over12{/tex}
= {tex}—6\over12{/tex}
= {tex}—1\over2{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{7}{8} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{16}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{9}{{16}}} \right) + \frac{5}{8}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}7\over8{/tex} + (-{tex}5\over16{/tex})] + [(-{tex}9\over16{/tex}) + {tex}5\over8{/tex}]
= [{tex}7\times2 + (—5) \times1\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}(—9) \times1 + 5\times2\over16{/tex}]
= [{tex}14—5\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}—9+10\over16{/tex}]
= {tex}9\over16{/tex} + {tex}1\over16{/tex}
= {tex}10\over16{/tex}
= {tex}5\over8{/tex}

questions.question: If a = 2 and b = 3, then value of {tex}{\left( {\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}} \right)^a}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given , a = 2 , b = 3 so,
{tex}{\left( {\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}} \right)^a}{/tex} = {tex}{\left( {\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{3}} \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {3+ 2}\over6 \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {5}\over6 \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}25\over36{/tex}

questions.question: What can the maximum number of digits be in the repeating block of digits in the decimal expression of {tex}\frac{22}{{7}}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}22\over 7{/tex} = 3.142857142857....
= {tex}3.\overline{142857}{/tex}
So, here the maximum number of digits in the repeating block of digits in {tex}22\over 7{/tex} is 6 digits.

questions.question: Associative property is not followed in ________.
questions.explanation:
The associative property states that you can add or multiply regardless of how numbers are grouped.
By grouping we mean, how you use parenthesis.
This property doesn't hold true for rational numbers. As they are in the form of {tex}{p \over q}{/tex}

questions.question: ________ is the identity for the addition of rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Any rational number added to 0 yields the same number.

questions.question: ________ is the multiplicative identity for rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Any rational number multiplied by 1 will yield the same number.

questions.question: Sum of two rational numbers is a ________.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two rational numbers is a rational number.
For, exampe, {tex}{2 \over 3}{/tex} + {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex} = {tex}{6\over 3}{/tex}

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{9}{8}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
The additive inverse of a number is the number which you add to the given number so that the resultant is zero.
So, Additive inverse of a number is number itself with a negative sign.
So, Additive inverse of {tex}\frac{9}{8}{/tex} is -{tex}\frac{9}{8}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{9}{8}+\frac{-9}{8} = 0{/tex}

questions.question: A number which cannot be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex} \neq{/tex} 0 is called a ________.
questions.explanation: A number which cannot be written in the form of {tex}p\over q{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q is not equal to zero is called an irrational number for example {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}, {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}, ...

questions.question: 0 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is 0 as any rational number  if multiplied by 0 the product becomes 0

questions.question: A number which can be expressed as {tex}\frac {p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q  \ne 0{/tex} is
questions.explanation: A number which can be expressed as ​​{tex}\frac {p}{q}{/tex} where p and q are integers and ​​​​{tex}q  \ne 0{/tex} is a rational number.

questions.question: A number of the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex} is said to be a rational number, if

options.option: p, q are integers and p {tex}\ne{/tex} 0, also q {tex}\ne{/tex} 0

questions.explanation: A number of the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex} is said to be a rational number, if p and q are integers and q {tex}\ne{/tex} 0

questions.question: {tex}-\frac{3}{8}+\frac{1}{7}=\frac{1}{7}+\left(\frac{-3}{8}\right){/tex} is an example to show that -
questions.explanation:
Give, {tex}-\frac{3}{8}+\frac{1}{7}=\frac{1}{7}+\left(\frac{-3}{8}\right){/tex}
Let two rational numbers, {tex}a=\frac{-3}{8}, b=\frac{1}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}a+b=\frac{-3}{8}+\frac{1}{7}=\frac{-21+8}{56}=\frac{-13}{56}{/tex}
and {tex}b+a=\frac{1}{7}+\frac{-3}{8}=\frac{8-21}{56}=\frac{-13}{56}{/tex}
Clearly, a + b = b + a
So, addition is communicative for rational numbers.

questions.question: Which of the following expressions shows that rational numbers are associative under multiplication.
questions.explanation:
given, {tex}\frac{2}{3} \times\left(\frac{-6}{7} \times \frac{3}{5}\right)=\left(\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{-6}{7}\right) \times \frac{3}{5}{/tex} [by associative property under multiplication, {tex}a\times(b\times c)= (a\times b) \times c{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{-18}{35}=\frac{-12}{21} \times \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
So, {tex}a\times(b\times c)= (a\times b) \times c{/tex}
Hence, the given expression shows that rational numbers are associative under multiplication.

questions.question: Zero (0) is

options.option: the identity for multiplication of rational numbers.
the identity for the subtraction of rational numbers.
the identity for the addition of rational numbers.
the identity for the division of rational numbers.

questions.explanation: Zero (0) is the identity for addition of rational numbers. That means, If a is a rational number. Then, a + 0 = 0+ a = a

questions.question: One (1) is:

options.option: the identity for the addition of rational numbers
the identity for the subtraction of rational numbers
the identity for multiplication of rational numbers
the identity for division of rational numbers

questions.explanation: One (1) is the identity for multiplication of rational numbers. That means, If a is a rational number. Then, a.1 = 1.a = a

questions.question: If x + 0 = 0 + x = x, which is rational number, then 0 is called
questions.explanation: We know that, the sum of any rational number and zero (0) is the rational number itself. Now, x + 0 = 0 + x = x, which is a rational number, then 0 is called identity for addition of rational numbers.

questions.question: To get the product 1, we should multiply {tex}\frac{8}{21}{/tex} by:
questions.explanation:
Let we should multiply {tex}\frac{8}{21}{/tex} by x. Then, according to question, {tex}x\times\frac{8}{21}{/tex}= 1
Hence, we should multiply{tex}\frac{8}{21}{/tex} by {tex}\frac{21}{8}{/tex}, for getting the product 1

questions.question: If x is any rational number, then x + 0 is equal to:
questions.explanation:
If x is any rational number,
then x + 0 = x [{tex}\because{/tex} 0 is the additive identity]

questions.question: The reciprocal of any rational number {tex}\frac {p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 is:
questions.explanation: The reciprocal of any rational number {tex}\frac {p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 is {tex}\frac {q}{p}{/tex}.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of the distributive property of multiplication over addition to rational numbers?
questions.explanation:
We know that, the distributive property of multiplication over addition for rational numbers can be expressed as a {tex}\times{/tex} (b + c) = ab + ac, where a, b and c are rational numbers.
Here, {tex}-\frac{1}{4} \times\left\{\frac{2}{3}+\left(\frac{-4}{7}\right)\right\}=\left[-\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{2}{3}\right]+\left[-\frac{1}{4} \times\left(\frac{-4}{7}\right)\right]{/tex} is the example of distributive property of multiplication over addition for rational numbers.

questions.question: A number which can be expressed as {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex} where p and q are integers and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is
questions.explanation:
Rational Numbers - A number which can be written in form of {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}
where p and q are integers and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0.

questions.question: The numerical expression {tex}\frac {3}{8}+(\frac{-5}{7})=\frac{-19}{56}{/tex} shows that

options.option: rational numbers are not closed under addition
addition of rational numbers is not commutative

questions.explanation: In the given expression the addition of two rational numbers is given and the result obtained is also a rational number.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not true?

  1. rational numbers are closed under addition
  2. rational numbers are not closed under subtraction
  3. rational numbers are closed under multiplication
  4. rational numbers are closed under division
questions.explanation: Rational numbers are not closed under division. As, 1 and 0 are the rational numbers but {tex}\frac{1}{0}{/tex} is not defined.

subtopics.name: Properties of Rational Numbers - The role of zero (0) (VIII-1.1)

questions.question: Find the additive identity for the rational number
questions.explanation: The additive identity for the rational number is 0.

questions.question:
Which statement is true?

  1. -5 + 3 {tex}\neq{/tex} 3 + (-5)
  2. {tex}\frac{-8}{12}=\frac{10}{-15}{/tex}
  3. 2 is not natural number
  4. 17 is not prime number
questions.explanation:
By options,

  1. -5 + 3 = -2 and 3 + (-5) = -2, whoch are equal.
  2. {tex}\frac{-8}{12}=\frac{10}{-15}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{-2}{3}=\frac{-2}{3}{/tex}, which are equal.
  3. 2 is not natural number.
  4. 17 is not prime number.

questions.question:
Which of the following is correct?

  1. a + 0 = b
  2. -a {tex}\times{/tex} b = b {tex}\times{/tex} (-a)
  3. a - b = b - a
  4. {tex}\frac{a}{b}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
-a {tex}\times{/tex} b = b {tex}\times{/tex} (-a)
Because multiplication of two numbers in any order are same.

questions.question:
Which of the following properties of rational numbers is shown below?
{tex}\frac{3}{4} \times\left(\frac{7}{3} \times \frac{-4}{5}\right)=\left(\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{7}{3}\right) \times \frac{-4}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation: Associativity of multiplication

questions.question: Divide the sum of {tex}\frac 49{/tex} and {tex}(-\frac 78){/tex} by their product.
questions.explanation:
Sum of {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{-7}{8}=\frac{4}{9}+\left(-\frac{7}{8}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{32-63}{72}=\frac{-31}{72}{/tex}
Product of {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} and {tex}-\frac{7}{8}=\frac{4}{9} \times \frac{-7}{8}=\frac{-7}{18}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{-31}{72} \div \frac{-7}{18}=\frac{-31}{72} \times \frac{18}{-7}=\frac{31}{28}{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{\left(-18 \frac{1}{3} \times 2 \frac{8}{11}\right)-\left(4 \frac{5}{7} \times 2 \frac{1}{3}\right)}{\left|\frac{3}{5}+\left(\frac{-9}{10}\right)\right|+\left|-\left(\frac{-3}{5}\right)\right|}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\frac{\left(-18 \frac{1}{3} \times 2 \frac{8}{11}\right)-\left(4 \frac{5}{7} \times 2 \frac{1}{3}\right)}{\left|\frac{3}{5}+\left(\frac{-9}{10}\right)\right|+\left|-\left(\frac{-3}{5}\right)\right|}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{-55}{3} \times \frac{30}{11}\right)-\left(\frac{33}{7} \times \frac{7}{3}\right)}{\left|\frac{3}{5}-\frac{9}{10}\right|+\left|\frac{3}{5}\right|}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-50-11}{\left|\frac{6-9}{10}\right|+\frac{3}{5}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{-61}{\frac{3}{10}+\frac{3}{5}}=\frac{-61}{\frac{3+6}{10}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-61 \times 10}{9}=\frac{-610}{9}=-67 \frac{7}{9}{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: 14 - [3 + 15{15 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 2(13 - 25)}]
questions.explanation:
14 - [3 + 15{15 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 2(13 - 25)}]
= 14 - [3 + 15{15 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 2(-12)}] = 14 - [3 + 15{15 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 24}]
= 14 - [3 + 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 69] = 14 - 1038 = -1024

questions.question: Find the value of 244 - [13 + 25 {15 {tex}\div{/tex} 3 - (13 - {tex}\overline{24 - 12}{/tex})}]
questions.explanation:
244 - [13 + 25 {15 {tex}\div{/tex} 3 -(13 - {tex}\overline{24 - 12}{/tex})}]
= 244 - [13 + 25 {15 {tex}\div{/tex} 3 - (13 - 12)}] = 244 - [13 + 25 {15 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 1}]
= 244 - [13 + 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 4] = 244 - 113 = 131

questions.question: Evaluate: | 44 - [1 + 5 {12 {tex}\div{/tex} 4 - 2 (1 - {tex}\overline {4-3}{/tex})}] |
questions.explanation:
| 44 - [1 + 5 {12 {tex}\div{/tex} 4 - 2(1- {tex}\overline {4-3}{/tex})}] |
= | 44 - [1 + 5 {12 {tex}\div{/tex} 4 - 2 (1 - 1)}] |
= | 44 - [1 + 5 {12 {tex}\div{/tex} 4 - 0}] | = | 44 - [1 + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3] | = | 44 - 16 | = |28| = 28

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is false?

  1. Every fraction is a rational number
  2. Every rational number is a fraction
  3. Every integer is a rational number
  4. All the above
questions.explanation: Every rational is not a If action eg: 4 is not a fraction

questions.question:
Which of the following is not a rational number?

  1. {tex}\frac{3}{17}{/tex}
  2. {tex}\frac{-4}{19}{/tex}
  3. {tex}\frac{0}{8}{/tex}
  4. {tex}\frac{3}{0}{/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{3}{0}{/tex} is not a rational number.

questions.explanation: Distributive property

questions.explanation: Addition of rational numbers satisfy commutative, associative and closure properties.

{tex}\frac{-7}{5}+\left(\frac{2}{-11}+\frac{-13}{25}\right){/tex} {tex}=\left(\frac{-7}{5}+\frac{2}{-11}\right)+\frac{-13}{25}{/tex}
This property is

Which one of the following is not true?

  1. There does not exist any rational number whose square is 4
  2. There does not exist any rational number whose square is 5

How many of the following four numbers are rational?
{tex}\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}-\sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3} \times \sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3}+\sqrt{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation: Three

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is false?

  1. Natural numbers are commutative for addition.
  2. Whole numbers are commutative for addition.
  3. Integers are not commutative for addition.
  4. Rational numbers are commutative for addition.
questions.explanation: Integers are not commutative for addition

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true?

  1. Natural numbers are commutative for subtraction.
  2. Whole numbers are commutative for subtraction.
  3. Integers are commutative for subtraction.
  4. Rational numbers are not commutative for subtraction.
questions.explanation: Rational numbers are not commutative for subtraction.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true?

  1. Natural numbers are associative for addition.
  2. Whole numbers are not associative for addition.
  3. Integers are not associative for addition.
  4. Rational numbers are not associative for addition.
questions.explanation: Natural numbers are associative for addition.

questions.question:
Which of the following is false?

  1. {tex}\frac{-4}{-5}+0=\frac{4}{5}{/tex}
  2. {tex}\frac{-5}{6}+\frac{-7}{12}=\frac{-7}{12}+\frac{-5}{6}{/tex}
  3. {tex}\frac{8}{9}+\left(\frac{11}{3}+\frac{-2}{3}\right){/tex} {tex}=\left(\frac{8}{9}+\frac{11}{3}\right)+\frac{-2}{3}{/tex}
  4. {tex}\frac{8}{9}-\frac{7}{11}=\frac{7}{11}-\frac{8}{9}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{8}{9}-\frac{7}{11}=\frac{88-63}{99}=\frac{25}{99}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{7}{11}-\frac{8}{9}=\frac{63-88}{99}=\frac{-25}{99}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{8}{9}-\frac{7}{11} \neq \frac{7}{11}-\frac{8}{9}{/tex}

questions.question: The property represented by a + b = b + a is
questions.explanation: commutative property

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is correct?

  1. 0 is called the additive identity for rational numbers.
  2. 1 is called the multiplicative identity for rational numbers
  3. The additive inverse of 0 is zero itself.
  4. All of these
questions.explanation: All the given statements are correct.

questions.explanation: Every rational is not a fraction eg: 4 is not a fraction.

Hence all rational numbers are not fractions.

subtopics.name: Solving Equations having the Variable on both Sides

questions.question: Solve: 2x - 3 = x + 2
questions.explanation:
By transposing numbers and variable both, their sign will change
2x - 3 = x + 2
2x - x = 2 + 3
x = 5

questions.question: Solve for x: 5x + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}x{/tex} - 14
questions.explanation:
5x + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}x{/tex} - 14
By transposing the number and variable both
i.e. taking variables(x) on left hand side and numbers({tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} and 14) on right hand side and changing the signs of transposing values
5x - {tex}\frac{3x}{2}{/tex} = -14 - {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
L.c.m of both sides
{tex}\frac{10x - 3x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-28-7}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{7x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-35}{2}{/tex}
By cancelling 2 both sides
7x = -35 (transpose 7 to other side in next step)
x = {tex}\frac{-35}{7}{/tex} (divide by 7)
x = -5.

questions.question: Solve: 5x - 2(2x - 7) = (3x - 1) + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
5x - 2(2x - 7) = (3x - 1) + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
5x - 4x + 14 = 3x - 1 + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
x + 14 = 3x - 1 + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
3x - x = 14 + 1 - {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
2x = {tex}\frac{{28 + 2 - 7}}{2}{/tex}
2x = {tex}\frac{{30 - 7}}{2}{/tex}
2x = {tex}\frac{{23}}{2}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{{23}}{{2 \times 2}}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{{23}}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 2x + 18
questions.explanation:
3x = 2x + 18
3x - 2x = 18 (Transposing 2x to L.H.S)
x = 18

questions.question: Solve: x = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}(x + 10)
questions.explanation:
x = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}(x + 10)
by transposing 5 to other side, we get
5x = 4(x + 10)
5x = 4x + 40 (opening the bracket and multiplying it by 4)
5x - 4x = 40 (transposing 4x to L.H.S.)
x = 40

questions.question: If 8x - 3 = 25 + 17x, then x is
questions.explanation:
8x - 3 = 25 + 17x
8x - 17x = 25 + 3
-9x = 28
x = {tex}\frac{28}{9}{/tex} ; a rational number

questions.question: If {tex}\frac { 5 x } { 3 } - 4 = \frac { 2 x } { 5 }{/tex}, then the numerical value of 2x - 7 is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac { 5 x } { 3 } - 4 = \frac { 2 x } { 5 }{/tex}
{tex}\frac { 5 x } { 3 } - \frac { 2 x } { 5 }{/tex} = 4
{tex}\frac{25x-6x}{15}{/tex} = 4
19x = 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
x = {tex}\frac{60}{19}{/tex}
hence,
2x - 7
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{60}{19}{/tex} - 7
= {tex}\frac{120}{19}{/tex} - 7
= {tex}\frac{120-133}{19}{/tex}
={tex}- \frac { 13 } { 19 }{/tex}

questions.question: The value of x for which the expressions 3x - 4 and 2x + 1 become equal is
questions.explanation:
When the value of x is 5 then both the expressions will be equal.
Proof
3x - 4 = 2x + 1
x = 5

questions.question: The number of boys and girls in a class is in the ratio 7:5. The number of boys is 8 more than the number of girls. What is the total class strength?
questions.explanation:
let the number of boys and girls = x
ratio = 7 : 5
boys =7x
girls = 5x
According to question,
7x = 5x + 8
By transposing,
7x - 5x = 8
2x = 8
x = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex}
x = 4
now the number of boys = 7x = 28
the number of girls = 5x = 20
total students = 28 + 20 = 48

questions.question: Solve the equation: 2x - 3 = x + 2
questions.explanation:
2x - 3 = x + 2
By transposig both sides
2x - x = 2 + 3
x = 5

questions.question: Solve: {tex}{\text{5}}t-{\text{ 3 }} = {\text{ 3}}t-{\text{ 5}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\text{5}}t-{\text{ 3 }} = {\text{ 3}}t-{\text{ 5}}{/tex}
by transposing both sides
5t - 3t = - 5 + 3
2t = -2
t = -2/2
t = -1

questions.question: Solve the following equation: 4z + 3 = 6 + 2z
questions.explanation:
4z + 3 = 6 + 2z
by transposing both sides
or, 4z - 2z = 6 - 3
or, 2z = 3
or, z = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 2x - 1 = 14 - x
questions.explanation:
2x - 1 = 14 - x
by transposing both sides
2x + x = 14 + 1
3x = 15
by transposing 3 to R.H.S
x = {tex}\frac{15}{3}{/tex}
x = 5

questions.question: Solve: 8x + 4 = 3(x - 1) + 7
questions.explanation:
8x + 4 = 3(x - 1) + 7
or, 8x + 4 = 3x - 3 + 7 (solve bracket first)
or, 8x + 4 = 3x + 4
By transposing both sides
or, 8x - 3x = 4 - 4
or, 5x = 0
or, x = 0

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{2} - \frac{1}{5} = \frac{x}{3} + \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{2} - \frac{1}{5} = \frac{x}{3} + \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
By L.C.M
or, {tex}\frac{(5x - 2)}{10}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(4x + 3)}{12}{/tex}
by cross multiplication
or, 60x - 24 = 40x + 30
by transposing
or, 60x - 40x = 30 + 24
or, 20x = 54
or, x = {tex}\frac{54}{20}{/tex}
in lowest term
or, x = {tex}\frac{27}{10}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{x - 5}}{3} = \frac{{x - 3}}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{x - 5}}{3} = \frac{{x - 3}}{5}{/tex}
by cross multiplication
or, 5(x - 5) = 3(x - 3)
or, 5x - 25 = 3x - 9
by transposing
or, 5x - 3x = -9 + 25
or, 2x = 16
or, x = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex}
or, x = 8

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{3x - 2}}{4} - \frac{{2x + 3}}{3} = \frac{2}{3} - x{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{3x - 2}}{4} - \frac{{2x + 3}}{3} = \frac{2}{3} - x{/tex}
L.C.M on both sides
or, {tex}\frac{(9x - 6 - 8x - 12)}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(2 - 3x)}{3}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{(x - 8)}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(2 - 3x)}{3}{/tex}
by cross-multiply
or, 3x - 54 = 24 - 36x
or, -54 - 24 = -36x - 3x
or, -78 = -39x
or, {tex}\frac{-79}{-39}{/tex} = x
or, 2 = x

questions.question: Solve: {tex}a - \frac{{a - 1}}{2} = 1 - \frac{{a - 2}}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}a - \frac{{a - 1}}{2} = 1 - \frac{{a - 2}}{3}{/tex}
By L.C.M on both sides
{tex}or,\frac{{2a- a+1}}{2} = \frac{{3 - a + 2}}{3}{/tex}
{tex}or,\frac{{a + 1}}{2}=\frac{{5 - a}}{3}{/tex}
By cross-multiply,
or, 3a + 3 = 10 - 2a
by transposing
or, 3a + 2a = 10 - 3
or, 5a = 7
or, a = {tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 3(t - 3) = 5(2t + 1)
questions.explanation:
3(t - 3) = 5(2t + 1
3t - 9 = 10t + 5
10t - 3t = -9 - 5
7t = -14
t = 14 {tex}\div{/tex} 7
t = -2

questions.question: Solve: 15(y - 4) - 2(y - 9) + 5(y + 6) = 0
questions.explanation:
15(y - 4) -2(y - 9) + 5(y + 6) = 0
15y - 60 - 2y + 18 + 5y + 30 = 0
18y - 12 = 0
18y = 12
y = {tex}\frac{12}{18}{/tex}
y = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve the equation: 3x = 2x + 18
Solve: 5x + 9 = 5 + 3x
Solve: {tex}\frac{{2x}}{3} + 1 = \frac{{7x}}{{15}} + 3{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{2x}}{3} + 1 = \frac{{7x}}{{15}} + 3{/tex}
by transposing 
or, {tex}\frac{2x}{3}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{7x}{15}{/tex} = 3 - 1
or, {tex}\frac{10x - 7x}{15}{/tex} = 2
or, 3x = 30
or, x = 10

questions.question: Solve: {tex}2y + \frac{5}{3} = \frac{{26}}{3} - y{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}2y + \frac{5}{3} = \frac{{26}}{3} - y{/tex}
or, 2y + y = {tex}\frac{26}{3}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{5}{3}{/tex}
or, 3y = {tex}\frac{21}{3}{/tex}
or, 3y = 7
or, y = {tex}\frac{7}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 3m = 5m - {tex}\frac{8}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}3m = 5m - \frac{8}{5}{/tex}
or, 3m - 5m = {tex}\frac{-8}{5}{/tex}
or, -2m = {tex}\frac{-8}{5}{/tex}
Divide both sides by (-2), we get,
or, m = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}x + 7 - \frac{{8x}}{3} = \frac{{17}}{6} - \frac{{5x}}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x + 7 - \frac{{8x}}{3} = \frac{{17}}{6} - \frac{{5x}}{2}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{x - 8x}{3}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{5x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17}{6}{/tex} - 7
or, {tex}\frac{(6x - 16x + 15x)}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17 - 42}{6}{/tex}
Eliminating denominator both sides and calculating, we get,
or, 5x = -25
or, x = -5

questions.question: Find two parts of 34 such that {tex}\left(\frac{4}{7}\right)^{\text {th }}{/tex} of one part is equal to {tex}\left(\frac{2}{5}\right)^{\text {th }}{/tex} of the other.
questions.explanation:
Let one part be x.
Then, other part be 34 - x.
According to question, we have
{tex}\frac{4}{7}(x)=\frac{2}{5}(34-x) \Rightarrow \frac{4 x}{7}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{68}{5}-\frac{2 x}{5} \Rightarrow \frac{4 x}{7}+\frac{2 x}{5}=\frac{68}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{20 x+14 x}{35}=\frac{68}{5} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{34}{35} \times x=\frac{68}{5} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{68}{5} \times \frac{35}{34}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 14
{tex}\therefore{/tex} One part = 14 and other part = 34 - 14 = 20

questions.question: The subtraction of 11 times of x from 22 times of y is ________.
Which one of the following is a solution of {tex}\frac {3x - 7}3 > \frac {4x + 2}5{/tex}?
questions.explanation: x > {tex}\frac {41}3{/tex}

questions.question:
Solve for x:
{tex}\frac{x+3}{2}+3 x{/tex} = 5(x - 3) + {tex}\frac{x+23}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x+3}{2}+3 x{/tex} = 5(x - 3) + {tex}\frac{x+23}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x+3+2(3 x)}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{25(x-3)+x+23}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5[x + 3 + 6x] = 2[25x - 75 + x + 23]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35x + 15 = 52x - 104 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 52x - 35x = 15 + 104
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 17x = 119 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 7

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{3}{7}\left(\frac{8 x}{13}-\frac{1}{3}\right)-\left(3 x+\frac{2-x}{7}\right)=\left(x+\frac{3}{5}\right) \div \frac{2}{7}{/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{-2301}{5545}{/tex}

questions.question:
Solve for x:
6(3x + 2) -5(6x - 1) = 6(x - 3) -5(7x - 6) + 12x
questions.explanation:
We have,
6(3x + 2) -5(6x - 1) = 6(x - 3) -5(7x - 6) + 12x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18x + 12 - 30x + 5 = 6x - 18 - 35x + 30 + 12x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = -5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -1

questions.question: If {tex}x=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, then the value of {tex}x+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}x+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{\frac{1}{2}}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{1+2}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{3}}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{\frac{4}{3}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}+\frac{3}{4}=\frac{2+3}{4}=\frac{5}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x-\frac{1}{x-2}=2-\frac{1}{x-2}{/tex}, then x is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}x-\frac{1}{x-2}=2-\frac{1}{x-2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=2-\frac{1}{x-2}+\frac{1}{x-2} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2

questions.question: Solve the following: 0.25(4x - 5) = 0.75x + 8
Solve for x if kx + a = mx + b
questions.explanation:
Given equation kx + a = mx + b
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} kx - mx = b - a [mx is transposed as - mx, a is transposed as - a]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(k-m) = b - a
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{b-a}{k-m}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve for y : {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(3y + 1) - {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (5y + 2) = y - 1
questions.explanation:
Given equation is
{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(3y + 1) - {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex}(5y + 2) = y - 1
To remove fractions, we multiply the equation on both sides by LCM of 2 & 3 i.e
6{tex}{/tex}[{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(3y + 1) - {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex}(5y + 2) = 6 (y - 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(3y + 1) - 2(5y + 2) = 6y - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9y + 3 - 10y - 4 = 6y - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9y - 10y - 6y = -6 + 4 - 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -7y = -5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y ={tex}\frac{5}{7}{/tex}

questions.question: Given that -0.3k + 2.1 = 0.4k, the value of k =
questions.explanation:
-0.3k + 2.1 = 0.4 k
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2.1 = 0.4k + 0.3k
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2.1 = 0.7k
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = {tex}\frac{2.1}{0.7}{/tex} = 3

questions.question: Given that {tex}\frac{-6 p-9}{3}=\frac{2 p+9}{5}{/tex}, the value of p is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{-6 p-9}{3}=\frac{2 p+9}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(-6p - 9) = 3(2p + 9)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -30p - 45 = 6p + 27
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -30p - 6p = 45 + 27
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -36p = 72
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = -2

questions.question: Find the value of n such that {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} (4n -1) - (2n -{tex}\frac{1+n}{3}{/tex}) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} n + {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}(4n - 1) - (2n - {tex}\frac{1+n}{3}{/tex}) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}n + {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}(4n -1) - ({tex}\frac{6 n-1-n}{3}{/tex}) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}n + {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}
2(4n - 1) - (5n - 1) = n + 4
8n - 2 - 5n + 1 = n + 4
(8n - 5n) + (-2 + 1) = n + 4
3n - 1 = n + 4
3n - n = 4 + 1
2n = 5
n = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}+\frac{2 x-3}{35}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5 x-32}{9}-\frac{x+9}{28}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x-4}{3}+\frac{2 x-3}{35}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{5 x-32}{9}-\frac{x+9}{28}{/tex}
Multiplying throughout by 9, we have
3x -12 + {tex}\frac{18 x-27}{35}{/tex} = 5x - 32 - {tex}\frac{9 x+81}{28}{/tex} transposing,
{tex}\frac{18 x-27}{35}+\frac{9 x+81}{28}{/tex} = 2x - 20
Now clear of fractions by multiplying by
5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 or 14
thus 72x - 108 + 45x + 405 = 280x -2800
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2800 - 108 + 405 = 280x - 72x - 45x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3097 = 163
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 19

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{4}+\frac{x}{5}+10000=x{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{4}+\frac{x}{5}+10000={/tex} x
{tex}\frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{4}+\frac{x}{5}-x={/tex} 1000
{tex}\frac{10 x+5 x+4 x-20 x}{20}={/tex} -10000
{tex}\frac{19 x-20 x}{20}={/tex} -10000
{tex}\frac{-x}{20}={/tex} -10000
x = 200000

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{x}{p q}+\frac{x}{q r}+\frac{x}{p r}{/tex} = p + q + r, then x =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{p q}+\frac{x}{q r}+\frac{x}{p r}{/tex} = p + q+ r
{tex}\frac{r x+p x+q x}{p q r}{/tex} = p + q + r
{tex}\frac{\mathbf{x}(p+q+r)}{p q r}{/tex} = p + q + r
x = {tex}\frac{(p+q+r) \times p q r}{(p+q+r)}{/tex}
= pqr

questions.question: Solve the following: (x + 1) + {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (x - 1) = {tex}\frac{5}{12}{/tex}(x - 2)
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (x + 1) + {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (x - 1) = {tex}\frac{5}{12}{/tex} (x - 2)
{tex}\frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{x}{3}-\frac{1}{3}=\frac{5 x}{12}-\frac{10}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{3}-\frac{5 x}{12}=\frac{-10}{12}+\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{6 x+4 x-5 x}{12}=\frac{-10+4-6}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5 x}{12}=\frac{-12}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5 x}{12}=-1{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{-12}{5}{/tex}.

questions.question: Solve the following: 3(5x - 7) + 2(9x - 11) = 4(8x - 7) - 111
The solution for {tex}\frac{2}{x+3}-\frac{4}{x-3}=\frac{-6}{x+3}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2}{x+3}-\frac{4}{x-3}=\frac{-6}{x+3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2(x-3)-4(x+3)}{(x+3)(x-3)}=\frac{-6}{x+3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x-6-4 x-12}{(x+3)(x-3)}=\frac{-6}{(x+3)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-2 x-18}{x-3}{/tex} = -6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x - 18 = -6x + 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 36 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 9.

questions.question: Solve for x: {tex}\frac{6 x-7}{2 x+1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 x+1}{x+5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Given equation is {tex}\frac{6 x-7}{2 x+1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 x+1}{x+5},{/tex} cross multiplying, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (6x - 7) (x + 5) = (3x + 1) (2x + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x2 + 30x - 7x - 35 = 6x2 + 3x + 2x + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 23x - 3x - 2x = 35 + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18x = 36
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{36}{18}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2 is the solution of given equation.

questions.question: How many diagonals does a convex quadrilateral have?
questions.explanation: The two diagonals of a convex quadrilateral are the line segments that connect opposite vertices.

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 5 sides.
questions.explanation: A pentagon is a regular polygon with 5 sides and 5 vertices.

questions.question: Which of the following quadrilaterals has two pairs of adjacent sides equal and diagonals intersecting at right angles?
questions.explanation: kite

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 45o.
questions.explanation:
The measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of n-sides = {tex}\frac{360}{n}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}\frac{360}{n}{/tex} = 45
so, 45n = 360
n = 8
the regular polygon has 8 sides

questions.question: The paper is a model for a ________.
questions.explanation: A paper is a model of a plane surface with no 3-d (three - dimensional) shape.

questions.question: Which of the following quadrilaterals has a pair of opposite sides parallel?
questions.explanation: parallelogram

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 8 sides.
questions.explanation: An octagon is an eight-sided polygon or 8-gon.

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 9 sides.
questions.explanation: A nonagon is a plane figure with nine straight sides and nine angles.

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 10 sides.
questions.explanation: The above picture shows that a decagon is a polygon with 10 sides

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 30o.
questions.explanation:
Number of sides 
n = {tex}{ 360^0 \over 30^0}{/tex}
= 12

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 60o.
questions.explanation:
Number of sides 
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 60^0}{/tex}
= 6

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 90o.
questions.explanation:
Number of sides
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 90^0}{/tex}
= 4

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 15o.
questions.explanation: Number of sides = 360 {tex}\div{/tex} 15 = 24

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 20o.
questions.explanation:
Number of sides = {tex}{ 360^0 \over exterior- angle}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 20^0}{/tex} = 18

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 40o.
questions.explanation:
Number of sides = {tex}{ 360^0 \over exterior -angle}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 40^0}{/tex} = 9

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 10 sides.
questions.explanation:
exterior angle= {tex}{ 360^0 \over n}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 10}{/tex} = 36o

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 20 sides.
questions.explanation:
Exterior angle=  {tex}{ 360^0 \over number -of -sides}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 20}{/tex} = 18o

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 30o sides.
questions.explanation:
Exterior angle= {tex}{ 360^0 \over number -of -sides}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 30}{/tex} = 12o

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 8o sides.
questions.explanation:
Exterior angle= {tex}{ 360^0 \over number- of- sides}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 8}{/tex} = 45o

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 12o sides.
questions.explanation:
Exterior angle= {tex}{ 360^0 \over number- of -sides}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 12}{/tex}= 30o

questions.question: What is the sum of the measures of the angles of a convex quadrilateral?
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD is a convex quadrilateral, then we draw a diagonal AC which divides the quadrilateral into two triangles.
{tex}\angle A + \angle B + \angle C + \angle D{/tex}
{tex} = \angle 1 + \angle 6 + \angle 5 + \angle 4 + \angle 3 + \angle 2{/tex}
{tex} = \angle (1 + 2 + 3) + \angle (4 + 5 + 6){/tex}
We are aware that the total sum of the interior angles of any triangle will be 180o and a quadrilateral is made up of two triangles
Thus, the sum of the interior angles of both the triangles are  {tex}180 +180 = 360 ^\circ {/tex}
So,the sum of the measures of the angles of a convex quadrilateral is 360o

questions.question: How many sides are there in a rectangle?
questions.explanation: A rectangle is a four-sided flat shape where every angle is a right angle (90o). Each internal angle is 90o opposite sides are parallel and of equal length (so it is a Parallelogram).

questions.question:
How many vertices are present in a rectangle?
Vertex (plural-vertices) is the angular point of rectangle. Since in rectangle 4 angles are formed which indicate the presence of 4 vertices.
questions.explanation: Here ABCD is a rectangle which has 4 sides, ​​​​​​​4 vertices, ​​​​​​​4 angles. These 4 vertices are formed by the 4 adjacent sides of the rectangle.

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 18o.
questions.explanation:
Number of sides = {tex}{ 360^0 \over exterior -angle}{/tex}
n = {tex}{360^0 \over 18}{/tex} = 20

questions.question: The quadrilateral whose opposite sides are parallel is a ________.
questions.explanation: A parallelogram is a simple quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides. The opposite sides of a parallelogram are parallel and equal in length.

questions.question: The quadrilateral whose diagonals are equal and bisect each other at the right angle is ________.
questions.explanation: A square is a regular quadrilateral which has four equal sides and equal angles each of 90o. The diagonals of a square are also equal and bisect each other at 90o.

questions.question: The ratio of two adjacent sides of a parallelogram is 4:5. If its perimeter is 72 cm, find its adjacent sides.
questions.explanation:
Let, two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are of length 'a' cm and 'b' cm respectively.
Given that, Perimeter of parallelogram = 72 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(a + b) = 72cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b = 36 cm ... (i)
Also, It is given that, a : b = 4:5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = {tex}{4\over5}b{/tex} ... (ii)
From eq. (i) & (ii), we get,
{tex}{4\over5}b{/tex} + b = 36cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4b + 5b = 180 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9b = 180 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 20 cm
putting value of b in eq. (ii), we get,
a = 16 cm.
Thus, two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are 20cm, 16cm.

questions.question: The park in a town is made in the form of a kite. Its perimeter is 90 metres and one side is 10 m more than another side. What are the lengths of all sides?
questions.explanation:
Let, the two different sides of the park be 'a' and 'b' metre respectively.
Given that, Parallelogram of park = 90m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 (a + b) = 90 m{tex}\implies{/tex}a + b = 45 m ... (i)
Also, it is given that,  one side is more than other by 10m, i.e.
a = b + 10 ... (ii)
From eq. (i) & (ii), we get,
b + 10 + b = 45 m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2b = 35 m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 17.5 m
Putting the value of b in eq. (ii), we get,
a = 17.5 + 10 = 27.5 m
Thus, the four sides of the park are 17.5 m, 17.5 m, 27.5 m, 27.5 m.

questions.question: A  polygon with minimum number of sides is __________.
questions.explanation: As we know a polygon is a closed loop formed by line segments. So, to form a closed loop, minimum 3 sides are required and a polygon with 3 sides is known as triangle. Thus, a polygon with minimum number of sides is Triangle.

questions.question: Polygons that have no portions of their diagonals in their exteriors are called ________.
questions.explanation: Polygons that does not have any portion of diagonals in the exterior of polygon are called Convex Polygons.

questions.question: Which property is true for all trapezoids?
questions.explanation: A trapezoid is a 4 sided flat shape with straight sides that has a pair of opposite sides parallel.

questions.question: The length of one diagonal of a rectangle is 6.2 cm, so the length of the other diagonal is ________.
questions.explanation: As we know, the length of the diagonals of rectangle is equal. So, if the length of one diagonal is 6.2 cm then the other diagonal will also be of 6.2 cm.

questions.question: In which quadrilateral only one diagonal is bisected?
questions.explanation:
A quadrilateral is a kite if and only if any one of the following condition is true:

  • Two disjoint pairs of adjacent sides are equal
  • One diagonal is the perpendicular bisector of other diagonal
  • one diagonal is a line of symmetry
  • one diagonal bisects a pair of opposite angles.

questions.question: The diagonals of which quadrilateral bisect each other at right angles?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a square are equal and bisect each other.

questions.question: The sum of the measures of the external angles of any polygon is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let the number of sides of a convex polygon be 'n'.
Let the interior angle be a1, a2, ... an and the corresponding exterior angles are b1, b2, ... ,bn.
We know that the sum of each interior angle and the corresponding exterior angle is 180o.
Hence, a1 + b1 = 180o; a2 + b2 = 180o and so on.
Let the sum of exterior angles of polygon be S1. Also, the sum of the interior angles is (2n-4) {tex}\times{/tex} 90.
Hence, (2n-4) {tex}\times{/tex} 90 + S1 = 180n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S1 = 180n - 180n + 360 = 360o
Thus the sum of the exterior angle of a polygon is 360o

questions.question: All the sides of a regular polygon are ________.
questions.explanation: A regular polygon is a polygon that have sides equal in length and angles are equal.

questions.question: Sum of all interior angles of a parallelogram is ________.
questions.explanation:
Consider a quadrilateral PQRS, Join QS such that PQRS is divided into two equal areas.
Now, consider {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQS, we have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQS + {tex}\angle{/tex}PSQ = 180o ... (i)
Similarly, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}QRS we have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}SQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}R + {tex}\angle{/tex}QSR = 180o ... (ii)
on adding (i) and (ii), we get,
{tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQS + {tex}\angle{/tex}PSQ + {tex}\angle{/tex}SQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}R + {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}Q + {tex}\angle{/tex}R + {tex}\angle{/tex}S  =360o

questions.question: How many diagonals does have in a convex quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: The two diagonals of a convex quadrilateral are the line segments that connect opposite vertices.

questions.question: A parallelogram each of whose angles measures 90o is ________.
questions.explanation: A rectangle is a type of parallelogram in which all the four angles are of {tex}{90^0}{/tex} but the opposite sides are equal.

questions.question: A parallelogram whose all sides are equal is called __________.
questions.explanation: A square is a type of a parallelogram in which all the sides are equal all the four angles are equal and each is of 90o.

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 3 sides.
questions.explanation: A triangle is a closed polygon with three sides.

questions.question: Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram have equal measure. Find the measurement of each of the angles of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
{tex}x+x=180{/tex}(Let an angle be {tex}x){/tex}
{tex}2x=180{/tex}
{tex}x=90{/tex}{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.question: Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram have equal measure. Find the measure of each of the angles of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
Let an angle = x
x + x = 180o (sum of adjecent angle of a parallelogram is 180o)
2x = 180o
x = {tex}{180^0 \over 2}{/tex}
x = 90o

questions.question: For which of the following, diagonals bisect each other?
questions.explanation: We know that, the diagonals of a square bisect each other but the diagonals of kite, trapezium and quadrilateral do not bisect each other.

questions.question: If three angles of a quadrilateral are each equal to 75°, then, the fourth angle is
questions.explanation: Given, three angles of quadrilateral = 75° Let the fourth angle be x°. Then, according to the property, 75° + 75° + 75° + x° = 360°, since sum of the angles of a quadrilateral is 360°. So, 225° + x° = 360° or x° = 360° - 225° = 135° Hence, the fourth angle is 135°.

questions.question: For which of the following figures, all angles are equal?
questions.explanation: In a rectangle, all angles are equal, i.e. all equal to 90o.

questions.question: For which of the following figures, diagonals are perpendicular to each other?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a kite are perpendicular to each other.

questions.question: For which of the following figures, diagonals are equal?
questions.explanation: By the property of a rectangle, we know that its diagonals are equal.

questions.question: Which of the following properties describe a trapezium?

options.option: A pair of opposite sides is parallel
The diagonals are perpendicular to each other

questions.explanation: We know that, in a trapezium, a pair of opposite sides are parallel.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of a parallelogram?

options.option: The diagonals bisect each other at right angles
The diagonals are perpendicular to each other

questions.explanation: We know that, in a parallelogram, opposite sides are parallel.

questions.question: If two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are (5x - 5)o and (10x + 35)o, then the ratio of these angles is
questions.explanation:
We know that adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary, i.e. their sum equals 180o.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (5x - 5) + (10x + 35) = 180o
= 15x + 30o = 180o
= 15x = 150o
= x = 10o
Thus, the angles are (5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 - 5) and (10 {tex}\times{/tex}10 + 35), i.e. 45o and 135o
Hence, the required ratio is 45o : 135o, i.e. 1 : 3.

questions.question: A quadrilateral whose all sides are equal, opposite angles are equal and the diagonals bisect each other at right angles is a ________.
questions.explanation: We know that, in rhombus, all sides are equal, opposite angles are equal and diagonals bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: A quadrilateral whose opposite sides and all the angles are equal is a
questions.explanation: We know that, in a rectangle, opposite sides and all the angles are equal.

questions.question: A quadrilateral whose all sides, diagonals, and angles are equal is a
questions.explanation: These are the properties of a square, i.e. in a square, all sides, diagonals and angles are equal.

questions.question: If the adjacent sides of a parallelogram are equal, then parallelogram is a
questions.explanation:
We know that, in a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal.
But according to the question, adjacent sides are also equal.
Thus, the parallelogram in which all the sides are equal is known as rhombus.

questions.question: If the diagonals of a quadrilateral are equal and bisect each other, then the quadrilateral is a
questions.explanation: Since, diagonals are equal and bisect each other, therefore it will be a rectangle.

questions.question: The sum of all exterior angles of a triangle is
questions.explanation:
We know that, the sum of exterior angles, taken in order of any polygon is 360° and triangle is also a polygon.
Hence, the sum of all exterior angles of a triangle is 360°.

questions.question: Which of the following is an equiangular and equilateral polygon?
questions.explanation: In a square, all the sides and all the angles are equal. Hence, square is an equiangular and equilateral polygon.

questions.question: Which one of the following has all the properties of a kite and a parallelogram?
questions.explanation:
Two pairs of equal sides.
Diagonals bisect at 90°.
One pair of opposite angles are equal.
In a parallelogram
Opposite sides are equal.
Opposite angles are equal.
Diagonals bisect each other.
So from the given options, all these properties are satisfied by rhombus.

questions.question: The angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 1 : 2 : 3 : 4. The smallest angle is
questions.explanation:
Let the angles of the given quadrilateral be x°, 2x°, 3x° and 4x°.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x°+ 2x° + 3x° + 4x° = 360° [{tex}\because{/tex} sum of the angles of a quadrilateral is 360°]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 360°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{360^{\circ}}{10}=36^{\circ}{/tex}
Hence, the smallest angle = 36°

questions.question: A quadrilateral has three acute angles. If each measures 80°, then the measure of the fourth angle is
questions.explanation:
Let the fourth angle be x, then 80° + 80° + 80° + x = 360° 
[{tex}\because{/tex} sum of all the angles of quadrilateral is 360°]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 240° + x = 360°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 360° - 240°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 120°

questions.question: The number of sides of a regular polygon where each exterior angle has a measure of 45° is
questions.explanation:
We know that, the sum of exterior angles taken in an order of a polygon is 360°.
Since, each exterior angle measures 45°, therefore the number of sides
{tex}=\frac{\text { Sum of exterior angles }}{\text { Measure of an exterior angle }}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{45^{\circ}}=8{/tex}

questions.question: In a parallelogram PQRS, if {tex}\angle{P}{/tex} = 60°, then other three angles are
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{P}{/tex} = 60°

Since, in a parallelogram, adjacent angles are supplementary,
{tex}\angle P+\angle Q=180^{\circ} \Rightarrow 60^{\circ}+\angle Q=180^{\circ}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \angle Q=120^{\circ}{/tex}
Also, opposite angles are equal in a parallelogram.
Therefore, {tex}\angle R=\angle P=60^{\circ}, \angle S=\angle Q=120^{\circ}{/tex}
Hence, other three angles are 60°, 120°, 120°.

questions.question: If two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are in the ratio 2 : 3, then the measure of angles are
questions.explanation:
Le the angles be 2x and 3x.
Then, 2x + 3x = 180° [{tex}\because{/tex} adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 36°
Hence, the measures of angles are 2x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 36° = 72° and 3x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 36° = 108°

questions.question: If PQRS is a parallelogram, then {tex}\angle{P} - \angle{R}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: Since, in a parallelogram, opposite angles are equal. Therefore, {tex}\angle{P} - \angle{R}{/tex} = 0, as {tex}\angle{P} \ and \ \angle{R}{/tex} are opposite angles.

questions.question: The sum of adjacent angles of a parallelogram is
questions.explanation: By property of the parallelogram, we know that, the sum of adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 180°.

questions.question: Length of one of the diagonals of a rectangle whose sides are 10 cm and 24 cm is
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\Delta{/tex}BCD,
{tex}\angle B D C=90^{\circ}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex}  Using Pythagoras theorem,
we have, BC2 = BD2 + CD2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC2= 102 + 242 = 100 + 576
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC2= 676
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC = {tex}\sqrt{676}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC =26 cm

questions.question: If the adjacent angles of a parallelogram are equal, then the parallelogram is a
questions.explanation: We know that, the adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary, i.e. their sum equals 180° and given that both the angles are same. Therefore, each angle will be of measure 90°. Hence, the parallelogram is a rectangle.

questions.question: Which of the following can never be the measure of exterior angle of a regular polygon?
questions.explanation:
Since, we know that the sum of measures of exterior angles of a polygon is 360°,
i.e. the measure of each exterior angle = {tex}\frac{360^\circ}{n}{/tex}, where n is the number of sides/angles.
Thus, the measure of each exterior angle will always divide 360° completely.
Hence, 22° can never be the measure of the exterior angle of a regular polygon.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true for an exterior angle of a regular polygon with n sides?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle = {tex}\frac{(n-2) \times 180^{\circ}}{n}{/tex}

questions.question: PQRS is a square. PR and SQ intersect at O. Then, {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ is a
questions.explanation:
We know that the diagonals of a square intersect each other at a right angle.
Hence, {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ = 90o, i.e. right angle.

questions.question: A parallelogram PQRS is constructed with sides QR = 6 cm, PQ = 4 cm and {tex}\angle {PQR}{/tex} = 90°. Then, PQRS is a
questions.explanation: We know that, if in a parallelogram one angle is of 90°, then all angles will be of 90° and a parallelogram with all angles equal to 90° is called a rectangle.

questions.question: Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are in the ratio 1 : 5. Then, all the angles of the parallelogram are
questions.explanation:
Let the adjacent angles of a parallelogram be x and 5x, respectively.
Then, x + 5x = 180° [{tex}\because{/tex} adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 30°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The adjacent angles are 30° and 150°.
Hence, the angles are 30°, 150°, 30°, 150° [{tex}\because{/tex} opposite angles are equal]

questions.question: PQRS is a trapezium in which PQ || SR and {tex}\angle{/tex}P = 130°, {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 110°. Then {tex}\angle{/tex}R is equal to
questions.explanation:
Since PQRS is a trapezium and PQ ||SR.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}Q + {tex}\angle{/tex}R =180° [{tex}\because{/tex} angles between the pair of parallel sides are supplementary]
{tex}\angle{/tex}R = 180° -110° = 70°

questions.question: The number of sides of a regular polygon whose each interior angle is of 135° is
questions.explanation:
Ws know that, the measures of each exterior angle of a polygon having n sides is given by {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{n}{/tex}.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The number of sides, n = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{\text { Exterior angle }}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{180^{\circ}-135^{\circ}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} exterior angle + interior angle = {tex}180^{\circ}{/tex}]
{tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{45^{\circ}}=8{/tex}

questions.question: If a diagonal of a quadrilateral bisects both the angles, then it is a
questions.explanation: If a diagonal of quadrilateral bisects both the angles, then it is a rhombus.

questions.question: To construct a unique parallelogram, the minimum number of measurements required is
questions.explanation:
We know that, in a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal and parallel. Also, the opposite angles are equal.
So, to construct a parallelogram uniquely, we require the measure of any two non-parallel sides and the measure of an angle.
Hence, the minimum number of measurements required to draw a unique parallelogram is 3.

questions.question: To construct a unique rectangle, the minimum number of measurements required is
questions.explanation:
Since, in a rectangle, opposite sides are equal and parallel, so we need the measurement of only two adjacent sides, i.e, length and breadth.
Also, each angle measures 90°.
Hence, we require only two measurements to construct a unique rectangle.

questions.question:
Which of the following figures satisfy the following property?
Only one pair of sides are parallel.
questions.explanation: We know that, in a trapezium, only one pair of sides are parallel and we can observe that figure P resembles a trapezium.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of obtuse angles that a quadrilateral can have?
questions.explanation:
We know that, the sum of all the angles of a quadrilateral = 360°
Also, an obtuse angle is more than 90° and less than 180°
Since, all the angles of a quadrilateral cannot be obtuse.
Therefore, atmost 3 angles can be obtuse.

questions.question: Which of the following can be four interior angles of a quadrilateral?
questions.explanation:
We know that, the sum of the interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°.
Therefore, the angles in option (a) can be four interior angles of a quadrilateral as 140°+ 40°+ 20°+ 160° = 360°.

questions.question: The number of diagonals in a polygon of n sides is
questions.explanation: We know that the number of diagonals in a polygon of n sides = {tex}\frac{n(n-3)}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The angles of a quadrilateral ABCD taken in an order are in the ratio 3 : 7 : 6 : 4. Then ABCD is a
questions.explanation:
It is given that the ratio of angles of quadrilateral ABCD is 3 : 7 : 6 : 4
Let the angles of quadrilateral ABCD be 3x, 7x, 6x, 4x respectively.
We know that the sum of all angles is 360o
3x + 7x + 6x + 4x = 360o
20x = 360o
x = 180
i.e., {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 3x = 54o
{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 7x = 126o
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 6x = 108o
{tex}\angle{/tex}D = 4x = 72o
Now, Sum of interior angles
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 126o + 54o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 108o + 72o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}BC || AD
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ABCD is a trapezium.

questions.question: If the diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other at right angles, it will be a
questions.explanation:
We know that In any rhombus and square, the diagonals bisect each other at right angles (90o)
Here, according to options, Rhombus is correct option.

questions.question: In a square ABCD, the diagonals meet at point O. The {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB is
questions.explanation:
We know that diagonal of a square bisect each other at 900.
Therefore, {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB is an isosceles right triangle.

questions.question: ABCD is a quadrilateral in which AB = 5 cm, CD = 8 cm and the sum of angle A and angle D is 180o. What is the name of this quadrilateral?
questions.explanation:
We have given that {tex}\angle A +\angle D=180{/tex}
Therefore, quadrilateral must be a Trapezium

questions.question: Rukmini has a farm land which is triangular in shape. What is the sum of all the exterior angles taken in an order of the farm land?
questions.explanation: The sum of all the exterior angles taken in an order of a triangle = 360o

questions.question: The sum of angles of a concave quadrilateral is
questions.explanation:
We know that, the sum of interior angles of any polygon (convex or concave) having n sides = (n - 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 180o
Therefore, the sum of angles of a concave quadrilateral = (4 - 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 180o 
= 360o

questions.question: The angles P, Q, R and S of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 1 : 3 : 7 : 9. Then PQRS is a
questions.explanation:
We have, a quadrilateral PQRS

Let the angles be x, 3x, 7x and 9x
then, x + 3x + 7x + 9x = 360o [{tex}\because{/tex} sum of angles in any quadrilateral = 360o]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20x = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{20}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 18o
So, the angles P, Q, R and S are 18o, 54o, 126o and 162o, respectively.
Since, {tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}S = 18o + 162o = 180o 
and {tex}\angle{/tex}Q + {tex}\angle{/tex}R = 54o + 126o = 180o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The quadrilateral PQRS is a trapezium with PQ || RS.

questions.question: The angle between the two altitudes of a parallelogram through the same vertex of an obtuse angle of the parallelogram is 30°. The measure of the obtuse angle is
questions.explanation:
Let EC and FC be altitudes and {tex}\angle{ECF}{/tex} = 30°

Let {tex}\angle E D C=x=\angle F B C{/tex} [Opposite angles of a parallelogram ABCD]
In {tex}\Delta{/tex}EDC, {tex}\angle E C D=90-x^{\circ}{/tex}
In {tex}\Delta{/tex}FBC, {tex}\angle FCB=90-x^{\circ}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\angle \mathrm{ADC}+\angle \mathrm{DCB}{/tex} = 180°[by adjacent angles sum property of the parallelogram]
{tex}\angle A D C+(\angle E C D+\angle E C F+\angle B C F){/tex} = 180°[{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}\angle \mathrm{DCB}{/tex} = {tex}\angle E C D+\angle E C F+\angle B C F{/tex}]
 x + 90°- x + 30°+ 90°- x = 180°
 - x = 180°- 210°
- x = - 30°
 x = 30°
Hence, {tex}\angle D C B{/tex} = 30° + 60° + 60° = 150°

questions.question: A quadrilateral has three acute angles each measuring 75o, the measure of fourth angle is
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\angle A+\angle B+\angle C+\angle D{/tex} = 360o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 75o + 75o + 75o + {tex}\angle{/tex}D
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 225o + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 360o - 225o = 135o

questions.question: The measures of the four angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio of 1 : 2 : 3 : 4. What is the measure of fourth angle?
questions.explanation:
Let the angles be x, 2x, 3x and 4x.
{tex}\because{/tex} x + 2x + 3x + 4x = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 36o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Fourth angle = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 36o = 144o

questions.question: The diagonals of a rectangle ABCD cut at O. OAL is an equilateral triangle drawn so that B and L are on the same side of AC. If {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 30o, then the angles of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ALB are
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} OA = OB [{tex}\because{/tex} the diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other]
Also, OA = OL {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AOBL is a rhombus.
Since, {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOL is an equilateral triangle.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOL = 60o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 2{tex}\angle{/tex}AOL = 120o

Since, CD || AB
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB = 30o
and {tex}\angle{/tex}OAL = 60o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BAL = 60o - 30o = 30o = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABL
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ALB,
{tex}\angle{/tex}ALB = 120o, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABL = 30o, {tex}\angle{/tex}LAB = 30o

questions.question: The sum of two opposite angles of a parallelogram is 130o. All the angles of parallelogram are
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 130o, then
{tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 360o - 130o
= 230o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Angles = {tex}\frac{130^{\circ}}{2}, \frac{230^{\circ}}{2}{/tex} = 65o, 115o

questions.question: The length of the diagonal of a rectangle whose sides are 12 cm and 5 cm, is
questions.explanation:
Since, here {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 90o
So, by pythagoras theorem,
diagonal AC = {tex}\sqrt{A B^2+B C^2}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5^2+12^2}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{25+44}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{169}{/tex} = 13 cm

questions.question: In a quadrilateral ABCD, if AO and BO be the bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}A and {tex}\angle{/tex}B respectively, {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 70o and {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 30o, then {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB is
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\angle A+\angle B+\angle C+\angle D{/tex} = 360o
{tex}\therefore \angle A+\angle B{/tex} = 360o - (130o + 70o)
= 360o - 200o = 160o

= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ({tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B) = 80o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 80o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180o - ({tex}\angle{/tex}OAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO)
= (180o - 80o) = 100o

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a parallelogram in which {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 75o and {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 60o. Then, {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle C=\angle A{/tex} = 75o [opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = 180o - (60o + 75o)
= 180o - 135o = 45o

questions.question: The values of x and y in the following parallelogram is
questions.explanation:
In a parallelogram, adjacent angles are supplementary.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 120o + (5x + 10)o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x + 10o + 120o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 180o - 130o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 50o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 10o
Also, opposite angles are equal in a parallelogram.
Therefore, 6y = 120o {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 20o

questions.question: The sides BA and DC of quadrilateral ABCD are produced as shown in figure. Then, which of the following statement is correct?
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\angle{/tex}A + b = 180o {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 180o - b
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}C + a = 180o [linear pair]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o - a
But {tex}\angle A+\angle B+\angle C+\angle D{/tex} = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (180o - b) + x + (180o - a) + y = 360o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y = a + b

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a square. A line segment DX cuts the side BC at X and the diagonal AC at O such that {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 105o, {tex}\angle{/tex}OCX = 45o and {tex}\angle{/tex}OXC = x. The value of x is
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 105o and {tex}\angle{/tex}OCX = 45o
{tex}\angle{/tex}COD + {tex}\angle{/tex}COX = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}COX = 180o - {tex}\angle{/tex}COD
= 180o - 105o = 75o
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OCX, {tex}\angle{/tex}OCX + {tex}\angle{/tex}COX + {tex}\angle{/tex}OXC = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 45o + 75o + x = 180o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 180o - 120o = 60o

questions.question: In rectangle READ, the values of {tex}\angle{/tex}EAR, {tex}\angle{/tex}RAD and {tex}\angle{/tex}ROD are respectively
questions.explanation:
Given, a rectangle READ, in which
{tex}\angle{/tex}ROE = 60o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}EOA = 180o - 60o = 120o [linear pair]
Now, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}EOA, {tex}\angle{/tex}OEA = {tex}\angle{/tex}OAE = 30o
[{tex}\because{/tex} OE = OA and equal sides make equal angles]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}EAR = 30o, {tex}\angle{/tex}RAD = 90o - {tex}\angle{/tex}EAR = 60o
and {tex}\angle{/tex}ROD = {tex}\angle{/tex}EOA = 120o

questions.question: In rectangle PAIR, the values of {tex}\angle{/tex}ARI, {tex}\angle{/tex}RMI and {tex}\angle{/tex}PMA are
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}RAI = 35o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PRA = 35o [PR || AI and AR is transversal]
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}ARI = 90o - {tex}\angle{/tex}PRA = 90o - 35o = 55o
{tex}\because{/tex} AM = IM, {tex}\angle{/tex}MIA = {tex}\angle{/tex}MAI = 35o
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AMI, {tex}\angle{/tex}RMI = {tex}\angle{/tex}MAI + {tex}\angle{/tex}MIA = 70o [exterior angle]
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}RMI = {tex}\angle{/tex}PMA
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PMA = 70o [vertically opposite angles]

questions.question: In parallelogram FIST, the value of {tex}\angle{/tex}OST is
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}FIS = 60o
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}FTS = {tex}\angle{/tex}FIS = 60o [{tex}\because{/tex} opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal]
Now, FT || IS and TI is a transversal, therefore 
{tex}\angle{/tex}FTO = {tex}\angle{/tex}SIO = 25o [alternate angles]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}STO = {tex}\angle{/tex}FTS - {tex}\angle{/tex}FTO = 60o - 25o = 35o
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}FOT + {tex}\angle{/tex}SOT = 180o [linear pair]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 110o + {tex}\angle{/tex}SOT = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}SOT = 180o - 110o = 70o
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}TOS, {tex}\angle{/tex}TSO + {tex}\angle{/tex}OTS + {tex}\angle{/tex}TOS = 180o [angle sum property of triangle]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OST = 180o - (70o + 35o) = 75o

questions.question: The values of x and y in the figure are measure of angles, then x + y is equal to
questions.explanation:
Since, ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral.
{tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
and {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 10 + 5y + 5 = 180o
x + 5y = 165o ...(i)
and 2x + 4 + 4y - 4 = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x + 4y = 180o ...(ii)
Solving Eqs.(i) and (ii), we get
x = 40o and y = 25o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y = 40o + 25o = 65o

questions.question: In the figure AD || BC, BE || CD and AB || CF, the {tex}\angle{/tex}CGE is equal to:
questions.explanation:
AB || CF and AE is a transversal line
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAF = {tex}\angle{/tex}GFE = 54o
BE || CD & DE is transveral line
{tex}\angle{/tex}GEF = {tex}\angle{/tex}CDE = 46o
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}GEF
{tex}\angle{/tex}EGC = {tex}\angle{/tex}GFE + {tex}\angle{/tex}GEF
= 54o + 46o
= 100o

questions.question: In the figure, ABCD is a rhombus, in which diagonal AC = side BC, then the value of {tex}\angle{/tex}CDA is:
questions.explanation:
Sides of rhombus are equal
Diagonals, AC = BC
AC = BC
AC = BC = DC
{tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC is equilateral triangle.
{tex}\angle{/tex}CDA = 60o

questions.question: In any parallelogram ABCD, the ratio of {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD is 1 : 2, the measurement of {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD is:
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a parallelogram
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC : {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD
1 : 2
Let {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC be xo
and {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 2xo
