{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 180o
x + 2x = 180o
3x = 180o
x = {tex}\frac{180^o}{3}{/tex}
x = 60o
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = {tex}\angle{/tex}180o - {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 180o - 60o
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 120o

questions.question: The perimeter of a rhombus ABCD is 100 cm. If the length of diagonal AC is 14 cm, then the length of diagonal BD is-
questions.explanation:
Side of rhombus = {tex}\frac{100}{4}{/tex} = 25 cm and AO = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of diagonal = 7 cm
DO = {tex} \sqrt{25^2-7^2} =\sqrt{625-49} =\sqrt{576}{/tex} = 24
Length of diagonal = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 48 cm

questions.question: The ratio of the angles of a parallelogram is 1 : 2 : 3 : 4. What is the difference in largest and smallest angle?
questions.explanation:
Let the angles be x, 2x, 3x & 4x
x + 2x + 3x + 4x = 360°
x = {tex}\frac{310}{10}{/tex}
= 36°
Largest angle - smallest angle = 4x - x
= 3x
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 36
= 108°
108° = {tex}\frac{\pi}{180}\times 108{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3\pi}{5}{/tex} radian

questions.question: The perimeter of a rhombus is 40 cm. If one of its diagonal is 12 cm, then the length of the second diagonal is
questions.explanation:
Side of a rhombus = {tex}\frac{40}{4}{/tex}
= 10 cm
Half part of 1st diagonal = {tex}\frac{12}{2}{/tex}
= 6 cm
Half part of Ilnd diagonal be x.
102 = 62 + x2
x2 = 100 - 36
x = {tex}\sqrt{64}{/tex}
x = 8
Ilnd diagonal = 2x
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 8
= 16 cm

questions.question: A side of square is {tex}3\sqrt2{/tex} cm, then the length of its diagonal is:
questions.explanation:
A/q
Diagonal = {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} side
{tex}=\sqrt{2} \times 3 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
= 6 cm

questions.question: The three angles of any quadrilateral is 105°, 120° and 75° respectively, the fourth angle is
questions.explanation:
Let the sum of all four angles of rhombus = 360°
A/q
The three angles of quadrilateral is 105°, 120°, 75°
The measurement of fourth angle = 360° - (105° + 120° + 75°)
= 360° - 300° = 60°

questions.question: If the interior angle of a regular polygon is 108°. The polygon has ________ sides.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} Each interior angle of a polygon = {tex}\frac{2(n-2) \times 90^{\circ}}{n}{/tex}
108° = {tex}\frac{2(n-2) \times 90^{\circ}}{n}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 108on = 180on - 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 72n = 360°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} n = 5
Hence, total sides of a polygon = 5

questions.question: The angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 1 : 2 : 3 : 4. The largest angle is
questions.explanation:
Let the angles of quadrilateral be x, 2x, 3x and 4x.
According to the question,
Sum of all angles of quadrilateral = 360°
x + 2x + 3x + 4x = 360°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 360°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 36°
Hence, largest angle of quadrilateral = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 36°
= 144°

questions.question: The sides of a rectangle are 2 m and 1.5 m. The length of its diagonal is
questions.explanation:
Length of rectangle = 2 m and breadth =1.5 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Diagonal of rectangle
= {tex}\sqrt{(\text { length })^2+(\text { breadth })^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(2)^2+(1 \cdot 5)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{4+2 \cdot 25}=\sqrt{6 \cdot 25}{/tex} = 2.5 m

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus are 24 cm and 10 cm. The perimeter of this rhombus (in cm) is-
questions.explanation:
AC = 10 cm
OC = AOB
{tex}\frac{10}{2}{/tex} = 5 cm
BD = 24 cm
OD = OB
= {tex}\frac{24}{2}{/tex}= 12 cm
BC = {tex}\sqrt{B O^2+O C}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{5^2+12^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{25+144}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{169}{/tex}
= 13 cm
Perimeter = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 13
= 52 cm

questions.question: ABCD is a rhombus in which {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 80°. The measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD is
questions.explanation:
In rhombus ABCD, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 80o
{tex}\because{/tex} ABCD is a rhombus,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 80o + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 180 - 80o = 100o
{tex}\because{/tex} AC is a diagonal,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD = {tex} \frac{\angle B A D}{2}=\frac{100^o}{2}{/tex} = 50o

questions.question: For what value of x, the perimeter of shape is 77 cm?
questions.explanation:
We have, perimeter = 77 cm
(x + 1) + (x + 2) + (2x + 2) + (2x + 1) + (x + 1) = 77
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x + 7 = 77 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = (77 - 7)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = 70 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}70 \times \frac{1}{7}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 10
Hence, the required value of x is 10 cm.

questions.question: In a quadrilateral, the sum of three angles is equal to twice the fourth angle. The measure of the fourth angle is
questions.explanation:
Let the fourth angle of the quadrilateral is x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Sum of remaining three angles = 2x
We know that, sum of all the angles of a
quadrilateral is 360°.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2a + a = 360° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 360°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 120°

questions.question: In a quadrilateral ABCD, if AB = 10 cm CD = 6 cm and {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180°, what type of quadrilateral is ABCD?
questions.explanation:
According to the question,

Here, AB and CD both are opposite sides and {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180°. Hence, AB will be parallel to CD.
Hence, ABCD is a trapezium.

questions.question: In a quadrilateral ABCD, AB = 6 cm, CD = 8 cm and sum of angles A and D is 180°. This is ________
questions.explanation:
Given, in quadrilateral ABCD
AB = 6 cm, CD = 8 cm
and {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180°

{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180°
AB || CD
and AB = 6 cm, CD = 8 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ABCD will not be a square, parallelogram and rhombus.
Hence, ABCD is a trapezium.

questions.question: One of the diagonals of a rhombus is equal to its side. Its larger angle is
questions.explanation:
Let in rhombus ABCD, diagonal BD is equal of side.

Then, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD, AB = AD = BD
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD is an equilateral triangle.
Then, {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA = 60o
and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 60o = 120o
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, side AC will be the largest, then {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC will be the largest.
Hence, the largest angle = 120o

questions.question: ABCD is a rhombus in which the altitude from D to side AB bisects AB. Then {tex}\angle{/tex}A and {tex}\angle{/tex}B respectively, are ________.
questions.explanation:
In the given figure, ABCD is a rhombus and DP {tex}\perp{/tex} AB such that AP = PB.

Join BD.
Let AB = BC= CD = AD = 2a
So, AP = a = PB
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}APD,
DP2 = AD2 - AP2 (By Pythagoras theorem)
= (2a)2 - a2 = 4a2 - a2 = 3a2
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}DPB, we have
BD2 = DP2 + PB2 = 3a2 + a2 = 4a2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} BD = 2a
So, AB = BD = AD = 2a
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD is an equilateral triangle.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 60° and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 60°
Similarly, {tex}\triangle{/tex}BDC is an equilateral triangle and {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 60°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 60° + 60° = 120°
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 60° and {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 120°

questions.question: The exterior angle of a regular polygon is one-third of its interior angle. How many sides does the polygon has?
questions.explanation:
Let n be the number of sides of the polygon.
Then, each exterior angle = {tex}\left(\frac{360}{n}\right)^{\circ}{/tex}
And each interior angle = {tex}\left(\frac{2 n-4}{n} \times 90\right)^{\circ}{/tex}
According to question, we have
Exterior angle = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} (Interior angle)
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{360}{n}\right)^{\circ}=\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{2 n-4}{n} \times 90\right)^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2n - 4 = 12 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2n = 16 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 8

questions.question: If each pair of opposite sides of a quadrilateral are equal and parallel, then it is a ________.
questions.explanation: Parallelogram

questions.question: The ratio of two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are in the ratio 3 : 5 and its perimeter is 48 cm. Find the measure of each of the angles of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the parallelogram in which
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 3x and {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 5x

Since opposite sides of a parallelogram are parallel.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 5x = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x = 180° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 22.5°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 67.5° and {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 112.5°
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 67.5° and {tex}\angle{/tex}D = {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 112.5°

questions.question: A quadrilateral with exactly two pairs of equal consecutive sides is called a ________.
questions.explanation: Kite

questions.question: A trapezium in which non-parallel sides are equal is said to be ________.
questions.explanation: Isosceles trapezium

questions.question: The quadrilateral having only one pair of opposite sides parallel is called a ________.
questions.explanation: Trapezium

questions.question: Sonu is playing in a playground which is of the form of a parallelogram. He observes that the diagonals of the playground are 80 m and 60 m long. So, the playground can be the shape of ________.
questions.explanation:
In rectangle and square, diagonals are of equal length.
Also, kite is not a parallelogram.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement (s) is/are true?

  1. In a trapezium the diagonals bisect each other
  2. In a rectangle diagonals intersect at right angle
  3. The diagonals of a rhombus are equal
  4. None of these
questions.explanation: All the given statements are false.

questions.question: In a parallelogram ABCD, diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. If AO = 5 cm, then AC = ________.
questions.explanation:
Diagonals bisect each other.
We have, AC = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} AO = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 10 cm.

questions.question: In a Rhombus ABCD, if AB = AC, then {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = ________.
questions.explanation:
AB = AC

{tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is an equilateral triangle.
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 60o, also {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 60o.
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 60o + 60o = 120o.

questions.question: A quadrilateral in which both pairs of opposite sides are parallel is a ________.
questions.explanation: parallelogram

questions.question: If ABCD is a parallelogram, then {tex}\angle{/tex}A - {tex}\angle{/tex}C = ________.
questions.explanation:
In a parallelogram, opposite angles are equal.
So, {tex}\angle \mathrm{A}=\angle \mathrm{C} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \angle \mathrm{A}-\angle \mathrm{C}=0^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: A quadrilateral in which diagonals are equal and bisect each other perpendicularly is a ________.
questions.explanation: square

questions.question: One of the diagonals of a rhombus and its sides are equal angles of the rhombus.
questions.explanation:
Let PQRS be a rhombus such that its diagonal PR is equal to its side, that is,
PQ = QR = RS = PS = PR
So, {tex}\triangle{/tex}PRS and {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR are equilateral.

{tex}\angle{/tex}S = {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 60o [ Each angle of an equilateral triangle is 60°.] and
{tex}\angle{/tex}P = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 = 60o + 60° = 120° = {tex}\angle{/tex}R
Hence {tex}\angle \mathrm{S}=\angle \mathrm{Q}=60^{\circ} {/tex} and {tex}\angle \mathrm{P}=\angle \mathrm{R}=120^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: The perimeter of a parallelogram is 180cm. One side exceeds the another by 10 cm. The adjacent sides of the parallelogram are ________.
questions.explanation:
Two sides are x, x + 10
Perimeter = 180 cm
i.e., x + x + 10 + x + x+ 10= 180
4x + 20 =180
x = 40 cm.

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram in which {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 75o and{tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 60o then {tex}\angle{/tex}CDB = ________.
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = {tex}\angle{/tex}CBD = 60o ({tex}\because{/tex} AD||BC)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o
75o + 60o + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 45o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 45o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDB = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 45o (Alternate interior angles)

questions.question: ABCD is a quadrilateral. AB = BC = CD = DA and {tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\angle{/tex}B ={tex}\angle{/tex} C = {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 90o. Then ABCD can be called
questions.explanation: From definitions, we can clearly see that ABCD can be called a rhombus, square or a parallelogram.

questions.question: RSTU is a parallelogram as shown in the figure below. Then the shown angles x and y are related
questions.explanation: As seen in the diagram given, triangle RSU and triangle STU are congruent,. But x and y are not corresponding angles.

questions.question: In the quadrilateral ABCD, the diagonals AC and BD are equal and perpendicular to each other. Then, ABCD is a
questions.explanation: We know that in a square the diagonals are equal and bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: The diagonals of a parallelogram ABCD intersect at O. If {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90o, then {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC =180o, {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90o or {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 90o
Alternate Method: In triangle, OAB, since sum of angles is 180o we have
{tex}\angle{/tex} AOB = 90o

questions.question: A diagonal of a rectangle is inclined to one side of the rectangle at 25o. The acute angle between the diagonals is
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB = 25o, clearly {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB = 65o.
Let diagonals meet at O. Triangle OCB is
an isosceles triangle.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 65o
Hence, {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 50o

questions.question: If angles P, Q, R and S of the quadrilateral PQRS; taken in order, are in the ratio 3 : 7 : 6 : 4, then PQRS is a
questions.explanation:
Let the angles be 3x, 7x, 6x and 4x.
{tex}\therefore {/tex} 3x + 7x + 6x + 4x = 360o
or 20x = 360o or x = 18o.
The angles are 54o, 126o, 108o, 72o.
We see that adjacent angles are supplementary but opposite angles are not equal.
Clearly, it is a trapezium.

questions.question: If two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are in the ratio 3 : 2, then the measure of the angles are
questions.explanation:
Let the angles be 3x and 2x.
We have, 3x + 2x = 180o
5x = 180o
x = 36o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The angles are
36 {tex}\times{/tex} 3,36 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 108o, 72o.

questions.question:
Consider the following statement:

  1. The diagonals of a parallelogram are equal.
  2. The diagonals of a square are perpendicualr to each other.
  3. If the diagonals of a quadrilateral intersect at right angles, it is not necessarily a rhombus.
  4. Every quadrilateral is either a trapezium or a parallelogram or a kite.
questions.explanation: Both (B) and (C) are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct?

options.option: The diagonals of a rectangle are perpendicular.
The diagonals of a rhombus are equal.

questions.explanation: Every square contains each property of Rhombus

questions.question: If the lengths of two diagonals of a rhombus are 12 cm and 16 cm, then the length of each side of the rhombus is
questions.explanation:
Length of the each side of a rhombus.
= {tex}\sqrt{\left(\frac{d_1}{2}\right)^2+\left(\frac{d_2}{2}\right)^2}{/tex}
Where d1 and d2 be the two diagonals
= {tex} \sqrt{\left(\frac{12}{2}\right)^{12}}+\left(\frac{16}{2}\right)^2 {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{6^2+8^2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{100}{/tex} = 10 cm.

questions.question: If each interior angle of a regular polygon is twice as large as the exterior angle, the number of sides is
questions.explanation:
Let the no. of sides = n
Now, interior angle of polygon of n sides
= {tex}\left(\frac{2 n-4}{n}\right) \times 90^{\circ}{/tex} and exterior angle
= {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{n}{/tex}
According to the question:
{tex}\left(\frac{2 n-4}{n}\right) 90^{\circ}{/tex} {tex}= 2\left(\frac{360^{\circ}}{n}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (n - 2)90o = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n - 2 = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 6.

questions.question: In figure points A, B, C and D are concyclic and {tex}\angle{/tex} CBE = 130o then {tex}\angle{/tex} FDC is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given: {tex}\angle{/tex} CBE = 130o
{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}CBA = 50o (Linear pair)
Now, ADCB is a quadrilateral and sum of
opposite angle =180o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = 180o - 50o = 130o
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}FDC = 50o (Linear pair)

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect:
questions.explanation:
Every trapezium is not a parallelogram.
(In || gm each pair of opposite sides should be parallel.)

questions.question: The sum of the exterior angles of a hexagon is
questions.explanation:
Since, sum of the measurements of exterior angles of a polygon is 360o.
Therefore, the sum of the exterior angles of a hexagon is 360o.

questions.question: Give one word for the below given explanation A quadrilateral in which the line segment joining any two arbitrary points in its interior lies wholly within the quadrilateral.
questions.explanation: Convex quadrilateral

questions.question: PQRS is a parallelogram. Then y equals :
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}PSR = {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 68o(opp. {tex}\angle{/tex}s of a ||gm are equal)
{tex}\angle{/tex}PTS = 180o - 139o = 41o (PTQ is a straight line)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}RST = {tex}\angle{/tex}PTS = 41o (SR || PQ alt. {tex}\angle{/tex}s are equal)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y = {tex}\angle{/tex}PSR - {tex} \angle{/tex}RST = 68o - 41o = 27o

questions.question: In a square ABCD. AB = (2x + 3) cm and BC = (3x - 5). The value of x is
questions.explanation:
In a square ABCD, all sides are equal

So, AB = BC = CD = AD
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB = BC
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x + 3) = (3x - 5)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 + 5 = 3x - 2x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 8

questions.question: If one angle of a parallelogram is 24o less than twice the smallest angle then the largest angle of the parallelogram is
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\angle{/tex}A = x

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 2x - 24
Sum of two adjacent angle is supplementry
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 2x - 24 = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 24 = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 204o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 68o
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 68o
{tex}\angle{/tex} B = 2x - 24 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 68 - 24 = 136 - 24 = 112o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The largest angle of the parallelogram is 112o

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is false?

  1. A square is a rectangle whose adjacent sides are equal.
  2. A square is rhombus whose one angle is a right angle.
  3. The diagonals of a square bisect each other at right angles.
  4. The diagonals of a square do not divide the whole square into four equal parts.
questions.explanation: Here statement is false as diagonals of a square divides it into for equal parts.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is false?

  1. All the rectangles are parallelograms
  2. All the squares are rectangles
  3. All the parallelograms are rectangles
  4. All the rhombuses are parallelogram
questions.explanation: All parallelograms are not rectangles because in a parallelogram all angles are not necessarily right angles.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true?

  1. All the rhombuses are squares
  2. Each squares is a parallelogram
  3. Each parallelogram is a square
  4. Each trapezium is a parallelogram
questions.explanation: In a square opposite sides are equal and parallel.

questions.question:
In a kite, what is false?

  1. The diagonals are perpendicular to each other
  2. The diagonals bisect each other
  3. Only one pair of opposite angles is equal
  4. All the four sides are equal
questions.explanation: In a kite two pairs of adjacent sides are equal.

questions.question: Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are (2x + 25)o and (3x - 5)o. The value of x is
questions.explanation:
We know that, sum of two adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 180o.
So, (2x + 25)o + (3x - 5)o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x + 20 = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 160
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 32

questions.question: If an angle of a parallelogram is two-third of its adjacent angle, the smallest angle of the parallelogram is
questions.explanation:
Let one angle of a parallelogram be xo Let the other angle of parallelogram be {tex}\frac{2 x^{\circ}}{3}{/tex}
Since sum of adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 180o
So, x+ {tex}\frac{2 x^{\circ}}{3}{/tex} = 180
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{5 x}{3}{/tex} = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 36o{tex}\times{/tex} 3o = 108o
The smallest angle of the parallelogram
= {tex}\frac{2}{3} x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 108 = 72o

questions.question: In the below given figure of parallelogram, find the measure of xo
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}EOF = {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB xo (Vertically opposite angles) In a parallelogram pair of opposite angles are equal.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 60o and {tex}\angle{/tex}B = {tex}\angle{/tex}D
Also in a parallelogram adjacent angles are supplementary.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o , {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180 - {tex}\angle{/tex}C
{tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180 - 60o = 120o
From figure it is clear that AO and BO are bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}A and {tex}\angle{/tex}B respectively.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = 30o and {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA = 60o
From {tex}\triangle{/tex}OAB
{tex}\angle{/tex}OAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180o [Angle sum property]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180o - ({tex}\angle{/tex}OAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA)
= 18 - (30 + 60)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180 - 90 = 90o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}x = {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 90o

questions.question:
In the diagram, ABCD is a rhombus.

The value of x - y is:
questions.explanation:
x = 90o
({tex}\because{/tex} diagonals of rhombus bisect each other at right angles.)
Now, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC, AD = DC ( sides of rhombus.)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 40o
{tex}\because{/tex} equal sides have equal angles opposite to them.)
Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex}OCD,
y = 180o - (90o + 40o)
=180o - 130o = 50o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x - y = 90o - 50o = 40o

questions.question: The measure of the angles of a quadrilateral ABCD are respectively in the ratio 1 : 2 : 3 : 4. Find the type of quadrilateral ABCD.
questions.explanation:
Let the ratio of angles be 'x'
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = x, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 2x,
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 3x, {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 4x,
x + 2x + 3x + 4x = 360o
10x = 360o
x = 360
{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 36o, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 72o
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o, {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 144o
Now we see that
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o and {tex}\angle{/tex}B +{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
It shows that AB || DC
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ABCD is a trapezium.

questions.question: The ratio of exterior angle to interior angle of a regular polygon is 1 : 4. find the number of sides of the polygon.
questions.explanation:
Let the exterior angle of the polygon be x Then, the interior angle of polygon
= 180o - x
According to question
{tex}\frac{x}{180^{\circ}-x}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
or, 4x = 180o - x
or, 5x = 180o
or, x = {tex}\frac{180^{\circ}}{5}{/tex}
So, x = 36o
Number of sides of polygon = {tex} \frac{360^{\circ}}{\text { exterior angle }}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{36^{\circ}}{/tex} = 10

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. DK perpendicular to BA produced at K and DM perpendicular to BC produced at M. Find the measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}KDM.
questions.explanation:
AD || BC and BC is extented to BM
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB || BM
So {tex}\angle{/tex}DCM = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = 30o
And from {tex}\triangle{/tex}CDM, {tex}\angle{/tex}CDM = 60o
Similarly BK is extended form of BA
{tex} \therefore{/tex} DC || BK
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAK = 30o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADK, {tex}\angle{/tex}KDA = 60o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}KDM = {tex}\angle{/tex}KDA + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC + {tex}\angle{/tex}CDM = 60+ 30 + 60
{tex}\angle{/tex}KDM = 150o

questions.question:
In the diagram, ABCD is a rhombus. AEC and BED are straight lines.

p + q + r + s + t = ?
questions.explanation:
In rhombus ABCD,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 360o
= {tex}\frac{\angle \mathrm{A}}{2}+\frac{\angle \mathrm{B}}{2}+\frac{\angle \mathrm{C}}{2}+\frac{\angle \mathrm{D}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{2}{/tex}
p + q + s + t = 180 ...(i) ({tex}\therefore{/tex} diagonals of rhombus are angle bisectors)
Now {tex}\angle{/tex}r = 90o ...(ii) ({tex}\therefore{/tex} diagonals of rhombus bisect the diagonals at right angles.)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
p + q + r + s + t = 180o + 90o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} p + q + r + s + t = 270o

questions.question: ABCD is a rhombus with {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 56°, find the value of {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD where AC is the diagonal of rhombus ABCD.
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a rhombus.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = BC
So {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = x(let)
from {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC

x + x + 56o = 180o
2x + 180o - 56o
2x = 124
x = 62o
Now AD || BC and AC is a transversal.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 62o (Alternate Interior Angles)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 62o

questions.question: The diagonal of rectangle is thrice its smaller side. The ratio of its sides is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let the smaller side of rectangle be x.
Let the length be l.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Diagonal = 3x
Now, since the side of rectangle are perpendicular,
(3x)2 = l2 + x2
(By pythogoras theorm)
or
l2 = 9x2 - x2 = 8x2
l = {tex}2 \sqrt{2 x} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ratio of sides = {tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{2 x}}{x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex} : 1

questions.question:
ABCD is a parallelogram in which {tex}\angle{/tex}DAO = 40o, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAO = 35o and {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 65o
Find {tex}\angle{/tex}ODC.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 65o
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB, {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}O + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o
= {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 80o
{tex}\angle{/tex}ODC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 80o
(Alternate interior angles)

questions.question: In the figure, HOPE is a rectangle. Its diagonals meet at G. If HG = 5x + 1 and EG = 4x + 19, find x.
questions.explanation:
Since diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other,
HP = 2HG = 2(5x + 1) = 10x + 2
and OE = 2EG = 2(4x + 19) = 8x + 38
Diagonals of a rectangle are equal. So HP = OE
or 10x + 2 = 8x + 38
or 2x = 36 or x = 18

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. The angle bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}A and {tex}\angle{/tex}D meet at O. Find the measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}AOD.
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = xo
{tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = 180 - x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\angle B A D}{2}=\frac{x}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\angle A D C}{2}={/tex} {tex}90-\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOD = 180 - {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}+90-\frac{x}{2}\right){/tex} 90o

questions.question: Find the dimensions of a rectangle with perimeter 16 cm, whose long side is 3 times its short side.
questions.explanation:
Let shouter side be x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Longer side = 3x
Perimeter = 2(x + 3x) = 2(4x)
But 8x = 16 cm
x = 2 cm
2 cm by 6 cm.

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a polygon whose exterior and interior angles are in the ratio 1 : 5.
questions.explanation:
Let no. of sides of polygon = n
Exterior angle = x0
Interior angle = 5xo
x = 30o ({tex}\because{/tex} x + 5x = 180)
Sum of all exterior angle = (360)o
n {tex}\times{/tex} 30o =  (360)o
n = 12

questions.question: The shorter side of a parallelogram is 4.8 cm and the longer side is half as much again as the shorter side. Find the perimeter of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
Shouter side = 4.8 cm
Longer side = (4.8 + 2.4) cm = 7.2 cm
Perimeter = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (4.8 + 7.2) cm
= (12.0) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 cm = 24 cm

questions.question: In the given figure, find a + b + c + d.
questions.explanation:
Since, sum of measure of interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360o.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 120 + 80 + 60 + {tex}\angle{/tex}RSP = 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}RSP = 100
a = ext {tex}\angle{/tex}SPQ = 60o
Similarly,
b = 100, c = 120, d = 80

questions.question: RICE is a rhombus. Find x, y, z.
questions.explanation:
OI = OE {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 2 = 5 or x = 5 - 2 = 3.
OC = OR {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12 = y + x or y = 12 - x
12 - 3 = 9
EOR is a right triangle
ER2 = OE2 + OR2
= 52 + 122
= 25 + 144 = 169
ER = {tex}\sqrt169{/tex} = 13 cm

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. The diagonals of a parallelogram are equal
  2. The diagonals of a rectangle are perpendicular to each other
  3. If the diagonals of a quadrilateral intersect at right angles, it is not necessarily a rhombus.
  4. Every quadrilateral is either a trapezium or a parallelogram or a kite
questions.explanation: If the diagonals of a quadrilateral intersect at right angles, it is not necessarily a rhombus.

questions.question: The diagonals of a quadrilateral intersect at right angles and it has exactly one axis of symmetry. The quadrilateral is a:
questions.explanation:
The diagonals of a quadrilateral intersect at right angles
{tex}\therefore{/tex} This quadrilateral is a square.
But it is also given that this quadrilateral
has exactly one axis of symmetry.
But square has 4 axes of symmetry.
So, this quadrilateral cannot be square and rhombus.
Hence, this quadrilateral is a kite

questions.question: The interior angle of a regular polygon exceeds the exterior angle by 132o. Then the number of sides in the polygon is
questions.explanation:
Let the number of sides of polygon = n. Now, interior angle of polygon of n sides.
= {tex}\left(\frac{2 n-4}{n}\right){/tex}90o
And exterior angle of polygon of n sides = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{n}{/tex}
According to the equestion, {tex}\left(\frac{2 n-4}{n}\right){/tex} 90o{tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{n}{/tex} + 132o
= {tex}\left(2-\frac{4}{n}\right) 90^{\circ}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{n}+132^{\circ}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(1-\frac{2}{n}\right) 90^{\circ}=\frac{180^{\circ}}{n}+66^{\circ}{/tex}
= {tex}1-\frac{2}{n}=\frac{2}{n}+\frac{11}{15}{/tex}
= {tex}1-\frac{11}{15}=\frac{4}{n} \Rightarrow \frac{4}{15}=\frac{4}{n} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 15.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement (s) is/are true?

  1. A parallelogram in which two adjacent angles are equal is a rectangle.
  2. A quadrilateral in which both pairs of opposite angles are equal is parallelogram.
  3. In a parallelogram the number of acute angles is zero (or) two
  4. All the above
questions.explanation: All the given statements are true.

questions.question: A quadrilateral is a rhombus but not a square if

options.option: its diagonals do not bisect each other
the length of diagonals are not equal

questions.explanation: Diagonals of a Rhobus not necessarily equal

questions.question: The parallel sides of a trapezoid are 3 cm and 9 cm. The non-parallel sides are 4 cm and 6 cm. A line parallel to the bases divides the trapezoid into two trapezoids of equal perimeters. The ratio in which each of the non-parallel sides is divided is:
questions.explanation:
Let FA = x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} FE = 6 - x
and CB = y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} DC = 4 - y

Perimeter of trapezium ABCF
= 9 + y + FC + x
Perimeter of trapezium FCDE
= FC + 4 - y + 3 + 6 - x
= FC + 13 - x - y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 + y + FC + x = FC + 13 - x - y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 + y + x = 1 3 - x - y
2(x + y) = 13 - 9 = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y = 2 ...(i)
But {tex}\frac{6-x}{x}=\frac{4-y}{y} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}y=\frac{2}{3} x{/tex}
From eq (i) {tex}\frac{2}{3} x+x-2{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}x=\frac{6}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required ratio = EF : FA [or DC : CB]
= {tex}\frac{6-x}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6-\frac{6}{5}}{\frac{6}{5}}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{24}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{1}{/tex} = 4 : 1

questions.question:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

List - I List - II
P. Opposite sides equal and parallel 1. Rectangle
Q. Opposite sides equal and one angle 90% 2. square
R. All side equal and one angle 90o 3. Rhombus
S. All side equal and diagonals at right angles 4. Parallelogram
In the figure given below AN and CP are perpendiculars to the diagonal BD of a parallelogram. Then
questions.explanation:
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD = Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD (diagonal of a || gm divides it into two triangles of equal area)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(BD {tex}\times{/tex} AN) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(PC {tex}\times{/tex} BD)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AN = CP

questions.question: The bisectors of angles of a parallelogram enclose a
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a parallelogram.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB || DC and AD cuts them
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2} \angle A+\frac{1}{2} \angle D{/tex} = 90o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADP, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAP + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADP = 90o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DPA = 90o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}SPQ 90o (vertically opp. angles)
Similary
{tex}\angle{/tex}PQR 90, {tex}\angle{/tex}QRS = 90o and RSP = 90o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PQRS is a quadrilateral each of whose angles is 90o therefore PQRS is a rectangle.

questions.question: ABCD is a rhombus whose one angle is 60°. The ratio of the lengths of its diagonals is
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the Rhombus where {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 60o
{tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD is Isosceles triangle
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB
Then by angle sum property
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 = {tex}\angle{/tex}2 = 60o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABCD is an equilaterl triangle
Let BD = x
Then by phthagorus theorem in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABO
(AO)2 = AB2 - OB2
= x2 - {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^2=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} x{/tex}
AC = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} OA = {tex}2 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} x=\sqrt{3 x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC : BD = {tex}\sqrt{3} x: x=\sqrt{3}: 1{/tex}

questions.question: The measure of each interior angle of a regular convex polygon is 156o. The number of sides of the polygon is :
questions.explanation:
We know that Sum of all the Internal angles of polygon
= 180 {tex}\times{/tex} (n - 2)
According to question 180 (n - 2) = 156 {tex}\times{/tex} n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180n - 360o = 156n
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180n - 156n = 360
24n = 360
n = {tex}\frac{360}{24}{/tex} = 15

questions.question: A quadrilateral that is not a parallelogram but has exactly two equal opposite angles is :
questions.explanation: Kite

questions.question: The difference between an exterior angle of (n-1) sides regular polygon and an exterior angle of (n + 2) sided regular polygon is 6o then find the value of n
questions.explanation:
Since, we know each exterior angle of a regular polygon of n sides is equal to {tex}\left(\frac{360}{n}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{360}{n-1}-\frac{360}{n+2}{/tex} = 6
= (n - 1) (n + 2) = 180
= n2 + n - 182 = 0
On solving the above equation, we get n = 13

questions.question: Two regular polygons are such that the ratio between their number of sides is 1 : 2 and the ratio of measures of their interior angles is 3 : 4, then number of sides of each polygon.
questions.explanation:
Let no. of sides are n and 2n
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A. T. Q.
{tex}\frac{(n-2) \times 180}{n}{/tex} : {tex} \frac{(2 n-2) \times 180}{2 n}{/tex} = 3: 4
{tex}\frac{n-2}{n-1}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
n = 5
no, of sides are 5 and 10

questions.question: The sum of all interior angles of a regular convex polygon is 1080o. The measure of each of its interior angles is
questions.explanation:
The sum of all interior = 1080o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (n - 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 180o = 1080o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n - 2 = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} n = 6 + 2 = 8
Interior angles = {tex}\frac{1080}{8}{/tex}
= 135o

questions.question: In the below figure, ABCD is a square and BCE is an equilateral triangle, what is the measure of angle DEC?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 90o ( angle of a square)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCE = 60o ( angle of an equilateral triangle)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DCE = 90o + 60o =150o
{tex}\triangle{/tex}DCE is an isosceles triangle with DC = EC
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDE = {tex}\angle{/tex}CED ( angles opposite of equal sides)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}EDC + {tex}\angle{/tex}DEC + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCE = 180o
2{tex}\angle{/tex}DEC = 180o - {tex}\angle{/tex}DCE
= 180o - 150o
{tex}\angle{/tex}DEC = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (30o) = 15o

questions.question: Quadrilateral EFGH is a rectangle in which J is the point of intersection of the diagonals. Find the value of x if JF = 8x + 4 and EG = 24x - 8.
questions.explanation:
Given,
JF = 8x + 4 and EG = 24x - 8
Quadrilateral is a rectangle. So,
{tex}\angle{/tex}EJF = 90o and
EJ = JG = {tex}\frac{24 x-8}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{24 x-8}{2}{/tex} = 8x + 4
24x - 8 = 2(8x + 4)
24x - 8 = 16x + 8
24x - 16x = 8 + 8
8x = 16
x = 2.

questions.question: ABCD is a rectangle with {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 48o. Then {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC equals:
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB,

BO = OA ( Diagonals of are equal; and bisects each other)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA = 48o (isos. {tex}\triangle{/tex} property)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA = {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA = 48o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = {tex}\angle{/tex}CBA - {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA
= 90o - 48o = 42o

questions.question: In the diagram, ABD and BCD are isosceles triangles, where AB = BC = BD. The special name that is given to quadrilateral ABCD is:
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAD,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDA = {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 57o ( isos. {tex}\triangle{/tex} property) In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BDC,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = 66o (isos {tex}\triangle{/tex} property)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 57o + 66o = 123o = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}D +{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 123o + 66o = 189o
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 123o + 66o = 189o
Hence, by the property that co-int. angles are supplementary therefore lines are parallel,
we have
AB || DC and AD not || BC
Hence, ABCD is a trapezium.

questions.question: The length and breadth of a rectangle are in the ratio 4 : 3. If the diagonal measures 25 cm then the perimeter of the rectangle is
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the rectangle.

Let AC be the diagonal, where AC = 25 cm.
Length and breadth of a rectangle are in the ratio 4 : 3
Length of rectangle = 4x and Breadth of rectangle = 3x
By Pythagoras theorem, we have AB2 + BC2 = AC2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4x)2 + (3x)2 = (25)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16x2 + 9x2 = 625
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25x2 = 625
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 25 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\pm{/tex}5
Since the side of the rectangle cannot be negative, so x = -5 is neglected {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -5
So, length of the rectangle = 4x = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 20 cm Breadth of the rectangle = 3x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 15 cm
So, perimeter of the rectangle = 2(l + b)
= 2(20 + 15) = 2(35) = 70 cm

questions.question: The perimeter of a parallelogram is 150 cm. One of its side is greater than the other by 25 cm. Find the length of all the sides of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
Let one pair of opposite sides of a parallelogram be x cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} other pair of opposite side be (x - 25) cm.
Perimeter of a parallelogram = 150 cm (given)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + (x - 25) + x + (x - 25) = 150
4x - 50 = 150
4x = 50 + 150
4x = 200
x = 50 cm
x - 25 = 25 cm

questions.question: In a parallelogram {tex}\angle{/tex}A : {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 1 : 2. Then, {tex}\angle{/tex}A =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}A : {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 1 : 2
Sum of the rations = 1 + 2 = 3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 180o = 60o

questions.question: The length of a rectangle is 8 cm and each of its diagonals measures 10 cm. Find its breadth.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the given rectangle in which length AB = 8 cm and diagonal AC = 10 cm. AC2 = AB2 + BC2
[Pythagoras Theorem]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC2 = (AC2 - AB)2 = {(10)2 - (8)2}
= (100 - 64) = 36
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC = {tex}\sqrt36{/tex} = 6 cm
Hence, breadth = 6 cm

questions.question: In a rhombus ABCD, {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 60o and AB = 6 cm., then the length of the diagonal BD in cm is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 90o ({tex}\therefore{/tex} Diagonals of rhombus bisect perpendicularly)
n {tex} \triangle{/tex}AOB, {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB +{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 180o (Angle sum property of {tex} \triangle{/tex})

30o + 90o + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 60o
Similarly {tex}\angle{/tex}ADO = 60o
Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB
{tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 60o
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB is equilateral triangle
Hence AB = AD = BD = 6 cm

questions.question: In a given rectangle ABCD, diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. If {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 120o. What is the value of {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA?
questions.explanation:
Given that {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 120o
Since ABCD is a rectangle Diagonals bisect each other i.e., AO = OC = DO = OB
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ODC, {tex}\angle{/tex}DOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}OCD+ {tex}\angle{/tex}ODC = 180o (Angle sum property of {tex}\triangle{/tex})
120o + 2{tex}\angle{/tex}OCD = 180o ({tex}\because{/tex} OD = OC)
{tex}\angle{/tex}OCD = 30o = {tex}\angle{/tex}ODC
ODC = {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA (Alternate interior angle)
{tex}\angle{/tex}OBA = 30o

questions.question: Which shape is possible?

options.option: a parallelogram with 4 angles of equal measure
A parallelogram with sides that measures 2 cm, 4 cm, 6 cm and 8 cm
A rhombus with sides that measures 4 cm, 4 cm, 8 cm, and 8 cm

questions.explanation:
1. Not possible; because in a rhombus 4 angles either 90o or both obtuse and acute.
  2. Possible; If parallelogram is of type square or rectangle then all the angle are of equal measure.
  3. Not possible; In rhombus all four sides are of equal measure.
  4. Not possible; In parallelogram pair of opposite sides are equal and parallel

questions.question: The two diagonals are not necessarily equal in a
questions.explanation: Rhombus

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.option: A regular polygon is equiangular and equilateral.
A rectangle is equilateral but not equiangular.
Convex polygons have no portions of their diagonals in their exteriors.

questions.explanation: Rectangle is equilateral but not equiangular.

questions.question: The shape of a pie-chart is ________.
questions.explanation: A pie chart is a circle that has been split up into different segments similar to a circular pie that has been cut into slices.

questions.question: The pie-chart is divided into ________.
questions.explanation: A pie-chart is a circular statistical graphics which is divided into slices known as sectors to illustrate numerical proportion.

questions.question: The central total angle in a pie chart is
questions.explanation: Because the pie chart represents the complete circle.

questions.question: 18 out of 36 people love reading, so reading in the pie chart will be represented by
questions.explanation: {tex}\left( {{{18} \over {36}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 360o = 180o and 180o degree represent semi-circle.

questions.question: If you try to start a scooter, what are the possible outcomes?
questions.explanation: When you will try to start a scooter then there are only two possible outcomes either it will start or not.

questions.question: When a die is thrown, what are the six possible outcomes?
questions.explanation: When a dice is thrown there are only six possible outcomes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6

questions.question: When a coin is thrown, what are the two possible outcomes?
questions.explanation: When you will throw a coin you will either get head or tail. Therefore, possible outcomes are H(head) and T(tail).

questions.question: When a die is thrown, what is the probability of getting the number 7?
questions.explanation: There is no possible outcome of 7 in throwing dice, in throwing a dice possible outcomes are 1,2,3,4,5,6 so probability will be 0.

questions.question: When a die is thrown, what is the probability of getting an even number?
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6 i.e 6
Even number = 2,4,6 i.e. 3
Probability of getting an even number = {tex}{3 \over 6} = {1 \over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: When a die is thrown, what is the probability of getting an odd number?
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6 i.e 6
Odd number = 1,3,5 i.e. 3
Probability of getting an odd number = {tex}{3 \over 6} = {1 \over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: When a die is thrown, what is the probability of getting a number greater than 3?
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6 i.e 6
Number greater than three = 4,5,6 i.e. 3
Probability of getting a number greater than 3 = {tex}{3 \over 6} = {1 \over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: When a die is thrown, what is the probability of getting a number less than 4?
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6 i.e 6
Number less than 4 = 1,2,3 i.e. 3
Probability of getting a number less than {tex}={3 \over 6} = {1 \over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: In an experiment of tossing a coin once, what is the probability of getting a head?
questions.explanation:
Total possible outcomes = 2 ( H and T)
Probability of getting a head = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: What is the probability of getting a number through 6 numbers?
questions.explanation: When there are only 6 numbers, if you select one of them, you will always be successful. So probability is 1.

questions.question: A bag has 4 red balls and 6 yellow balls. A ball is drawn from the bag without looking into the bag. Find the probability of getting a red ball.
questions.explanation:
Total number of balls = 10
Number of red balls = 4
Probability of getting a red ball = {tex}{4 \over {10}} = {2 \over 5}{/tex}

questions.question: A bag has 4 red balls and 6 yellow balls. A ball is drawn from the bag without looking into the bag. Find the probability of getting a yellow ball.
questions.explanation:
Total number of balls = 10
Number of yellow balls = 6 
Probability of getting a yellow ball = {tex}{6 \over {10}} = {3 \over 5}{/tex}

questions.question: A card is drawn at random from a pack of 52 cards. Find the probability that the card drawn is a black king.
questions.explanation:
Total card in pack = 52
Black king = 2
Probability of getting a black king = {tex}{2 \over {52}} = {1 \over 26}{/tex}

questions.question: A card is drawn at random from a pack of 52 cards. Find the probability that the card drawn is a black card
questions.explanation:
Total number of cards = 52
Black cards = 26
Probability of getting a black card = {tex}{26 \over {52}} = {1 \over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: A card is drawn at random from a pack of 52 cards. Find the probability that the card is drawn is a red king.
questions.explanation:
Total card in pack = 52
Red king = 2
Probability of getting a red king = {tex}{2 \over {52}} = {1 \over 26}{/tex}

questions.question: When a die is thrown, list the outcomes of an event of getting a prime number.
questions.explanation:
Total outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6
Prime numbers = 2,3,5

questions.question: When a die is thrown, list the outcomes of an event of getting a number greater than 5.
questions.explanation:
Total outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6
only 6 is the number which is greater than 5

questions.question: When a die is thrown, list the outcomes of an event of getting a composite number.
questions.explanation:
Total outcomes = 1,2,3,4,5,6
Composite numbers = 4,6

questions.question: A bag contains 3 red and 2 blue marbles. A marble is drawn at random. What is the probability of drawing a blue marble?
questions.explanation:
Total number of marbles = 5
Blue marbles = 2
Probability of getting a blue marble = {tex} {2 \over 5}{/tex}

questions.question: A bag contains 3 red and 2 blue marbles. A marble is drawn at random. What is the probability of drawing a red marble?
questions.explanation:
Total number of marbles = 5
Red marbles = 3
Probability of getting a red marble = {tex}{3 \over 5}{/tex}

subtopics.name: Chance and Probability - Getting a result (VIII-4.2)

questions.question: A card is drawn at random from a pack of 52 cards. Find the probability that the card drawn is a black.
questions.explanation: There are 26 black card out of 52 cards. So the probability that the card is drawn is a black is {tex}\frac{26}{52} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: A card is drawn at random from a pack of 52 cards. Find the probability that the card drawn is a red king.
questions.explanation: There are 2 red king out of 52 cards. So the probability that the card is drawn is a red king {tex}\frac{2}{52} = \frac{1}{26}{/tex}

questions.question: A bag has 10 red marbles and 6 blue marbles. A marble is drawn from the bag without looking into the bag. What is the probability of getting a blue marble?
questions.explanation:
Total number of marbles = 16
P (getting a blue marble) = {tex}{6 \over {16}} = {3 \over 8}{/tex}

questions.question: A geometric representation showing the relationship between a whole and its parts, is a
questions.explanation: A pie chart (circle graph) shows the relationship between the whole and its parts.

questions.question: In a pie chart, the total angle at the centre of the circle is:
questions.explanation: The total angle at the centre of the circle is 360°

questions.question: Which of the following is not a random experiment?

options.option: Throwing a stone from the roof of a building
Choosing a card from a deck of 52 cards

questions.explanation: Tossing a coin, rolling a die and choosing a card from a deck of 52 cards are the random experiments, as we don’t have an idea about the output of these experiments. But if we throw a stone from the roof of a building, we know the output, it will fall on the ground.

questions.question: What is the probability of choosing a vowel from the alphabets?
questions.explanation:
Total number of alphabets = 26 Total number of vowels =5
Probability of choosing a vowel from the alphabets = {tex}\frac {Total\;number \;of \;vowels}{Total \;number \;of \;alphabets }{/tex} ={tex}\frac {5}{26}{/tex}

questions.question: In a school, only 3 out of 5 students can participate in a competition. What is the probability of the students who do not make it to the competition?
questions.explanation:
Given, 3 out of 5 students can participate in a competition, i.e. 2 out of 5 students cannot participate in a competition.
Hence, probability of students who do not make it to competition = {tex}\frac {2}{5}{/tex} = 0.4

subtopics.name: Chance and Probability - Equally likely outcomes (VIII-4.2)

questions.question: Ram put some buttons on the table. There were 4 blue, 7 red, 3 black and 6 white buttons in all. All of a sudden, a cat jumped on the table and knocked out one button on the floor. What is the probability that the button on the floor is blue?
questions.explanation:
Total number of buttons = 4+ 7 + 3+ 6 = 20
Probability that button on the floor is blue = {tex}\frac{\text { Number of blue buttons }}{\text { Total number of buttons }}=\frac{4}{20}=\frac{1}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: A coin is tossed 200 times and head appeared 120 times. The probability of getting a head in this experiment is:
questions.explanation:
Given, head appeared 120 times, if a coin is tossed 200 times.
Then, probability of getting a head in this experiment {tex}=\frac{\text { Number of times head appeared }}{\text { Number of times coin is tossed }}=\frac{120}{200}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Data collected in a survey shows that 40% of the buyers are interested in buying a particular brand of toothpaste. The central angle of the sector of the pie chart representing this information is:
questions.explanation:
We know that,
The central angle of the pie chart = 360o
Buyers interested in buying a particular brand of toothpaste = 40%
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}The central angle of the sector = 40% of Central angle of the pie chart.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}The central angle of the sector = 40% of 360o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The central angle of the sector = {tex}\frac{40}{100} \times 360^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The central angle of the sector = 144o
Hence, the central angle of the sector of the pie chart representing this information is 144o

questions.question: Monthly salary of a person is ₹15000. The central angle of the sector representing his expenses on food and house rent on a pie chart is 60°. The amount he spends on food and house rent, is:
questions.explanation:
We know that,
The central angle of the pie chart = 360o
The central angle of the sector of the pie chart = 60o
The monthly salary of a person = ₹15000
Part of the monthly salary spent on food and house rent = {tex}\frac{\text{central angle of the sector of pie chart}}{\text{central angle of the pie chart}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Part of the monthly salary spent on food and house rent = {tex}\frac{60^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Part of the monthly salary spent on food and house rent = {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Amount of monthly salary spent on food and house rent = {tex}\frac{1}{6} \times 15000{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Amount spent on food and house rent = ₹ 2500
Hence, the amount he spends on food and house rent is ₹ 2500

questions.question: Total number of outcomes, when a ball is drawn from a bag which contains 3 red, 5 black and 4 blue balls, is:
questions.explanation:
Given, a bag contains 3 red, 5 black and 4 blue balls.
Then, total number of outcomes = Total number of balls = 3 + 5 + 4 = 12

questions.question: Numbers 1 to 5 are written on separate slips, i.e. one number on one slip and put in a box. Wahida pick a slip from the box without looking at it. What is the probability that the slip bears an odd number?

Number of slips bears an odd number = 3
Probability that the slip bears an odd number = {tex}\frac{\text { Number of slips wih odd numbers }}{\text { Total number of slips }}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: A coin is tossed two times. The number of possible outcomes is
questions.explanation:
When a coin is tossed two times the possible outcomes are
HH – Two heads
HT – First head and second tail
TH – First tail and second tail
TT – Two tails
Therefore,
The sample space is {HH, HT, TH, TT} = 4
Hence, the number of possible outcomes is 4.

questions.question: A coin is tossed three times. The number of possible outcomes is:
questions.explanation: Number of possible outcomes is 8, i.e. HHH, HHT, HTH, THH, TTH, THT, HTT, TTT.

questions.question:
The pie-graph given here shows expenditure detail on building a house. By supposing this, the total expenditure on building this home is ₹600000.

questions.question: Two coins are tossed simultaneously. What is the probability of getting one head and one tail?
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 4
Number of favourable outcomes = 2 [i.e. (H, T), (T, H)]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Probability of getting one head and one tail = {tex}\frac{2}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: A bag contains 3 white and 2 red balls. One ball is drawn at random. What is the probability that the ball drawn is red?

questions.question: A dice is thrown. What is the probability of getting an even number?
questions.explanation:

subtopics.name: Chance and Probability - Outcomes as events (VIII-4.2)

questions.question: From a well-shuffled deck of 52 cards, one card is drawn at random. What is the probability that the drawn card is a queen?
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes in a deck of cards = 52
Number of favourable outcomes (queen) = 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P (getting queen) = {tex}\frac{4}{52}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{13}{/tex}

questions.question: Monthly salary of a person is ₹ 50000. The central angle of the sector representing his expenses on medicine and entertainment on a pie chart is 90°. The amount he spends on medicine and entertainment is

questions.question: A die is thrown. The probability of getting an odd prime number is ________.
questions.explanation:
Total number of outcomes = 6
Odd prime numbers are {3, 5} i.e., 2 in number.

= {tex}\frac{2}{6}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: A bag contains a number of marbles of which 80 are red, 24 are white and the rest rate blue. If the probability of randomly selecting a blue marble from this bag is {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}, how many blue marbles are there in the bag?
questions.explanation:
Let total no. of ball be x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} No. of blue balls = x - (80 + 24)
= x - 104
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-104}{x}=\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
5(x - 104) = x
5x - 520 = x
4x = 52
x = 130
{tex}\therefore{/tex} No. of blue balls = 130 - 104
= 26

questions.question: What is the probability that a leap year selected at random will contain 53 Sundays?
questions.explanation:
In a leap year there are 366 days.
366 days = 52 weeks and 2 days.
These remaining two days can be

  1. Sunday, Monday
  2. Monday, Tuesday
  3. Tuesday, Wednesday
  4. Wednesday, Thursday
  5. Thursday, Friday
  6. Friday, Saturday
  7. Saturday, Sunday

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total no. of cases (a to g) = 7
No. of favourable cases (a, g) = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required probability = {tex}\frac{2}{7}{/tex}

questions.question: One card is drawn from a pack of 52 cards, each of the 52 cards being equally likely to be drawn. The probability that the drawn card is either red or king is
questions.explanation:
Out of 52 cards one card can be draw in 52 ways. Total number of elementary events = 52.
There are 26 red cards including 2 red kings and there are 2 more kings.
Hence there are 28 cards which are either red or king. Out of this 1 card can be drawn in 28 ways.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required probability = {tex}\frac{28}{52}=\frac{7}{13}{/tex}

The probability that a student passes in Mathematics is {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} and he passes in physics in {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex}. Assuming that passing in Mathematics and Physics are independent of each other, what is the probability that he passes in Mathematics and fails in Physics?
questions.explanation:
P(pass in Mathematics) = {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex}
P(pass in Physics) = {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P(fail in Physics) = {tex}1-\frac{2}{5}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P(pass in Mathematics and fail in Physics)
= {tex}\frac{4}{9} \times \frac{3}{5}=\frac{4}{15}{/tex}

questions.question: A man A speaks truth in 80% of the cases and another man B in 90% of the cases. While stating the same fact, what is the probability that they contradict?
questions.explanation:
Probability (contradiction)
= P(A speaks truth) {tex}\times{/tex} P(B does not speak truth) + P(A does not speak truth) {tex}\times{/tex} P(B speaks truth)
= {tex}\frac{80}{100} \times \frac{10}{100}+\frac{20}{100} \times \frac{90}{100}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{8}{100}+\frac{18}{100}=\frac{26}{100}=\frac{13}{50}{/tex}.

If Rajnish has two children and he truthfully answers yes to the question Is atleast one of your children a girl?
The probability that both his children are girls is
questions.explanation:

questions.question: A card is drawn from a well-shuffled deck of playing cards. Find the probability of drawing a face card.
questions.explanation:
Number of face cards (Jack, Queen, King and Ace) in a deck of cards = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 16
Total no. of cards = 52.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} total number of possible outcomes = 52
Let E1 represents event of drawing a face card.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} no. of favourable outcomes = 16
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P(E1) = {tex}\frac{\text { Number of favourable outcomes. }}{\text { Total number of outcomes }}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{16}{52}=\frac{4}{13}{/tex}

questions.question: From a well shuffled deck of playing cards, what is the probability of drawing a red face card?
questions.explanation:
Let E2 represents event of drawing a red face card.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of favourable outcomes = 4 + 4 = 8
P(E2) = {tex}\frac{\text { Number of favourable outcomes }}{\text { Total number of outcomes }}{/tex}

questions.question: If one member of a pythagorean triplet is 2m, then the other two members are
questions.explanation:
We know that, for any natural number m greater than 1, (2m, m2 - 1, m2 +1) is a Pythagorean n triplet.
So, one number is 2m, then other two numbers are m2 + 1 and m2 - 1. e.g If one number is 4, then the pythagorean triplet, 2m = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = 2
m2 + 1 = 22 + 1 = 4 + 1 = 5
and m2 - 1 = 22 - 1 = 4 - 1 = 3
Now, 32 + 42 = 52
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 + 1 6 = 25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25 = 25
So, 3, 4 and 5 are pythagorean triplets.

questions.question: The sum of first n odd natural numbers is
questions.explanation:
Sum of first n odd natural numbers = {tex}\Sigma(2 n-1)=2 \Sigma n-n{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2 \times n(n+1)}{2}-n{/tex} 
= n(n + 1) - n
= n2 + n - n
= n2

questions.question: How many natural numbers lie between 182 and 192?
questions.explanation:
Here is a solution using a sequence approach:
182 = 324
192 = 361
The natural numbers between 182 and 192 are the numbers in the sequence:
325, 326,..., 359, 360
Using the formula for the number of terms, n in and A.P. sequence:
last term = first term + (n -1) {tex}\times{/tex} common difference, we get:
360 = 325 + (n - 1) {tex}\times{/tex} 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 360 - 325 = n -1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35 = n - 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 35 +1 = 36.
Therefore, 36 natural numbers lie between 18 squared and 19 squared.
or  The natural numbers lie between n squared and (n+1) squared = 2n
hence,  natural numbers lie between 18 squared and 19 squared = 2{tex}\times{/tex}18 = 36

questions.question: The square root of {tex}\frac{36}{5}{/tex} correct upto two decimal places is ________.
questions.explanation:
Since we have to find the square root of {tex}\frac{36}{5}{/tex} correct upto two decimal places. So, we have to find its square root upto three decimal places.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{36}{5}{/tex} = 7.200000
Now,
 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{\frac{36}{5}}{/tex} = 2.683 {tex}\approx{/tex} 2.68

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt{610+\sqrt{212+\sqrt{169}}}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\sqrt{610+\sqrt{212+\sqrt{169}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{610+\sqrt{212+13}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{610+\sqrt{225}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{610+15}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{625}{/tex} = 25

questions.question: If the three numbers are in the ratio 3 : 4 : 7, so that the sum of their squares is 1184. Find the numbers respectively.
questions.explanation:
Let the numbers be 3x, 4x and 7x
According to question, we have
(3x)2 + (4x)2 + (7x)2 = 1184
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x2 + 16x2 + 49x2 = 1184 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 74x2 = 1184
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = {tex}\frac{1184}{74}{/tex} = 16 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 4
Hence, the numbers are 12, 16 and 28

questions.question: Square root of {tex}\frac{0.081}{0.0064} \times \frac{0.484}{6.25} \times \frac{2.5}{12.1}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{\frac{0.081}{0.0064} \times \frac{0.484}{6.25} \times \frac{2.5}{12.1}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\frac{81}{64} \times \frac{484}{625} \times \frac{25}{121}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{9}{8} \times \frac{22}{25} \times \frac{5}{11}{/tex} = 0.45

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\left(\sqrt{\frac{625}{9801}}+\sqrt{\frac{576}{1089}}\right) {/tex} {tex}\times\left(\sqrt{\frac{121}{\sqrt{21025}+144}}\right){/tex}.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\left(\sqrt{\frac{625}{9801}}+\sqrt{\frac{576}{1089}}\right) {/tex} {tex}\times\left(\sqrt{\frac{121}{\sqrt{21025}+144}}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{25}{99}+\frac{24}{33}\right) \times\left(\frac{11}{\sqrt{145+144}}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{25+72}{99}\right) \times \frac{11}{\sqrt{289}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{97}{99} \times \frac{11}{17}=\frac{97}{153}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a square field is {tex}80 \frac{244}{729}{/tex} sq. m. The length of each side of the field, is ________.
questions.explanation:
Area of square field = {tex}80 \frac{244}{729}{/tex} sq. m = {tex}\frac {58564}{729}{/tex} sq. m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Side of square field = {tex}\sqrt{\frac{58564}{729}}{/tex} m = {tex}\frac{242}{27}{/tex} m = 8.96 m

questions.question: A teacher arranges his students in rows to form a square. He finds that in doing so, 45 students are left out. If the total number of students be 4945, then find the number of students in each row.
questions.explanation:
Let x be the number of students in each row. To form a square, number of students in each row must be equal to the number of rows.
According to question,
Number of students who form square = 4945 - 45 = 4900
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x = 4900
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 4900 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 70
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of students in each row = 70

questions.question: A housing society has been allotted a square piece of land measuring 2550.25 sq. m. What is the side of the plot?
questions.explanation:
Side of plot = {tex}\sqrt{2550.25}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{\frac{255025}{100}}=\frac{505}{10}{/tex} = 50.5 m

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+\sqrt{108+\sqrt{154+\sqrt{225}}}}}{/tex} {tex}+\left(\sqrt{\frac{225}{729}}-\sqrt{\frac{25}{144}}\right) \div \sqrt{\frac{16}{81}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+\sqrt{108+\sqrt{154+\sqrt{225}}}}}{/tex} {tex}+\left(\sqrt{\frac{225}{729}}-\sqrt{\frac{25}{144}}\right) \div \sqrt{\frac{16}{81}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+\sqrt{108+\sqrt{154+15}}}}{/tex} {tex}+\left(\frac{15}{27}-\frac{5}{12}\right) \div\left(\frac{4}{9}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+\sqrt{108+13}}}{/tex} {tex}+\left(\frac{60-45}{108}\right)\div\frac{4}{9}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+11}}+\frac{15}{108} \times \frac{9}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{10+6}+\frac{5}{16}{/tex}
= {tex}4+\frac{5}{16}=\frac{64+5}{16}=\frac{69}{16}{/tex}

questions.question: The smallest number by which 294 must be multiplied so that the product becomes a perfect square is ________.
Square numbers can only have _______ at the end.

options.option: Both Odd and Even number of zeroes

questions.explanation: Even number of zeroes

questions.question: 7396 students are sitting in an auditorium in such a manner that there are as many students in a row as there are rows in the auditorium. How many rows are there in the auditorium?
questions.explanation:
Let number of students sitting in a row = ‘x’
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of rows in auditorium = ‘x’
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of students sitting in auditorium
= x {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2
Now x2 = 7396
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\sqrt{7396}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\sqrt{7396}{/tex} = 86
Number of rows in auditorium = 86

questions.question: The smallest number by which 136 must be multiplied so that it becomes a perfect square is
questions.explanation:
Resolve 136 into prime factors and make group of two of each prime factor
136 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex}17

136 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 17
We find that 2 and 17 doesn’t appear in group of two. So, 136 has to be multiplied with 34 to make it a perfect square.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 136 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (17 {tex}\times{/tex} 17)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4624 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (17 {tex}\times{/tex} 17)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Required square root = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 68

questions.question: The smallest number by which 3888 must be divided so that the resulting number is a perfect square is
questions.explanation:
Resolving 3888 into its prime factors, we find that
3888 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
3888 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} 3

Here we find that prime factor 3 is appearing alone.
So, if we divide 3888 by 3, we will get a perfect square number
{tex}\frac{3888}{3}{/tex} = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex}3) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1296 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{1296}{/tex} = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 36

questions.question: The product of two numbers is 1936. If one number is 4 times the other, the numbers are
questions.explanation:
Let one number = a
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Second number = 4a
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4a {tex}\times{/tex} a = 1936
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = {tex}\frac{1936}{4}{/tex} = 484
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = 484
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (11 {tex}\times{/tex} 11)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 = 22

and 4a = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 = 88
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Numbers are 22 and 88.

questions.question: Class X-A of a school collected ₹ 1024 as contribution to AP flood relief fund. If each student contributed as one student in the class. The number of students in the class are
questions.explanation:
Let no. of students in the class = x
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Contribution of each student = ₹ x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x = 1024
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 1024
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 32
{tex}\therefore{/tex} no. of students in the class = 32

questions.question: The least square number exactly divisible by 4, 6, 10, 15 is
questions.explanation:
Least number which is divisible by 4, 6, 10, 15 is LCM (4, 6, 10, 15)
LCM (4, 6, 10, 15) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2
LCM (4, 6, 10, 15) = 60
60 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5

Here we find that 3 and 5 occurs alone. So, if we multiply 60 by 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 15, we get a perfect square no.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 900
900 is the least square no. which is divisible by 4, 6, 10, 15

questions.question: The least number to be subtracted from 24136 to make it a perfect square
questions.explanation:
Let us extract the square root from 24136.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} 24136, is 111 more than (155)2. So if we Subtract 111 from 24136, we will get a perfect sq. number

questions.question: What must be added to 24136 to make it a perfect square?
questions.explanation:
24136 >1552
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Least number must be added to 24136 to make it perfect square
= (156)2 - 24136
= 24336 - 24136 = 200

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt{388+\sqrt{127+\sqrt{289}}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{388+\sqrt{127+\sqrt{289}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{388+\sqrt{127+17}}{/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{289}{/tex} = 17)
= {tex}\sqrt{388+\sqrt{144}}{/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{144}{/tex} = 12)
= {tex}\sqrt{388+12}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{400}{/tex}
= 20 ({tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{400}{/tex} = 20)

questions.question: The sides of a rectangular field are 80 m and 18 m respectively. The length of the diagonal is
questions.explanation:
All angles of a rectangle are right angle
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of diagonal
= {tex}\sqrt{80^2+18^2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{6400+324}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{6724}{/tex} = 82 cm

questions.question: The square root of 1,764 is:
questions.explanation:
1764 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 72 {tex}\times{/tex} 32

= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= 42

questions.question: The square root of 53,361 is:

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{53361}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{11^2 \times 21^2}{/tex}
= 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 = 231

questions.question: The least perfect square which is divisible by 2, 4 and 6 is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} LCM of 2, 4, 6 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 12
Least perfect square which is a multiple of 12 is 36.

questions.question: You have a rectangular frame that is 40 cm by 60 cm. Can you put a square picture that has an area of 800 cm2 completely inside the frame?
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangular frame = 40 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 60 cm = 2400 cm2
Area of square picture = 800 cm2
Area of picture < Area of frame
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Picture can be put completely inside the frame.

questions.question: Pooja’s rectangular living room is 12 m by 5.5 m She has a square rug that covers {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} the area of the floor. The side length of square rug is
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangular room = 12 m {tex}\times{/tex} 5.5 m = 66 m2
Area covered by the rug is
= {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 66 m2
= {tex}\frac{198}{7}{/tex} m2
The rug is in the square shape.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} side of rug = {tex}\left(\frac{198}{7}\right)^{\frac12}{/tex} = 5.3 m

questions.question: The Pythagorean triplet whose smallest number is 14 is:
questions.explanation:
We can find pythagorean triplet by using general formula 2m, m2 - 1, m2 + 1
2m = 14
m = 7
Second number = m2 - 1
= 72 - 1 = 48
Third number = (7)2 + 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Pythagorean triplet is (17, 48, 50) with 14 as the smallest number.

questions.question: Which of the following integers is the square of an integer for every integer n?
questions.explanation:
If n is an integer then (n + 1)2 is also integer
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n2 + 2n + 1 is a square of an integer for every integer n.

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+\sqrt{108+\sqrt{154+\sqrt{225}}}}}{/tex} is

= {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{25+11}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{10+\sqrt{36}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{10+6}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{16}{/tex} = 4

questions.question: The smallest of {tex}\sqrt{8}+\sqrt{5}, \sqrt{7}+\sqrt{6}, \sqrt{10}+\sqrt{3}{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt{11}+\sqrt{2}{/tex} is:

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Smallest is {tex}\sqrt{11}+\sqrt{2}{/tex}.
