questions.question: If T = {tex}2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{L}{g}}{/tex}, then 'L' is equal to
questions.explanation:
T = {tex}2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{L}}{\mathrm{g}}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt{\frac{\overline{\mathrm{L}}}{\mathrm{g}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{T}}{2 \pi}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{L}}{\mathrm{g}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{T^2}{(2 \pi)^2}{/tex}
L = {tex}\frac{T^2 \mathrm{~g}}{4 \pi^2}{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}4 \sqrt{3}(2 \sqrt{27}-4 \sqrt{48}){/tex} - {tex}5 \sqrt{3}(\sqrt{12}-3 \sqrt{75}){/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}4 \sqrt{3}(2 \sqrt{27}-4 \sqrt{48}){/tex} - {tex}5 \sqrt{3}(\sqrt{12}-3 \sqrt{75}){/tex}
= {tex}4 \sqrt{3}(2 \sqrt{3 \times 3 \times 3}{/tex} - {tex}4 \sqrt{4 \times 4 \times 3}{/tex} - {tex}-5 \sqrt{3}(\sqrt{2 \times 2 \times 3}{/tex} - {tex}3 \sqrt{5 \times 5 \times 3}){/tex}
= {tex}4 \sqrt{3}(2 \times 3 \sqrt{3}-4 \times 4 \sqrt{3}){/tex} - {tex}5 \sqrt{3}(2 \sqrt{3}-3 \times 5 \sqrt{3}){/tex}
= {tex}4 \sqrt{3}(6 \sqrt{3}-16 \sqrt{3}){/tex} - {tex}5 \sqrt{3}(2 \sqrt{3}-15 \sqrt{3}){/tex}
= {tex}4 \sqrt{3}(-10 \sqrt{3}){/tex} - {tex}5 \sqrt{3}(-13 \sqrt{3}){/tex}
= {tex}-40 \sqrt{3 \times 3}{/tex} + {tex}65 \sqrt{3} \times 3{/tex}
= -40 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 65 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = -120 +195 = 75

questions.question: Find the least square number which is exactly divisible by 3, 4, 5, 6 and 8.
questions.explanation:
LCM of 3, 4, 5, 6, 8
{tex}\begin{array}{c|c} 2 & 3,4,5,6,8 \\ \hline 2 & 3,2,5,3,4 \\ \hline 2 & 3,1,5,3,2 \\ \hline 3 & 3,1,5,3,1 \\ \hline 5 & 1,1,5,1,1 \\ \hline & 1,1,1,1,1 \end{array}{/tex}
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 120

questions.question: The least 6 digit number which is perfect square is
questions.explanation:
Least six digit number is 100000, which is. not a perfect square because it has odd number of zeroes.
First let us extract the square number hidden in it.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} We find that 100000 > (316)2 by 154 Next square number (317)2 > 100000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (316)2 < 100000 < (317)2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} If we add (317)2 - 100000 = 489 to 100000
We get least six digit perfect sq. no.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Least four digit perfect square no. is 100489.

questions.question: How many square metres of carpet will be required for a square room of side 6.5 m to be carpeted.
questions.explanation:
Side of room = 6.5 m
Area of square = Side {tex}\times{/tex} Side
= 6.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6.5 = 42.25 m2
So, 42.25 m2 of carpet will be required for the room to be carpeted.

questions.question: Area of a square field is 22500 m2. A man cycles along its boundary at 15 km/ hr. The time will be taken by a man to return to starting point, is
questions.explanation:
Let the side of square field = 'a' m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of square field = a2 sq. m
a2 = 22500 m2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 150 m
Speed of cycling = 15 km / hr
= {tex}\frac{15 \times 1000}{60 \times 60}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{25}{6} {/tex} m/s.
Now, total distance to be covered along the boundary = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 150 = 600 m
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{25}{6} \mathrm{~m}{/tex} is covered in 1 sec.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 600 m is covered in {tex}\frac{600}{25} \times 6{/tex} = 144 sec = 2 min 24 sec.

questions.question: Find the side of a square whose area is equal to the area of a rectangle with sides 6.4 m and 2.5 m.
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangle = Length x Breadth = 6.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 = 16 m2
Now, area of rectangle = Area of square 16 m2 = Side {tex}\times{/tex} Side
Side2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
Side = 4 m

questions.question: A gardener arranges plants in rows to form a square. He finds that in doing so 15 plants are left out. If the total number of plants are 3984, the number of plants in each row are,
questions.explanation:
Gardener arranges (3984 - 15) = 3969 plants in different rows to form a square. Let no. of plants in each row be ‘x’
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x = 3969
x2 = 3969 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 63

questions.question: The area of a square plot is {tex}101 \frac{1}{400}{/tex} m2. Find the length of one side of the plot.
questions.explanation:
Area of square = Side {tex}\times{/tex} Side
{tex}101 \frac{1}{400}{/tex} = Side2
Side = {tex}\sqrt{101 \frac{1}{400}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\frac{40401}{400}}{/tex}
{tex}\begin{array}{c|c} 201 & 40401 \\ \hline 201 & 201 \\ \hline & 1 \end{array}{/tex}
{tex}\begin{array}{c|c} 2 & 400 \\ \hline 2 & 200 \\ \hline 2 & 100 \\ \hline 2 & 50 \\ \hline 5 & 25 \\ \hline 5 & 5 \\ \hline & 1 \end{array}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{\frac{201 \times 201}{2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 5 \times 5}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{201}{2 \times 2 \times 5}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{201}{20}{/tex} = {tex}10 \frac{1}{20}{/tex} m

questions.question: A number is multiplied by {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} times itself and 18 is added to the result. If the final result is 2901, the number is
questions.explanation:
Let the required number be ‘x’ Now, as per question.
x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{3} x{/tex} + 18 = 2901
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^2}{3}{/tex} = 2901 - 18 = 2883
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 8649
x2 = (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 ) {tex}\times{/tex} (31 {tex}\times{/tex} 31)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 31 = 93

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{1369}{/tex} + {tex}\sqrt{0.0615+x}{/tex} = 37.25, then x is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{1369}{/tex} + {tex}\sqrt{0.0615+x}{/tex} = 37.25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{0.0615+x}{/tex} = 37.25 - {tex}\sqrt{1369}{/tex}
= 37.25 - 37 = 0.25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.0615 + x = (0.25)2 = 0.0625
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0.0625 - 0.0615
= 0.001 = {tex}\frac{1}{10^3}{/tex} = 10-3

questions.question: Find the smallest perfect square divisible by 3, 4, 5 and 6.
questions.explanation:
The least number divisible by 3, 4, 5 and 6 is their LCM. The LCM of 3, 4, 5 and 6 is 60. Now, 60 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3.
We see that prime factors 5 and 3 are not in pairs.
Therefore 60 is not a perfect square. So, 60 should be multiplied by 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 15 to get a perfect square.
Thus, the required least square number = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 900.

questions.question: The area of a circular play ground is {tex}\frac{3168}{7}{/tex}m2. the diameter of the ground is
questions.explanation:
Area of circular ground = {tex}\frac{3168}{7} {/tex} m2
{tex}\pi r^2=\frac{3168}{7}{/tex}
where r is the radius
r2 = {tex}\frac{3168}{7 \times \pi}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3168 \times 7}{7 \times 22}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3168}{22}{/tex}
r = {tex}\sqrt{\frac{3168}{22}}{/tex} = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} d = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 24 m

questions.question: If the product of four consecutive natural numbers increased by a natural number p is a perfect square, then the value of p is
questions.explanation:
Let the four consecutive natural numbers be x, x + 1, x + 2 and x + 3
Then, A perfect square = x(x + 1) (x + 2) (x + 3) + p
= x(x + 3) (x + 1) (x + 2) + p
= (x2 + 3x) {tex}\times{/tex} (x2 + 3x + 2) + p
= (x2 + 3x)2 + 2(x2 + 3x + 2) + p
= (x2 + 3x)2 + 2(x2 + 3x) + p
The expression on the right hand side will be a perfect square if and only p = 1 Perfect square number
= [(x2 + 3x)2 + 2(x2 + 3x) + 1]
= (x2 + 3x+ 1)2

questions.question: What will be the number of zeroes in the squares of the number 100?
questions.explanation:
Number of zeroes at the end of the number 100 = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of zeroes at the end of the square of the number 100 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 4

questions.question: A four - digit perfect square whose first two digits and last two digits taken separately are also perfect squares, is:

questions.question: What will be the number of zeroes in the square of the number 50?

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of zeroes at the end of the square of the number 50 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 2

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{0.04 \times 0.4 \times \mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.04 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}, then value of {tex}\frac{b}{a}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{0.04 \times 0.4 \times a}{/tex} = 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.04 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{b}{/tex}
0.04 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} a = {tex}(0.4 \times 0.04 \times \sqrt{b})^2{/tex} (Squaring both sides)
{tex}\frac{0.04 \times 0.4 \times a}{(0.4)^2 \times(0.04)^2}{/tex} = b
{tex}\frac{b}{a}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{(0.4) \times(0.04)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1000}{4 \times 4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{250}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{125}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If a = 0.1039, then the value of 3a - {tex}\sqrt{4 a^2-4 a+1}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have to find the value of
3a - {tex}\sqrt{4 a^2-4 a+1}{/tex}
Now, 4a2 - 4a + 1
= 4a2 - 2a - 2a + 1
= 2a (2a - 1) - (2a - 1)
= (2a - 1) (2a - 1) = (2a - 1)2
3a - {tex}\sqrt{(2 a-1)^2}{/tex} = = 3a - 2a + 1 = a + 1
= 0.1039 + 1 = 1.1039

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{\frac{125 a^6 b^4 c^2}{5 a^4 b^2}}{/tex} = x then find {tex}\frac{x^2}{a b c}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Consider {tex}\sqrt{\frac{125 a^6 b^4 c^2}{5 a^4 b^2}}{/tex} = x

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5abc = x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 25a2b2c2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^2}{a b c}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{25 a^2 b^2 c}{a b c}{/tex} = 25abc

questions.question: 25, 50, 100, 89 : Of these numbers which is not a sum of two squares?
The area of a rectangular field whose length is twice its breadth is 2450 m2. Find the perimeter of the field.
questions.explanation:
Let the breadth of the field be x metres. Then length of the field is 2x metres.
Therefore, area of the rectangular field = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth = (2x)(x) = (2x2)m2
Given that area is 2450 m2
Therefore, 2x2 = 2450
x2 = {tex}\frac{2450}{2}{/tex}
x = {tex}\sqrt{1225}{/tex} or x = 35 m
Hence, breadth = 35 m and length 35 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 70 m
Perimeter of the field = 2(1 + b) = 2(70 + 35)m = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 m = 210 m

questions.question: What is the value of {tex}\sqrt{\frac{x+2 y}{8 y}}{/tex} if x = 5.6 and y = 3.1 correct to 2 decimal places?
questions.explanation:

questions.question: Find the square root of {tex}x^{m^2-n^2} \cdot x^{n^2+2 m n} \cdot x^{n^2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Consider {tex}x^{m^2-n^2} \cdot x^{n^2+2 m n} \cdot x^{n^2}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{m^2-n^2+n^2+2 m n+n^2}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{m^2+2 m n+n^2}{/tex} = {tex}x^{(m+n)^2}{/tex}
So, required square root = {tex}x^{\frac{(m+n)^2}{2}}{/tex}

questions.question: If x2 + y2 = 47 and xy = {tex}\frac{19}{2}{/tex} then the value of 3(x + y)2 + (x - y)2 is
questions.explanation:
x2 + y2 = 47 ....(i)
xy = {tex}\frac{19}{2}{/tex} ... (ii)
(x + y)2 = x2 + y2 + 2xy
= 47 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{19}{2}{/tex} = 47 + 19
(x + y)2 = 66
Also, (x - y)2 = x2 + y2 - 2xy
= 47 - 19 = 28
3(x + y)2 + (x - y)2 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 66 + 28 = 226

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{5^n}{/tex} = 125 then find the value of {tex}5^{\sqrt[4]{64}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}5^{\sqrt[n]{64}}{/tex} = 52 ({tex}\because{/tex} 6th root of 64 = 2)
= 25

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\sqrt{\sqrt{63}}+\sqrt{56}{/tex}.

= {tex}\sqrt[4]{7} \sqrt{(3+2 \sqrt{2})}{/tex}
Let {tex}\sqrt{3+2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{y}{/tex}
on squaring, we get x = 1, 2 or y = 2, 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required value {tex}=\sqrt[4]{7}(\sqrt{2}+1).{/tex}

questions.question: During a mass drill exercise, 6250 students of different schools are arranged in rows such that the number of students in each row is equal to the number of rows. In doing so, the instructor finds out that 9 children are left out. Find the number of children in each row of the square.
questions.explanation:
Total number of students = 6250
Number of students forming a square = 6250- 9 = 6241
Thus, 6241 students form a big square which has number of rows equal to the number of students in each row.
Let the number of students in each row be x, then the number of rows = x
Therefore, x {tex}\times{/tex} x = 6241
or x = {tex}\sqrt{6241}{/tex} = 79
Hence, there are 79 students in each row of the square formed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Pythagorean triplet?

{tex}\therefore{/tex} (6, 8, 10) is a Pythagorean triplet.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers is a perfect cube?
questions.explanation:

216 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)
Clearly, in the grouping, the factors of triplets of equal factors, no factor is left over.
So, 216 is a perfect cube.

questions.question: The unit’s digit of the cube of a number is 9. The unit’s digit of its cube root is ________.
Two cubes have volumes in the ratio 1 : 27. The ratio of the area of the face of one to that of the other is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let sides of two cubes be a1 and a2
So, {tex}\frac{a_1^3}{a_2^3}=\frac{1}{27}{/tex}
Taking cube root, we get {tex}\frac{a_1}{a_2}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
Area of face of first cube = {tex}a_1^2{/tex}
And area of face of other cube = {tex}a_2^2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required ratio = {tex}\frac{a_1^2}{a_2^2}=\left(\frac{a_1}{a_2}\right)^2{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^2=\frac{1}{9}{/tex} i.e., 1 : 9

Clearly, to make it a perfect cube it should be multiplied by 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 4.

By resolving 5184 into its prime factors, we get
{tex}\begin{array}{l|l} 2 & 5184 \\ \hline 2 & 2592 \\ \hline 2 & 1296 \\ \hline 2 & 648 \\ \hline 2 & 324 \\ \hline 2 & 162 \\ \hline 3 & 81 \\ \hline 3 & 27 \\ \hline 3 & 9 \\ \hline & 3 \end{array}{/tex}
5184 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} 3
In a perfect cube prime factors occurs thrice.
We find that in 5184 prime factor ‘3’ is ungrouped.
The smallest number by which we should multiply 5184 to make it a perfect cube is 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 9.

questions.question: Find the smallest number by which the number 100 must be multiplied to obtain a perfect cube.

{tex}\begin{array}{l|l} 2 & 100 \\ \hline 2 & 50 \\ \hline 5 & 25 \\ \hline & 5 \end{array}{/tex}
Here prime factors of ’2’ and '5' are ungrouped
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Smallest number which is required is 10

questions.question: The cube of the number p is 16 times the number. Then find p where p {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 and p {tex}\ne{/tex} 4.

p(p2 - 16) = 0 either p = 0 or p2 -16 = 0
But p {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 (Given)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p2 - 16 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\ne{/tex}4
But p {tex}\ne{/tex} -4, Hence p = 4.

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt [3]{4096}{/tex} is ________.

{tex}(4096)^{(1 / \sqrt{3})^{(\sqrt{2})^2}}{/tex}

questions.question: A picnic is being planned in a school for Class VII. Girls are  60% of the total number of students and are 18 in number. Find the ratio of the number of girls to the number of boys in the class.

questions.question: The population of a city was 20,000 in the year 1997. It increased at a rate of 5% p.a. Find the population at the end of the year 2000.

The population is calculated as compound Interest Formula
A = P{tex}{\left( {1 + {r \over {100}}} \right)^n}{/tex}
Intial Population (P) = 20,000
Rate(r) = 5%
Time(n) = 3 Years
A (Population at the end of year 2,000) = 20,000{tex}{\left( {1 + {5 \over {100}}} \right)^3}{/tex}
= 23,152.5 ( But persons cannot be taken in points)
= 23,153

questions.question: Calculate the Amount on Rs 10,800 for 3 years at 12.5% per annum compounded annually.
questions.explanation:
A( Amount) =?

n(Time) = 3Years
r (Rate) = 12.5%
A = {tex}P{\left( {1 + {r \over {100}}} \right)^n}{/tex}
A = 10,800{tex}{\left( {1 + \frac{{12.5}}{{100}}} \right)^3}{/tex}
= 10,800{tex}{\left( {\frac{{45}}{{40}}} \right)^3}{/tex}
= Rs.15,377.34

questions.question: Calculate the Compound interest on Rs 10,800 for 3 years at 12.5% per annum Compounded annually.
questions.explanation:
A( Amount) = ?, P (Principal) = Rs.10,800, T(Time) = 3 years, R (Rate) = 12.5%
{tex}A = P{\left( {1 + \frac{r}{{100}}} \right)^n}{/tex}
{tex}A = 10,800{\left( {1 + \frac{{12.5}}{{100}}} \right)^3}{/tex}
{tex} = 10,800{\left( {\frac{{45}}{{40}}} \right)^3}{/tex}
= Rs.15,377.34
Compound Interest = Amount - Principal
= 15,377.34-10,800 = Rs.4,577.34

questions.question: Suppose a certain sum doubles in 2 yr at r% rate of simple interest per annum and R% rate of interest per annum compounded annually. Then,
questions.explanation:

questions.question: The compound interest on ₹50000 at 4% per annum for 2yr compounded annually is
questions.explanation:
Given, principal (P) = ₹50000
Rate of interest (R%) = 4% per annum
Time period (7) = 2 yr
We know that,
{tex}A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{T}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad A=50000\left(1+\frac{4}{100}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}50000\left(1+\frac{1}{25}\right)^{2}=50000 \times \frac{26}{25} \times \frac{26}{25}=80 \times 26 \times 26{/tex} = ₹54080
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound interest, CI = A - P = ₹ 54080 - ₹ 50000 = ₹4080

questions.question: If marked price of an article is ₹1200 and the discount is 12%, then the selling price of the article is

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount = Discount % on marked price
= {tex}\frac{12}{100} \times 1200=12 \times 12{/tex} = ₹144
{tex}\because{/tex} Selling price = Marked price - Discount
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Selling price = ₹1200 - ₹144 = ₹1056

questions.question: If 90% of x is 315 km, then the value of x is

{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{315 \times 100}{90}=\frac{315 \times 10}{9}{/tex} = 350
x = 350 km

questions.question: If a % is the discount per cent on marked price x, then discount is

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount = Discount % on marked price = {tex}x \times\frac{a}{100} {/tex}
Hence, {tex}x \times\frac{a}{100} {/tex} is correct.

questions.question: The marked price of an article is ₹80 and it is sold at ₹76, then the discount rate is

Selling price of the article = ₹ 76
We know that,
Selling price = Marked price - Discount
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount = Marked price - Selling price
Discount = ₹ 80 - ₹ 76 = ₹ 4
Discount % = {tex}\frac{4}{80} \times 100=\frac{40}{8}=5 \% \quad\left[\because \text { discount } \%=\frac{\text { discount }}{\text { marked price }} \times 100\right]{/tex}

questions.question: A jacket was sold for ₹1120 after allowing a discount of 20%. The marked price of the jacket is

Discount % on marked price = 20%
Selling price of jacket = ₹ 1120
Then, {tex}1120=x-x \times \frac{20}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 1120=x-\frac{x}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 1120=\frac{4 x}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{1120 \times 5}{4}=280 \times 5{/tex} = ₹ 1400
So, marked price of the jacket is ₹ 1400.

questions.question: 40% of [100 - 20% of 300] is equal to
questions.explanation:
40% of [100 - 20% of 300]
= {tex}\frac{40}{100} \times\left[100-\frac{20}{100} \times 300\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{40}{100}[100-60]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{40}{100} \times 40=4 \times 4{/tex} = 16

questions.question: To gain 25% after allowing a discount of 10%, the shopkeeper must mark the price of the article which cost him ₹360 as
questions.explanation:
Let the marked price of the article be ₹x.
Cost price of the article = ₹360
According to the question,
{tex}x-x \times \frac{10}{100}-\frac{25 \times 360}{100}{/tex} = 360
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x-\frac{x}{10}-90{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} discount is always calculated on marked price and profit is always calculated on cost price]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{9 x}{10}-90{/tex} = 360
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{9 x}{10}=360+90{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{450 \times 10}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{9 x}{10}=450{/tex}
= x = ₹500
So, the market price is ₹500.

questions.question: A shirt with marked price Rs 800 was sold at Rs 680. The rate of discount allowed on the shirt is
questions.explanation:
The marked price = Rs 800{tex}{/tex}
Selling price = Rs 680
Discount = 800 - 680 = Rs 120
percentage discount = {tex}{120\over 800}\times100{/tex} 
= 15%

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{7}{3} \%{/tex} of a number is 42, then the number is
questions.explanation:
We have {tex}\frac{7}{3} \%{/tex} of a number = 42
then the number = {tex}\frac{42}{\frac{7}{3}}\times100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{42}{{7}}\times3\times100{/tex}
= 1800

questions.question: Shyama purchases a scooter costing Rs 36,450 and the rate of sales tax is 9%, then the total amount paid by her is
questions.explanation:
Since sales tax is charged on the sale of an item by the government and is added to the bill
amount.
Shyama purchase a scooter of costing = Rs. 36450
Sales tax paid = 9 %
So, total amount paid by her
=  Rs. 36450 + 9 % of Rs. 36450
=  36450 + {tex}\frac{9}{100} \times 36450{/tex}
= 36450 + 3280.5
= Rs 39730 .5

questions.question: A TV set was bought for ₹26250 including 5% VAT. The original price of the TV set is
questions.explanation:
The cost price of TV set = ₹26250.
VAT including = 5% 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Original price = {tex} 26250\times\frac{100}{105} {/tex}
= ₹ 25000
Hence, ₹25000 is correct.

questions.question: The original price of a washing machine which was bought for ₹13500 including 8% VAT, is
questions.explanation:
The price of the washing machine = ₹ 13500 VAT [Value Added Tax] is included in the selling price, which is 8%.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The original price of the washing machine including 8% VAT
= {tex}13500 \times \frac{100}{108}{/tex}
= ₹12500
Hence, ₹12500 is correct.

questions.question: A toy is purchased at ₹1500 including 5% SGST and 5% CGST. Find the actual price of the toy without GST.
questions.explanation:
Now, SGST =5% of 1500
{tex}\therefore{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1500 = ₹75
Similarly, CGST = 5% of 1500 = ₹75
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Actual price of toy = 1500 - SGST - CGST
= 1500 - 75 - 75 = 1500 - 150
= ₹1350

questions.question: The difference between a discount of 40% on ₹500 and two successive discounts of 36% and 4% on the same amount is
questions.explanation:
SP at 40% discount = 60% of ₹500 = ₹300
SP after two successive discounts of 36% and 4%
= 96% of (64% of 500)
{tex}=\frac{96}{100} \times \frac{64}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 500 = ₹307.20
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required difference = ₹7.20

questions.question: The marked price of an article is 10% higher than the cost price. A discount of 10% is given on the marked price. In this kind of sale, the seller
questions.explanation:
Let CP of article be ₹100, then marked price be ₹110.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} SP = 90% of ₹110
{tex}=\frac{90}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 110 = ₹99
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Loss per cent = 100 - 99 = 1%

questions.question: If a commission of 10% is given to the marked price of a book, the publisher gains 20%. If the commission is increased to 15%, the gain is
questions.explanation:
Let CP be ₹100, then SP be ₹120.
Now, SP = ₹120, commission = 10%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Marked price = {tex}\frac{100}{90}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 120 = ₹{tex}\frac{400}{3}{/tex}
Now, marked price = {tex}\frac{400}{3}{/tex}, commission = 15%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} SP = 85% of {tex}\frac{400}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{85}{100} \times \frac{400}{3}=\frac{340}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Gain percent = {tex}\frac{340}{3}{/tex} - 100 = {tex}13 \frac{1}{3} \%{/tex}

subtopics.name: Sales Tax/Value Added Tax/Goods and Services Tax (VIII-7.2)

questions.question: Lakshmi buys an article with 25% discount on the marked price. She makes a profit of 10% by selling it at ₹660. What was the marked price?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} SP = ₹660, Profit % = 10%, discount = 25 %
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} CP = ₹600
{tex}\therefore{/tex} MP = {tex}\frac{600 \times 100}{75}{/tex} = ₹800

questions.question: If a shirt cost ₹64 after a 20% discount, what was its original price?
questions.explanation:
Let the original CP be ₹ x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{80}{100}{/tex} = 64 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = ₹80

questions.question: The amount of a certain sum at compound interest for 2 yr at 5% is ₹4410. The sum is
questions.explanation:
According to given condition,
4410 = P{tex}\left(1+\frac{5}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{4410}{(\frac{21}{20})^2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{4410 \times 400}{441}{/tex} = ₹4000

questions.question: The difference of the compound interest and the simple interest on ₹60000 at 6% annually for 2 yr will be
questions.explanation:
Now, simple interest for two years, SI = {tex}\frac{P R T}{100}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{60000 \times 6 \times 2}{100}{/tex} = ₹7200
and compound interest for two years Cl = A - P
= P{tex}\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^n{/tex} - P = 60000{tex}\left(1+\frac{6}{100}\right)^2{/tex} - 60000
= 60000{tex}\left(\frac{53}{50}\right)^2{/tex} - 60000
= 67416 - 60000 = ₹7416
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required difference = Cl - SI
= 7416 - 7200 = ₹216

questions.question: A sum of money doubles itself at compound interest in 15 yr. In how many years will it become eight times?
questions.explanation:
P{tex}\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{15}{/tex} = 2P
or {tex}\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{15}{/tex} = 2
Now, P{tex}\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{n}{/tex} = 8P
{tex}\Rightarrow \left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^n{/tex} = 8 = (2)3 = {tex}\left\{\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{15}\right\}^3{/tex} [from Eq. (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow \left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^n=\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{45}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 45

questions.question: Any amount taken as a loan on 5% per year compounded interest had been given back in two equal annual installments of ₹882. The amount taken as a loan was-
questions.explanation:
Rent amount = (The present value of ₹ 882 after one year) + (Present value of ₹882 after 2 years)
= {tex}\frac{882}{\left(1+\frac{5}{100}\right)}+\frac{882}{\left(1+\frac{5}{100}\right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}882 \times \frac{20}{21}+882 \times \frac{20}{21} \times \frac{20}{21}{/tex}
= 840 + 800
= ₹1640

questions.question: An amount becomes ₹ 16900 in 2 years and ₹ 17576 in 3 years on compounded interest. Find out the annual rate of interest?
questions.explanation:
1 year interest of ₹ 16900
= 17576 - 16900
= ₹ 676
Rate of interest = {tex}\frac{676}{16900}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100
= 4%

questions.question: The difference of compounded and simple interest on any amount at 4% annual rate of interest for 2 years ₹ is 4. Find the principal?
questions.explanation:
Difference between C.I. and S.I. for 2 years.
d = {tex}P\left(\frac{r}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
4 = {tex}P\left(\frac{4}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
4 = {tex}P \times\left(\frac{1}{25}\right)^2{/tex}
P = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 625
= ₹2500

questions.question: A toy is purchased at ₹1320 including 5% SGST and 5% CGST. The actual price of the toy without GST is
questions.explanation:
Cost price of toy = ₹1320
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text { Actial Price } \times(100+\mathrm{GST} \%)}{100}{/tex} = Cost Price
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text { Actual Price } \times(100+5+5)}{100}{/tex} = 1320
{tex}\because{/tex} Actual Price {tex}\times \frac{110}{100}{/tex} = 1320
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Actual Price (without GST) = {tex}\frac{1320 \times 100}{110}{/tex} = ₹1200

questions.question: The difference of the compound interest and the simple interest on ₹50000 at 4% annually for 2 yr will be
questions.explanation:
Given, principal = ₹50000, Rate = 4%, Time = 2 yrs
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required difference
= Compound interest - Simple interest
= Principal {tex}\left(1+\frac{\text { Rate }}{100}\right)^{\text {Time }}{/tex}- Principal - {tex}\frac{\text { Principal } \times \text { Rate } \times \text { Time }}{100}{/tex}
= 50000 {tex}\left[\left(1+\frac{4}{100}\right)\left[-1-\frac{4 \times 2}{100}\right]\right.{/tex}
= 50000{tex}\left[\left(\frac{26}{25}\right)^2-1-\frac{2}{25}\right]{/tex}
= 50000{tex}\left[\frac{676}{625}-1-\frac{2}{25}\right]{/tex}
= 50000 {tex}\left[\frac{676-625-50}{625}\right]=\frac{50000}{625}{/tex} = ₹80

questions.question: The difference of a amount of two years compounded interest and simple interest is ₹160. If simple interest for 2 years is ₹2880, then what is rate of interest?
questions.explanation:
S.I. for 2 years = ₹2880
S.I. for 1 year = ₹1440
Difference between C.I. & S.I. for
2 years = 160
Rate = {tex}\frac{160}{1440} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{100}{9}{/tex}
= {tex}11 \frac{1}{9}{/tex}%

questions.question: Samir purchased a pair of shoes and paid ₹441, including GST. If the sale price of shoes is ₹420, then the rate of GST is
questions.explanation:
Let the rate of GST be x %.
Price including GST = SP + SP {tex}\times \frac{\text { Rate of } G S T}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 441 = 420 + {tex}420 \times \frac{x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 441 - 420 = {tex}420 \times \frac{x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 21 = {tex}420 \times \frac{x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{21 \times 100}{420}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 5%

questions.question: A trader marks his goods 25% above the cost price and gives a discount of 20% on the marked price. His gain or loss per cent is
questions.explanation:
Let CP of goods be ₹x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} MP = {tex}x \times \frac{125}{100}=\frac{5 x}{4}{/tex}
SP = {tex}\mathrm{MP} \times \frac{100-\text { discount } \%}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5 x}{4} \times \frac{80}{100}{/tex}= x
SP = CP
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Neither profit nor loss i.e., profit or loss = 0%

questions.question: The sum which will amount to ₹8748 in 2 yr at 8% per annum compound interest is
questions.explanation:
Let sum be ₹x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A = {tex}P\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^n{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8748 = {tex}x\left(1+\frac{8}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8748 = {tex}x \times \frac{27}{25} \times \frac{27}{25}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{8748 \times 25 \times 25}{27 \times 27}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = ₹7500

questions.question: What will be the increase in an amount in 2 years by 10% annual compounded interest?
questions.explanation:
Let Principal be ₹P
A = {tex}P\left(1+\frac{1}{10}\right)^2{/tex}
= {tex}P\left(\frac{11}{10}\right)^2{/tex}
A = {tex}P \times \frac{121}{100}{/tex}
Percentage increase = {tex}\frac{P \times \frac{121}{100}-P}{P} \times 100{/tex}
= 21%

questions.question: If an amount is taken as a loan at 5% annually compounded rate of interest for 2 years. Then the ratio of mixed amount is-
questions.explanation:
A = {tex}P\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
= {tex}P\left(1+\frac{5}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
= {tex}P\left(\frac{21}{20}\right)^2{/tex}
= {tex}P \times \frac{441}{400}{/tex}
A : P = 441 : 400

questions.question: Manu buys a pressure cooker at ({tex}\frac{9}{10}{/tex})th of its marked selling price and sells it at 8% more than its marked selling price. What is her gain per cent?
questions.explanation:
Let the marked price of pressure cooker = ₹x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost price (CP) = ₹ {tex}x \times \frac{9}{10}{/tex}
and selling price (SP) = ₹ {tex}x \times \frac{108}{100}{/tex}
Hence the profit per cent = {tex}\frac{S P-C P}{C P} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac{108}{100}x - \frac{9}{10}x}{\frac{9}{10}x} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{108-90}{100} \times \frac{10}{9} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}18 \times \frac{10}{9}{/tex}
= 20%

questions.question: Samir purchased a pair of shoes for ₹1344 including GST. If the sale price of the shoes is ₹1200, then the rate of GST is
questions.explanation:
Let the rate of GST be x%,
According to the question,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1200 + {tex}1200 \times \frac{x}{100}{/tex} = 1344
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = 44
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{144}{12}{/tex} = 12%

questions.question: The population of a city first increased by 10% and then decreased by 20%. The net decrease in the population is
questions.explanation:
Let the population of a city be x.
According to the question, new population = {tex}x \times \frac{110}{100} \times \frac{80}{100}=\frac{22}{25} x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The net decrease population = {tex}\frac{x-\frac{22}{25} x}{x} \times 100=\frac{3}{25} \times 100{/tex} = 12%

questions.question: An amount becomes its 8 times in 3 years at compounded interest, what will be the rate of interest?
questions.explanation:
let principle be ₹P
After 3 years, amount = ₹ 8P
8P = {tex}P\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^3{/tex}
23 = {tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^3{/tex}
1 + {tex}\frac{r}{100}{/tex} = 2
{tex}\frac{r}{100}{/tex} = 1
r = 100%

questions.question: If Nidhi purchases a book of ₹500 and GST is 12 per cent. She will pay ₹________
questions.explanation:
Cost of book = ₹500
GST = 12%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} GST for book = 12% of ₹500
= {tex}500 \times \frac{12}{100}{/tex} = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = ₹60
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Pay by Nidhi = Cost of book + GST for book
= 500 + 60 = ₹560

questions.question: The compound interest on ₹10 at the rate of 10% per annum for two years will be ________
questions.explanation:
Principal = ₹10,
Interest rate = 10% per annum
Time = 2 yr
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound amount = Principal (1 + {tex}\frac{\text { rate }}{100}{/tex})Time
= {tex}10\left(1+\frac{10}{100}\right)^2{/tex}
= {tex}10 \times \frac{11}{10} \times \frac{11}{10}=\frac{121}{10}{/tex} = 12.10
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound interest = Compound Amount - Principal
= 12.10 - 10
= ₹2.10

questions.question: How much compounded interest will be given ₹3000 after 2 years at the rate of 8% per annum?
questions.explanation:
C.I. = {tex}3000\left[\left(1+\frac{8}{100}\right)^2-1\right]{/tex}
= 3000{tex}\left[\left(\frac{27}{25}\right)^2-1\right]{/tex}
= 3000{tex}\left[\frac{729-625}{625}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}3000 \times \frac{104}{625}{/tex}
= ₹499.20

questions.question: The difference between the compound interest and simple interest on a certain sum of money at 10% per annum for 2 years is ₹ 500. Find the sum when the interest is compounded annually.
questions.explanation:
Let P be the principal
According to question, C.l. - S.l. = ₹ 500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[P\left(1+\frac{10}{100}\right)^2-P\right]-\left[\frac{P \times 10 \times 2}{100}\right]{/tex} = 500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{21}{100} P-\frac{1}{5} P{/tex} = 500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{P}{100}{/tex} = 500 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = 500 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = ₹ 50000

questions.question: The difference between compound interest and simple interest on a certain sum at 15% annum for 2 years is Rs. 450. What is the value of the sum?
questions.explanation:
Given:

Difference between compound interest and simple interest = Rs.450

Rate (R) = 15%

Time = 2 years

Formula used:

S.I. = (P × R × T)/100 

C.I. = P(1 + R/100)T – P

The difference between S.I. and C.I. for 2 years = PR2/(100)2

Where

P = Principal

R = Rate

T = Time

Calculations:

Let the principal be Rs. P.

S.I. = (P × R × T)/100 

 (P × 2 × 15)/100 

 3P/10 

C.I. = P(1 + R/100)T – P

 P × (1 + 15/100)2 – P 

 529P/400 – P

 129P/400 

The difference between S.I. and C.I. for 2 years = (129P/400) – 3P/10

 9P/400 = 450 

P = 20000

∴ The value of the sum is Rs. 20,000.

questions.question: Compound interest Compounded annually on a certain sum of money for 3 years at 8% per annum is ₹ 1688.128. Find the simple interest on the same sum for the same rate and same period.
(0.5% of 250) + (0.8% of 120) = ?
questions.explanation:
(0.5% of 250) + (0.8% of 120)
= {tex}\left(\frac{0.5 \times 250}{100}\right)+\left(\frac{0.8 \times 120}{100}\right){/tex}
= 1.25 + 0.96 = 2.21

questions.question: If 30% of 140 = x% of 840, then the value of x is ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, 30% of 140 = x% of 840
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{30}{100} \times 140{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{100} \times 840{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 42 = {tex}\frac{42}{5} x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 5

questions.question: If x% of y is 100 and y% of z is 200, then
questions.explanation:
It is given that x% of y = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x y}{100}{/tex} = 100 {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} xy = 10000 {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} y = {tex}\frac{10000}{x}{/tex}
Also, y% of z = 200
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{y z}{100}{/tex} = 200 {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} yz = 20000
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{10000}{x}\right) z{/tex} = 20000
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{z}{x}{/tex} = 2 or z = 2x

questions.question: If 12.5% of 192 = 50% of x, then x =
questions.explanation:
We have, 12.5% of 192 = 50% of x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{12.5}{100} \times 192=\frac{50}{100} \times x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 48

questions.question: A T.V. worth ₹ 7200 is offered for sale at ₹ 4800. What percent discount is offered during the sale?
questions.explanation:
We have, M.R = ₹ 7200 and S.P. = ₹ 4800
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount = M.P. - S.P. = ₹ 7200 - ₹ 4800 = ₹ 2400
Thus, Discount % = {tex}\left(\frac{\text { Discount }}{\text { M.P. }} \times 100\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{2400}{7200} \times 100\right){/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{100}{3}\right) \%{/tex}

questions.question: The population of a town was decreasing every year due to migration, poverty and unemployment. The present population of the town is 6,31,680. Last year the migration was 4% and the year before last, it was 6%. What was the population two years ago?
questions.explanation:
Let the population two years ago be x
After 1 year, it remained = {tex}x-\frac{6}{100} x{/tex}
After 2 years, it remained = {tex}\left(x-\frac{6 x}{100}\right)-\left(x-\frac{6 x}{100}\right) \times \frac{4}{100}{/tex}
According to question, we have
631680 = {tex}x-\frac{6 x}{100}-\frac{4 x}{100}+\frac{24 x}{10000}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 631680 = {tex}\frac{10000 x-600 x-400 x+24 x}{10000}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 631680 = {tex}\frac{9024 x}{10000} \Rightarrow \frac{631680 \times 10000}{9024}{/tex} = x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 700000
Thus, the population two years ago was 700000

questions.question: Mohan buys a bike at 20% discount of its value and sells it for 20% more than its value. What will be his profit percentage?
questions.explanation:
Let marked price (M.P.) of bike = ₹ 100
So, cost price (C.P.) for Mohan = 80% of ₹ 100 = ₹ 80
As, selling price of bike is 20% more than its marked price.
So, S.P for Mohan = 120% of ₹ 100 = {tex}₹\left(\frac{120 \times 100}{100}\right){/tex} = ₹ 120
Thus, profit percentage = {tex}\frac{\text { S.P. }- \text { C.P. }}{\text { C.P. }} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{120-80}{80} \times 100=\frac{40}{80} \times 100{/tex} = 50%

questions.question: In an office the working hours are 10.30 AM to 8.30 PM and in between 30 minutes are spent on lunch. Find the ratio of office hours to the time spent for lunch.
questions.explanation:
Office hours = 10.30 AM to 5.30 PM
= 7 hrs = 420 min.
Lunch time = 30 min.
420:30
14:1

questions.question: Ratio of 250 ml to 2 L is
The ratio of the least prime number to the least composite number is
questions.explanation:
Least prime : Least composite number
= 2 : 4 or 1 : 2

questions.question: The third proportional between (a2 - b2) and (a + b)2 is
questions.explanation:
Let x be the required third proportional
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{a^2-b^2}{(a+b)^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(a+b)^2}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}x=\frac{(a+b)^4}{a^2-b^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(a+b)^3(a+b)}{(a+b)(a-b)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(a+b)^3}{(a-b)}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{5 x-3 y}{5 y-3 x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}, then value of {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{5 x-3 y}{5 y-3 x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{5-3\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{5\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}20-12\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} = {tex}15\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-9{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}27\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} = {tex}29 {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {29}{27}{/tex}

questions.question: Some on rupee, 50 paisa and 25 paise coins make up ₹ 93.75 and their number are in proportion 3 : 4 : 5. The number of each type of coins, are
questions.explanation:
Let no. of one-rupee, 50 paise and 25 paise coins be
3x, 4x and 5x respectively
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 4x {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5 + 5x {tex}\times{/tex} 0.25 = 93.75
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 2x + 1.25x = 93.75
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6.25x = 93.75
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 15
{tex}\therefore{/tex} No. of coins are 45, 60, 75

questions.question: The price of an article diminished twice successively, first by 30% and then by 40%. If the original price was ₹ 1000, what is it now?
questions.explanation:
1000 {tex}\times \frac{(100-30)}{100} \times \frac{(100-40)}{100}{/tex}
= 1000 {tex}\times\frac{70}{100} \times \frac{60}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 420.

questions.question: Two shopkeepers A and B sells machines at the same list price. The first (A) allows two successive discounts of 30% and 16% and the second (B) 20% and 26%. Which discount series is more advantageous to the customer?
questions.explanation:
If two successive discounts on an article are x% and y% respectively, then a single discount equivalent to the two successive
discounts will be {tex}\left(m+n-\frac{m n}{100}\right) \%{/tex}
A single discount equivalent to the two successive discounts of 30% and 16% is
= {tex}\left(30+16-\frac{30 \times 16}{100}\right) \%{/tex}
= {tex}\left(46-\frac{24}{5}\right) \%{/tex} = {tex}41 \frac{1}{5} \%{/tex}
A single discount equivalent to the two successive discounts of 20% and 26% is
= {tex}\left(20+26-\frac{20 \times 26}{100}\right) \%{/tex}
= {tex}\left(46-\frac{26}{5}\right) \%{/tex} = {tex}40 \frac{4}{5} \%{/tex}
Clearly discount series offered by the first shopkeeper is more advantage our to the purchaser.

questions.question: Population of a town increases at a certain rate per cent per annum. Present population of the town is 3600 and in 5 years’ time it becomes 4800. How much will it be in 10 years time?
questions.explanation:
Let rate of increase in population = r% p
Then 4800 = 3600 {tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^5{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^5{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4800}{3600}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}
Population in the next 5 years will become
4800 {tex}\times \frac{4}{3}{/tex} = 6400.

questions.question: Compound interest on ₹ 10000 for 3 years if rate of interest is 5%, 10% and 20% for first, second and third years respectively, is
questions.explanation:
Amount after 3 years
= Principal {tex}\times \frac{100+R_1}{100} \times \frac{100+R_2}{100} \times \frac{100+R_3}{100}{/tex}
= ₹ 10000 {tex}\times \frac{21}{20} \times \frac{11}{10} \times \frac{6}{5}{/tex} = ₹ 13860
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound interest = ₹ 13860 - ₹ 10000
= ₹ 3860

questions.question: ₹ 8000 invested at compound interest gives ₹ 1261 as interest after 3 years. The rate of interest per annum is:
questions.explanation:
P = ₹ 8000, C.I. = ₹ 1261
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Amount = ₹ 9261, n = 3, r = ?
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 9261 = {tex}8000\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^3{/tex} = {tex}\frac{9261}{8000}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{21}{20}\right)^3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} 1+\frac{r}{100}=\frac{21}{20}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{r}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{21}{20}-1{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{20}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}r=\frac{100}{20} \%{/tex} = 5% P.a.

questions.question: The compound interest on ₹ 30000 at 7% per annum is ₹ 4347. The period (in years) is:
questions.explanation:
P = ₹ 30000, r = 7% P.a., C.I = ₹ 4347,
n = ?
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Amount = ₹ 30000 + ₹ 4347 = ₹ 34347
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 34347 = {tex}30000\left(1+\frac{7}{100}\right)^n{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{107}{100}\right)^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{34347}{30000}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{11449}{10000}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{107}{100}\right)^n{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{107}{100}\right)^2{/tex}{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = 2

subtopics.name: Deducing a Formula for Compound Interest (VIII-7.3)

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are in the ratio {tex}\frac{1}{2}: \frac{1}{3}: \frac{1}{4}{/tex} and its perimeter is 104 cm. The length of the longest side is
questions.explanation:
Sides are in the ratio {tex}\frac{1}{2}: \frac{1}{3}: \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
i.e. 6 : 4: 3
Let the ratio be x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} sides are 6x, 4x and 3x
Given that 6x + 4x + 3x = 104
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 13x = 104
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 8
{tex}\therefore{/tex} longest side = 6x = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 48 cm

questions.question: The population of a city is 155625, for every 1000 men, there are 1075 women. If 40% of men and 24% of women be literate, then what is the percentage of literate people in the city?
questions.explanation:
Ratio of men and women = 1000 : 1075 = 40 : 43
No. of men in total population
= {tex}\frac{40}{83} \times{/tex}155625 = 75000
No. of women in total population
= 155625 - 75000 = 80625
No. of literate men = 40% of 75000
= {tex}\frac{40 \times 75000}{100}{/tex}
= 30000
No. of literate women = 24% of 80625
= {tex}\frac{24 \times 80625}{100}{/tex} = 19350
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Total no. of literate people = 30000 + 19350
= 49350
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Required % = {tex}\frac{49350}{155625}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100%
= {tex}\frac{2632}{83} \%{/tex} = {tex}31 \frac{59}{83} \%{/tex}

questions.question: A shopkeeper’s price is 50% above the cost price. If he allows his customer a discount of 30% what profit does he make?
questions.explanation:
Let CP =100
MP = 150
discount = 30%
We have
SP = {tex}\frac{C P(100+g \%)}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{M P(100-d \%)}{100}{/tex}
So {tex}\frac{100 \times(100+\mathrm{g} \%)}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{150 \times(100-30 \%)}{100}{/tex}
100 + g% = {tex}\frac{150 \times 70}{100}{/tex} = 105
g% = 105 - 100 = 5%

questions.question: A sum of money doubles itself in 3 years at Cl, when the interest is compounded annually. In how many years will it amount to 16 times of itself?
questions.explanation:
2P = {tex}P\left(1+\frac{\mathrm{R}}{100}\right)^3{/tex}
{tex}1+\frac{R}{100}=2^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} ...(i)
Suppose the money becomes 16 times itself in n years.
16P = {tex}P\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^n{/tex}
{tex}1+\frac{R}{100}{/tex} = {tex}2^{\frac{4}{n}}{/tex} ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii)
{tex}2^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}= {tex}2^{\frac{4}{n}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{n}{/tex}
n = 12 years.

questions.question: Vishakha offers a discount of 20% on all the items at her shop and still makes a profit of 12%. What is the cost price of an article marked at ₹ 280?
questions.explanation:
Marked price = ₹ 280
Disount = 20% of ₹ 280
= {tex}\frac{20}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 280 = ₹ 56
So selling price = ₹ (280 - 56) = ₹ 224
Let the cost price be ₹ 100
Profit = 12% of ₹ 100
= ₹ 12
So selling price = ₹(100 + 12) = ₹ 112
If the selling price is ₹112, cost price = ₹ 100
If the selling price is ₹ 224, cost price
= {tex}₹\left(\frac{100}{112} \times 224\right){/tex} = ₹ 200

questions.question: If the discount is 10%, an item bought for ₹ 9 is priced at
questions.explanation:
Let MP be ₹ x. Then,
SP = {tex}x-\frac{10 x}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{90 x}{100}{/tex}
But SP = 9
So, {tex}\frac{90 \mathrm{x}}{100}{/tex} = 9
x = 10

questions.question: A sum of money placed at compound interest doubles itself in 4 years. In how many years will it amount to eight times itself?
questions.explanation:
Reasoning: If x be the sum it becomes 2x in four years at a certain rate, say r%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2x = {tex}x\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^4{/tex} or
2 = {tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^4{/tex}
Cube both sides
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 23 = {tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^{3 \times 4}{/tex}
or 8 = {tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^{12}{/tex}
or 8x = x{tex}\left(1+\frac{r}{100}\right)^{12}{/tex}
Hence, the required number of years = 12 years

questions.question: At compound interest, if a certain sum of money doubles in n years then the amount will be four fold in
questions.explanation:
Let principal = ?x and rate = R. According to question,
{tex}x\left(1+\frac{\mathrm{R}}{100}\right)^n{/tex} = 2x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \left(1+\frac{\mathrm{R}}{100}\right)^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 2
Let it become 4 folds in N years.
So, {tex}x\left(1+\frac{\mathrm{R}}{100}\right)^{\mathrm{N}}{/tex} = 4x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} N = 2n years

questions.question: A bag contains Rs 600 in the form of 1- rupee, 50 paise and 25 paise coins in the ratio 3 : 4 : 12, the number of 25 paise coins is:
questions.explanation:
Let x = 1 rupee coin, y = 50 paise coin, z
= 25 paise coin
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ₹ {tex}\left(x+\frac{y}{2}+\frac{z}{4}\right){/tex} = ₹ 600
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x + 2y + z = 2400 ...(i)
Also, let x = 3k, y = 4k, z = 12k
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From (i)
4(3k) + 2(4k) +12 k = 2400
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12k + 8k + 12k = 2400{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 32k
= 2400
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = {tex}\frac{2400}{32}{/tex} = 75
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of 25-paise coin = 12(k)
= 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 75 = 900

questions.question: 729 ml of a mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 7 : 2. How much more water is to be added to get a new mixture containing milk and water in the ratio 7 : 3?
questions.explanation:
Ratio of water and milk are 2 : 7
Total mixture is 729 ml
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Quantity of milk
= {tex}\left[729 \times \frac{7}{9}\right]{/tex} = 567ml
Quantity of water = [729 - 567] = 162 m
Now, {tex}\frac{567}{162+x}=\frac{7}{3}{/tex}
567 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 7[162 + x]
1701 = 1134 + 7x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 81

questions.question: A cycle shop allows a discount of 25% on the marked price and earns a profit of 20% on the cost price. Its marked price on which shop earns ₹ 40 is
questions.explanation:
Let marked price = ₹ x
Selling price (S.P) = {tex}x-\frac{25}{100} x{/tex}
SP = ₹ {tex} \frac{3}{4} x{/tex}
Let cost price (CP) = ₹ y
Profit = 20%
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{20}{100} y{/tex} = 40
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 200
selling price (SP) = 200 + 40 = ₹ 240
{tex}\frac{3}{4} x{/tex} = 240 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} x=\frac{240 \times 4}{3}{/tex} = 320

questions.question: Each issue of a weekly cost ₹ 10. However on subscription of 156 issues 30% of the total amount and on 52 issues 25% of the total amount gets saved. What difference exists between the per-issue cost obtained through both subscription?
questions.explanation:
Cost of 1 issue of weekly = ₹ 10
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Actual cost of 156 issues = 156 {tex}\times{/tex} 10
= ₹ 1560.
Cost of subscription of 156 issues
= {tex}\frac{156 \times 70}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 1092
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of 1 issue {tex}= \frac{1092}{156}{/tex} = ₹ 7
Actual cost of 52 issues = 52 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = ₹ 520
Cost of subscription of 52 issues
= {tex}\frac{520 \times 75}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 390
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of 1 issue = {tex}\frac{390}{52}{/tex} = ₹ 7.5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} If difference per issue on subscription
= ₹ 7.5 - ₹ 7
= ₹ 0.5 = 50 paise

questions.question: A fan is listed at ₹ 1500 and a discount of 20% is offered on the list price. What additional discount must be offered to the customer to bring the net price to ₹ 1104.
questions.explanation:
M.P. = ₹ 1500,
Discount = 20%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S.P. = 80% of ₹1500 = {tex}\frac{80}{100} \times 1500{/tex}
= ₹ 1200
Final S.P = ₹ 1104
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Additional discount = ₹ 1200 - ₹ 1104
= ₹ 96
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Additional discount rate = {tex}\frac{96}{1200} \times 100{/tex}
= 8%

questions.question: The cost of a vehicle is ₹ 1,75, 000. If its value depreciates at the rate of 20% per annum, then the total depreciation after 3 years was
questions.explanation:
Value of the vehicle after 3 years
= 1,75,000 {tex}\times\left(1-\frac{20}{100}\right)^3{/tex}
= l,75,000 {tex}\times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{4}{5}{/tex} = ₹ 89, 600
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total depreciation = 1, 75,000 - 89,600
= ₹ 85,400

questions.question: What will be the difference between simple and compound interests at the rate of 10% per annum on a sum of ₹ 1000 after 4 years?
questions.explanation:
P = ₹ 1000 R = 10%T = 4 yrs
S.L = {tex}\frac{1,000 \times 10 \times 4}{100}{/tex} = 400
{tex}A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^n{/tex} = {tex}1000\left(1+\frac{10}{100}\right)^4{/tex}
= {tex}1000\left(\frac{11}{10}\right)^4{/tex} = {tex}1000 \times \frac{11 \times 11 \times 11 \times 11}{10 \times 10 \times 10 \times 10}{/tex} = 1464.1
C.I. = A - P = 1464.1- 1000 = 464.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C.I - S .I. = 64.1

questions.question: The population of a village increases @ 5% p.a.. If present population is 8000, after how many years the population will be 9261?
questions.explanation:
Let population become 9261 in ‘x’ years.
{tex}\frac{\text { Amount }}{\text { Principal }}=\left(1+\frac{\text { Rate }}{100}\right)^{\text {Time }}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{9261}{8000}=\left(\frac{21}{20}\right)^x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left(\frac{21}{20}\right)^3=\left(\frac{21}{20}\right)^x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Time = 3 years

questions.question: In a mixture of 120 litres, the ratio of milk and water is 2 : 1. If the ratio of milk and water is 1 : 2, then the amount of water (in litres) is required to be added is:
questions.explanation:
Quantity of milk = {tex}120 \times \frac{2}{3}=80{/tex} litres
Quantity of water = 120 - 80 = 40 litres
New ratio required =1 : 2
Let quantity of water to be added further be x litres
Then milk : water : {tex}=\frac{80}{40+x}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
= 160 = 40 + x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 120
Quantity of water further to be added = 120 litres

questions.question: A sum of money is put at compound interest for 2 years at 20% p.a. It would fetch ₹ 482 more, if the interest were payable half-yearly, than if it were payable yearly. The sum is
questions.explanation:
{tex}P\left[\left(1+\frac{R}{100 \times 2}\right)^{2 \times 2}-\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^2\right]=482{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{P}\left[\left(1+\frac{20}{100 \times 2}\right)^4-\left(1+\frac{20}{100}\right)^2\right]=482{/tex}
=> P x 0.0241 = 482 => P =  ₹20000.

questions.question: Find the compound interest on ₹ 2,000 for 2 years at 10% per annum compounded annually.
questions.explanation:
C.I. = 2000{tex}\left(1+\frac{10}{100}\right)^2{/tex} - 2000 = 2000 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{11}{10} \times \frac{11}{10}{/tex} - 2000
= ₹ (2420 - 2000) = ₹ 420

subtopics.name: Addition and Subtraction of Algebraic Expressions

questions.question: Add: 7xy + 5yz - 3zx, 4yz + 9zx - 4y and -3xz + 5x - 2xy.
questions.explanation:
(7xy + 5yz - 3zx) + (4yz + 9zx - 4y) + (-3xz + 5x - 2xy)
= 7xy + 5yz - 3zx + 4yz + 9zx - 4y - 3xz + 5x - 2xy
= 7xy - 2xy + 5yz + 4yz - 3zx + 9zx - 3xz + 5x - 4y
= 5xy + 9yz + 3zx + 5x - 4y

questions.question: Subtract 7x - 3x2 from 4x + 8x2.
When numbers/literals are added or subtracted, they are called ________.
questions.explanation: Various parts of an algebraic expression which are separated by the signs of + or - are called the terms of the expression.

questions.question: Find the product of (7x - 4y) and (3x - 7y)
questions.explanation:
(7x - 4y) (3x - 7y)
= 7x (3x - 7y) - 4y (3x - 7y)
= 7x {tex}\times{/tex} 3x - 7x {tex}\times{/tex} 7y - 4y {tex}\times{/tex} 3x + 4y {tex}\times{/tex} 7y
= 21x2 - 49xy  -12xy + 28y2
= 21x2 - 61xy + 28y2

questions.question: Subtract 5x2 - 4y2 + 6y - 3 from 7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y.
questions.explanation:
(7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y) - (5x2 - 4y2 + 6y – 3)
opening brackets we get,
7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y - 5x2 + 4y2 - 6y + 3
solving like terms we get,
7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y - 5x2 + 4y2 - 6y + 3
7x2 - 5x2 + 8y + 4y2 + 5x - 3y - 6y - 4xy + 3
2x2 + 12y2 + 5x - 9y - 4xy + 3
2x2 - 4xy + 12y2 + 5x - 9y + 3

questions.question: Add: ab - bc, bc - ca, ca - ab
questions.explanation:
(ab - bc) + (bc - ca) + (ca - ab)
opening brackets we get,
ab - bc + bc - ca + ca - ab
solving like terms we get,
ab - ab - bc + bc - ca + ca
0 + 0 + 0 = 0

questions.question: Add: a - b + ab, b - c + bc, c - a + ac
questions.explanation:
(a - b + ab) + (b - c + bc) + (c - a + ac)
opening brackets we get,
a - b + ab + b - c + bc + c - a + ac
solving like terms and unlike terms we get,
a - a - b + b - c + c + ab + bc + ac
0 + 0 + 0 + ab + bc + ac
ab + bc + ac

questions.question: 5x {tex}\times{/tex} 4x2 =?
questions.explanation:
5x {tex}\times{/tex} 4x2
Multiply constants and variables we get,
5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x2
20 {tex}\times{/tex} x2
20x3

questions.question: 5x {tex}\times{/tex} (-4xyz) = ?
questions.explanation:
5x {tex}\times{/tex} (-4xyz)
Multiply the constants and variables
5 {tex}\times{/tex} (-4) {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
-20 {tex}\times{/tex} x2 {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
-20x2yz

questions.question: 2x {tex}\times{/tex} 5y {tex}\times{/tex} 7z =?
questions.explanation:
2x {tex}\times{/tex} 5y {tex}\times{/tex} 7z
Multiply the constants and  variables we get,
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
70 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex}z
70xyz

questions.question: 4xy {tex}\times{/tex} 5x2y2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6x3y3 =?
questions.explanation:
4xy {tex}\times{/tex} 5x2y2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6x3y3
Multiply the constants and variables we get,
4 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x2 {tex}\times{/tex} x3 {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} y2 {tex}\times{/tex} y3
120 {tex}\times{/tex} x6 {tex}\times{/tex} y6
120x6y6

questions.question: 4x {tex}\times{/tex} 5y {tex}\times{/tex} 7z =?
questions.explanation:
4x {tex}\times{/tex} 5y {tex}\times{/tex} 7z
Multiply the constants and variables we get,
4 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
140 xyz

questions.question: Obtain the product of xy, yz and zx
questions.explanation:
product of xy, yz and zx = (xy) (yz) (zx)
xy {tex}\times{/tex} yz {tex}\times{/tex} zx
multiply the variables we get,
x {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z {tex}\times{/tex} z
x2y2z2

questions.question: Obtain the product of a, 2b, 3c, 6abc.
questions.explanation:
product of a, 2b, 3c, 6abc = (a) (2b) (3c) (6abc)
{tex}a \times 2b \times 3c \times 6abc{/tex}
Multiply constant terms and variable we get,
{tex}2 \times 3 \times 6 \times a \times a \times b \times b \times c \times c{/tex}
36a2b2c2

questions.question: (-3x) {tex}\times{/tex} (-5y + 2) =?
questions.explanation:
(-3x) {tex}\times{/tex} (-5y + 2)
(-3x)(-5y + 2)
Open the brackets we  get,
[(-3x) (-5y) - 6x]
15xy - 6x

questions.question: 5xy {tex}\times{/tex} (y2 + 3) =?
questions.explanation:
5xy {tex}\times{/tex} (y2 + 3)
5xy(y2 + 3)
Open the brackets we get,
5xy3 + 15xy

questions.question: (5y + 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 3x = ?

questions.question: The product of a monomial and a binomial is a
questions.explanation: Monomial consists of only single term and binomial contains two terms. So, the multiplication of a binomial by a monomial will always produce a binomial.

questions.explanation:
Given, monomials are -7pq and 2pq.
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Their sum = - 7pq + 2pq = (-7 + 2) pq [both monomials consist of like terms, so adding their numerical coefficient]
= -5 pq

questions.question: If we subtract -3x2y2 from x2y2, then we get
questions.explanation:
Given, monomials are -3x2 y2 and x2y2. Now, we have to subtract the first one from the second one.
i.e. x2y2 - (-3x2y2)
= x2y2 + 3x2y2
= (1 + 3) x2 y2
= 4x2y2

questions.question: Sum of a - b + ab, b + c - bc and c - a - ac is
questions.explanation:
Required sum = (a - b + ab) + {(b + c - bc) + (c - a - ac)}
= a - b + ab + b + c - b c + c - a - a c
= 2c + ab - ac - bc [adding the like terms and retaining others]

= 196 p2q4 [multiplying the literal factors having same variables]

This is the product of two monoials.
Area of rectangle = (4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6) ab {tex}\times{/tex} b2 = 24ab3

questions.explanation:

questions.question: Factorised form of r2 - 10r + 21 is
questions.explanation:
We have, r2 - 10r + 21
= r2 -7r - 3r + 21 = r (r - 7) - 3(r - 7) [by splitting the middle term, so that the product of their numerical coefficients is equal constant term ]
= (r - 7)(r - 3) [{tex}\because{/tex} x2 + (a + b)x + ab = (x + a)(x + b)]

questions.question: On dividing 57 p2qr by 114pq, we get
questions.explanation: Required value = {tex}\frac{57 p^{2} q r}{114 p q}=\frac{57 \times p \times p \times q \times r}{114 \times p \times q}=\frac{57}{114} p r=\frac{1}{2} p r{/tex}

questions.explanation:
We have,

questions.question: The value of ( - 27x2y ) {tex}\div{/tex} (- 9xy) is
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\left(-27 x^{2} y\right) \div(-9 x y){/tex} = {tex}\frac{-27 x^{2} y}{-9 x y}=\frac{27 \times x \times x \times y}{9 \times x \times y}=\frac{27}{9} x{/tex} = 3x

questions.question: The value of (2x2 + 4) {tex}\div{/tex} 2 is
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\left(2 x^{2}+4\right) \div 2=\frac{2 x^{2}+4}{2}=\frac{2\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{2}{/tex} [taking 2 as common]
= x2 +2

questions.question: The value of (3x3 + 9x2 + 27x) {tex}\div{/tex} 3x is
questions.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\left(3 x^{3}+9 x^{2}+27 x\right) \div 3 x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 x^{3}+9 x^{2}+27 x}{3 x}=\frac{3 x^{3}}{3 x}+\frac{9 x^{2}}{3 x}+\frac{27 x}{3 x}{/tex} = x2 + 3x + 9

Grouping like terms, we get

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{3}{2} x^2\left(x^2-1\right)+\frac{1}{4} x^2\left(x^2+x\right)-\frac{3}{4} x\left(x^3-1\right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\frac{3}{2} x^2\left(x^2-1\right)+\frac{1}{4} x^2\left(x^2+x\right){/tex} {tex}-\frac{3}{4} x\left(x^3-1\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3}{2} x^4-\frac{3}{2} x^2+\frac{1}{4} x^4+{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{4} x^3-\frac{3}{4} x^4+\frac{3}{4} x{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3}{2} x^4+\frac{1}{4} x^4-\frac{3}{4} x^4{/tex} {tex}+\frac{1}{4} x^3-\frac{3}{2} x^2+\frac{3}{4} x{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6 x^4+x^4-3 x^4}{4}{/tex} {tex}+\frac{1}{4} x^3-\frac{3}{2} x^2+\frac{3}{4} x{/tex}
= {tex}x^4+\frac{1}{4} x^3-\frac{3}{2} x^2+\frac{3}{4} x{/tex}

Required expression
= (2x4 + 6x - 4) - (x4 - 3x2 + 7x - 5)
= 2x4 + 6x - 4 - x4 + 3x2 - 7x + 5
= x4 + 3x2 - x + 1

questions.question: Multiply: {tex}\left(4 x+\frac{3 y}{5}\right){/tex} and {tex}\left(3 x-\frac{4 y}{5}\right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(4 x+\frac{3 y}{5}\right) \times\left(3 x-\frac{4 y}{5}\right){/tex}
= {tex}4 x\left(3 x-\frac{4 y}{5}\right)+\frac{3 y}{5}\left(3 x-\frac{4 y}{5}\right){/tex}
= {tex}12 x^2-\frac{16 x y}{5}+\frac{9 x y}{5}-\frac{12 y^2}{25}{/tex} = {tex}12 x^2-\frac{7 x y}{5}-\frac{12 y^2}{25}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the value of (4x2 + 3y) (9y2 + 4x) at x = 1, y = -2.
The perimeter of a triangular field is 6p2 - 4p + 9 and two of its sides are p2 - 2p + 1 and 3p2 - 5p + 3. Find the third side of the field.
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of triangle = Sum of all sides
i.e. (6p2 - 4p + 9) = (p2 - 2p + 1) + (3p2 - 5p + 3) + Third side
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Third side = (6p2 - 4p + 9) - (4p2 - 7p + 4)
= 6p2 - 4p + 9 - 4p2 + 7p - 4 = 2p2 + 3p + 5

questions.question: If 1 cap costs ₹ (x2 + 4x - 21), then what is the cost of (x + 5) caps?
questions.explanation:
Cost of 1 cap is ₹ (x2 + 4x - 21)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of (x + 5) caps = (x2 + 4x - 21) (x + 5)
= x(x2 + 4x - 21) + 5(x2 + 4x - 21)
= x3 + 4x2 - 21x + 5x2 + 20x - 105
= ₹ (x3 + 9x2 - x - 105)

questions.question: Mohit wants to buy a rectangular field whose perimeter is (4a2 + 6ab + 2b2) units. If its length is (a + b)2 units, then find its breadth.
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of rectangular field = (4a2 + 6ab + 2b2) units
One side = (a + b)2 units
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2(length + breadth) = Perimeter of field
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2((a + b)2 + breadth) = 4a2 + 6ab + 2b2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(a2 + b2 + 2ab + breadth) = 2(2a2 + 3ab + b2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + b2 + 2ab + breadth = 2a2 + 3ab + b2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Breadth = 2a2 + 3ab + b2 - a2 - b2 - 2ab = (a2 + ab) units

questions.question: A T-shirt costs ₹ (x2 - xy - y2), a sweater costs ₹ (2x2 + 8xy - 2y2) and a saree costs ₹ (x2 - 3xy + 4y2). After buying these items Vishal paid ₹ (2x + y)2 to the cashier. How much amount Vishal receive from the cashier?
questions.explanation:
Cost of T-shirt = ₹ (x2 - xy - y2)
Cost of sweater = ₹ (2x2 + 8xy - 2y2)
Cost of saree = ₹ (x2 - 3xy + 4y2)
Total amount to be paid
= ₹ (x2 - xy - y2 + 2x2 + 8xy - 2y2 + x2 - 3xy + 4y2)
= ₹ (4x2 + 4xy + y2) = ₹ (2x + y)2 ({tex}\because{/tex} (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2)
Amount Vishal paid to the cashier = ₹ (2x + y)2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Amount Vishal receives = ₹ ((2x + y)2 - (2x + y)2) = ₹ 0

questions.question: The formula for the area, A sq cm of the white cross is
questions.explanation:
Area of bigger square = (2x + a)2 cm2 = 4x2 + a2 + 4ax cm2
Side of smaller square (shaded) = x cm
Area of 1 small square = x2 cm2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of white cross = Area of bigger square - Area of 4 small squares
= (4x2 + a2 + 4ax - 4x2) cm2
= 4ax + a2 cm2

questions.question: The height of a triangle is x4 + y4 and its base is 14xy. Find the area of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Given,

b = 14xy
Now,
Area of a triangle = {tex} \frac{1}{2} {/tex} (b {tex}\times{/tex} h)
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex}(x4 + y4) {tex}\times{/tex} 14xy
= 7xy(x4 + y4).

questions.question: Find the product of (x2 + x + 1) and (x2 - x + 1).
questions.explanation:
(x2 + x + 1) {tex}\times{/tex} (x2 - x + 1)
= x2(x2 - x + 1) + x {tex}\times{/tex} (x2 - x + 1) + 1(x2 1- x + 1)
= x4 - x3 + x2 + x3 - x2 + x + x2 - x + 1
= x4 + x2 + 1

questions.question: Subtract 4p2 - 2q + 7r3 - 3 from 3q + 7p2 - 2r3 + 4.
questions.explanation:
3q + 7p2 - 2r3 + 4 - [4p2 - 2q + 7r3 - 3]
= 3q + 7p2 - 2r3 + 4 - 4p2 + 2q - 7r3 + 3
= 3p2 - 9r3 + 5q + 7

questions.question: Simplify: (3a - 4b)(9a2 + 12ab + 16b2) + (4a - 3b)(16a2 + 12ab + 9b2)
questions.explanation:
(3a - 4b) (9a2 + 12ab + 16b2) + (4a -3b)(16a2 + 12ab + 9b2)
First expression
= 3a(9a2 + 12ab + 16b2) - 4b(9a2 + 12ab + 16b2)
= 27a3 + 36a2b + 48ab2 - 36a2b - 48ab2 - 64b3
= 27a3 - 64b3 ...(i)
Second expression
= (4a - 3b)(16a2 + 12ab + 9b2)
= 64a3 + 48a2b + 36ab2 - 48a2b - 36ab2 - 27b3
= 64a3 - 27b3 ...(ii)
Adding (i) & (ii) both sides, we get 0

questions.question: Find the product of 3a2 - 4ax + x2 by 5x2 - 2ax
questions.explanation:
(3a2 - 4ax + x2) {tex}\times{/tex} (5x2 - 2ax)
= (3a2 - 4ax + x2) {tex}\times{/tex} 5x2 - (3a2 - 4ax + x2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2ax
= 23a2x2 - 22ax3 + 5x4 - 6a3x

questions.question: {tex} 1-\frac{p}{2 p-3 p}-\frac{p}{2 p+3 p} {/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex} 1-\frac{p}{2 p-3 q}-\frac{p}{2 p+3 q} {/tex}
1 - p {tex} \left[\frac{2 p+3 q+2 p-3 q}{4 p^2-9 q^2}\right] {/tex}
= 1 - p {tex} \left[\frac{4 p}{4 p^2-9 q^2}\right] {/tex}
= 1 - {tex} \frac{4 p^2}{4 p^2-9 q^2} {/tex}
= {tex} \frac{4 p^2-9 q^2-4 p^2}{4 p^2-9 q^2} {/tex} = {tex}\frac{-9 q^2}{4 p^2-9 q^2} {/tex}

The volume of a cuboid of dimensions a, b, c is

questions.question: By how much is the sum of (a - 5)(a - 2) and a2 + 6a + 7 is greater than -a2 - 2a + 1?
questions.explanation:
Sum of (a - 5) (a - 2) + (a2 + 6a + 7)
= a2 - 2a - 5a + 10 + a2 + 6a + 7
= 2a2 - 7a + 6a + 10 + 7
= 2a2 - a + 17
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2a2 - a + 1 7 - (-a2 - 2a + l)
= 2a2 - a + 17 + a2 + 2a - 1
= 3a2 + a + 16

questions.explanation: Product = (x2)(-x3)(-x4) = x9

questions.question: 3x {tex}-\frac{1}{2} x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{2} x{/tex} - 4x = ________
questions.explanation:
3x - {tex}\frac{1}{2} x+\frac{3}{2} x{/tex} - 4x
= -x + {tex}\frac{2 x}{2}{/tex} = -x + x = 0

questions.question: Simplify the expression : -3x(7x - 4) + 6x - (13 - 24x2)
The sum of two binomials is 5x2 - 6x. If one of the binomial is 3x2 - 2x. What is the other binomial?
questions.explanation:
Other binomial = 5x2 - 6x - (3x2 - 2x)
= 5x2- 6 x - 3x2 + 2x
= 2x2 - 4

questions.question: If P = 3x - 4y - 8z, Q = -10y + 7x + 11z and R = 19z - 6y + 4x, then P - Q + R is equal to
What must be subtracted from 3x2 + 4y2 - 5 to get 2x2 - 3y2 + 5 ?
questions.explanation:
Let z should be Subtracted from 3x2 + 4y2 - 5 to get 2x2 - 3y2 + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 + 4y2 - 5 - z = 2x2 - 3y2 + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 3x2 + 4y2 - 5 - (2x2 - 3y2 + 5)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 + 4y2 - 5 - 2x2 + 3y2 - 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + 7y2 - 10

questions.question: How many triangles would you find in a net that folds into a square pyramid?
questions.explanation: A square pyramid has four faces so there will be 4 triangles and one base which is square.

questions.question: Gaurav wants to make a pentagonal pyramid. How many triangles does he need to make the object?
questions.explanation:
Gaurav needs 5 triangles to make a pentagonal pyramid. A pentagonal pyramid is a pyramid with a pentagonal base upon which are erected five triangular faces that meet at a point.
Hence 5 triangles are required to make a pentagonal pyramid.

questions.question: The base of a pyramid is a polygon with n sides. How many triangular faces does the pyramid have?
questions.explanation: It will have the same number of faces as the number of sides. A pyramid with n sided base has n+1 vertices, n+1 faces and 2n edges.

questions.question: The most important part of a map is ________.
questions.explanation: The scale of a map is the ratio of a distance on the map to the corresponding distance on the ground.

questions.question: ________ are used to depict different objects/places in a map.
questions.explanation: Symbols are used to depict different objects/places in a map.

questions.question: What are the three views in a solid?
questions.explanation: The three views of solid structures are front view, top view and side view.

questions.question: How many views are there in a solid?
questions.explanation: There are three views of a solid structure. They are a front view, top view and side view.

questions.question: There is no reference or perspective in a ________.
questions.explanation: There is no reference or perspective in a map .i.e. objects that are closer to the observer are shown to be of the same size as those that are farther away.

questions.question: Side of a square garden is 30 m. If the scale used to draw its picture is 1 cm : 5 m, the perimeter of the square in the picture is
questions.explanation:
Given, side of a square garden = 30m
{tex}\because{/tex} Perimeter of a square = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} Side
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter of a square garden = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = 120m
Scale used to draw its picture = 1cm : 5m
Hence, the perimeter of the square in the picture = {tex}\frac {120}{5}{/tex} = 24 cm

questions.question: In a blueprint of a room, an architect has shown the height of the room as 33 cm. If the actual height of the room is 330 cm, then the scale used by her is
questions.explanation:
Given, in a blueprint height of the room = 33 cm,
and actual height of the room = 330 cm
We know that,
Scale of map {tex}\frac {Size \;drawn }{ Actual \;size}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{33}{330}=\frac{1}{10}{/tex}
i.e. Scale = 1 : 10

questions.question: Which of the following is a three dimensional shape?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Euler’s formula for any polyhedron, where F stands for the number of faces, V stands for the number of vertices and E stands for the number of edges is ________.
questions.explanation: Euler’s formula is F + V - E = 2

questions.question: Which of the following is the net of a cylinder?
Which of the following is the net of a hexagonal prism?
Which of the following is true for a polyhedron?
questions.explanation:
Euler’s formula for polyhedron is F + V - E = 2
F = 5, V = 6, E = 9
{tex}\therefore{/tex} F + V - E = 5 + 6 - 9 = 2, True

questions.question: The number of faces in a square prism is ________.
Number of faces, vertices and edges in the rectangular prism are respectively ________.
questions.explanation:
Rectangular prism

Faces = 6,
Vertices = 8,
Edges = 12

questions.question: If a polyhedron has 12 edges and 6 faces, then the number of vertices of the polyhedron is ________.
questions.explanation:
By Euler’s formula F + V - E = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} V = 2 - 6 + 12 = 8

questions.question: The three-dimensional figure formed by rotating a circle is:
questions.explanation: Sphere

questions.question: Which of the following is a front view of a cone?
questions.explanation:
From front, cone looks like a triangular shape.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Matches with the front view of cone.

questions.question: If all corners of a polygon are joined to a point not lying in its plane, we get a
questions.explanation: Pyramid

questions.explanation: height

questions.question: Which of the following is/are true about triangular Pyramid?
questions.explanation: Since, triangular Pyramid has triangular base.

questions.question: The following figure represents a:
questions.explanation: Convex polyhedron, as it is bounded by plane polygonal faces.

questions.question: The three-dimensional figure formed by rotating a triangle is:
questions.explanation: Cone

questions.question: The distance between City A and City B on a map is given as 6 cm. If the scale represents 1 cm = 200 km, then find the actual distance between City A and City B.
questions.explanation:
Actual distance represented by 1 cm = 200 km
Actual distance represented by 6 cm = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 200 km = 1200 km
So, actual distance between city A and city B = 1200 km

questions.question: How many equal triangular faces does a regular pentagonal pyramid have?
questions.explanation: 5 faces

questions.question: Height of a building is 9 m and this building is represented by 9 cm on a map. What is the scale used for the map?
questions.explanation:
Scale of map = {tex}\frac {Size \ drawn} { Actual \ size}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{9 \mathrm{~cm}}{900 \mathrm{~cm}}{/tex} (because 9 m = 900 cm)
= {tex}\frac{1}{100}{/tex}
Thus, scale is 1 : 100.

questions.question: The scale on a map is 1 mm : 4m. Find the distance on the map for an actual distance of 52 m.
questions.explanation:
Distance on map for an actual distance of 4 m = 1 mm
Distance on map for actual distance of 52 m = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \times 52{/tex} = 13 mm
Thus, distance on map for actual distance of 52 m is 13 mm.

questions.question: The shape formed by rotating a right triangle about its height is:
questions.explanation:
Let AABC be triangle right angled at B.

When right triangle ABC is rotated about its height the figure we get is a cone.

questions.question: The distance between school and house of a girl is given by 5 cm in a picture, using the scale 1 cm : 5 km. Find the actual distance between the two places?
questions.explanation:
Distance between school and house in picture = 5 cm
Scale given = 1 cm : 5 km
So, the actual distance between two places
= 5 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{5}{1}{/tex}
= 25 km.

questions.question: The actual length of a painting was 2 m. What is its length in the photograph if the scale used is 1 mm : 20 cm.
questions.explanation:
Actual length of a painting = 2 m = 200 cm Scale used = 1 mm : 20 cm
Length of painting in photograph
= 20 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{20}{/tex} = 10 mm
