questions.question: A garrison of 500 men had provision for 27 days. After 3 days a reinforcement of 300 men arrived. For how many more days will the remaining food last now?
questions.explanation:
Let the remaining food will last for x days.
500 men had provisions for (27 - 3) = 24 days.
(500 + 300) men had provisions for x days. More men, less days
{tex}\therefore{/tex} .800 : 500: : 24 : x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 800 {tex}\times{/tex} x = 500 {tex}\times{/tex} 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}=\frac{500 \times 24}{800}{/tex} = 15

questions.question: A man completes {tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex} of a job in 10 days. At this rate, how many more days will it take him to finish the job?
questions.explanation:
Work done = {tex}\frac{5}{8},{/tex} Balance = {tex}\left(1-\frac{5}{8}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{8}{/tex}
Less work, Less days;
More work, more days
Let the required number of days be x.

Work Days
{tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex} 10
1 {tex}10 \div \frac{5}{8} or 10 \times \frac{8}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3}{8}{/tex} {tex}10 \times \frac{8}{5} \times \frac{3}{8}{/tex} = 6 days

questions.question: A fort is provisioned for 42 days, after 10 days a reinforcement of 200 men arrives and the food will now last 24 days. How many men were there in the fort?
questions.explanation:
Day Men
(42 - 10) x
24 (x + 20)

Above problem is of inverse variation.
So {tex}\frac{42-10}{24}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x+200}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 600

questions.question: Some men promised to do a job in 18 days, but 6 of them became absent and remainder men did the job in 20 days. What is the original number of men?
questions.explanation:
Men Days
x 18
(x - 6) 20

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x}{x-6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{20}{18}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 60

questions.question: A water tap fills a tank in p hours and the tap of the bottom of the tank empties it in q hours. If p is less than q and when both the taps are open, the tank is filled in r hours. Then
questions.explanation:
Time taken by the tap to fill the tank = p hours
Time taken by the tap to empty the tank 7 Rate of burning of shorter candle = = q hours
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In one hour the tap fills {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{p}} {/tex}th part of the tank.
In one hour the tap empties {tex}\frac{1}{q} {/tex}th part of the tank.
Thus, in one hour {tex}\left(\frac{1}{p}-\frac{1}{q}\right) {/tex}th part is filled.
But given tank is filled in r hours when both the taps are opened.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In 1 hour {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{r}} {/tex}th part of tank is filled.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{r}=\frac{1}{p}-\frac{1}{q}{/tex}

questions.question: If the cost of printing a book of 380 pages with 20 lines on each page and on an average 11 words in each line is ₹ 19, then the cost of printing a book with 280 pages 22 lines on each page and 10 words in each line is
questions.explanation:
No. of pages No. of lines No. of words
380 20 11
19    
280 22 10
x    

all are in direct variation
{tex}\left.\begin{array}{c}380: 280 \\ 22: 22 \\ 11: 10 \end{array}\right\}:: 40: x{/tex}
x = {tex}19 \times \frac{280}{380} \times \frac{22}{20} \times \frac{10}{11}{/tex} = ₹ 14

questions.question: A girl 1.2 metre tall casts a shadow 1.1 metre at the time when a building casts a shadow 6.6 metre long. The height of the building is
questions.explanation:
1.2 m tall girl’s shadow = 1.1
x m tall building’s shadow = 6.6
{tex}\frac{1.2}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1.1}{6.6}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1.2}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 7.2 m

questions.question: Two candles have different lengths and thickness. The shorter lasts 11 hours and the longer 7 hours when both are lit simultaneously. If after 3 hours of other being lit together at the same time, both have the same length, then the ratio of their original lengths is
questions.explanation:
Let l2, and l2 be the lengths of longer and shorter candle.
Rate of burning of Longer candle = {tex}\frac{l_1}{11}{/tex}
Rate of burning of shorter candle = {tex}\frac{l_2}{7}{/tex}
Since after 3 hours their length are equal
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{3 l_1}{11}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 l_2}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7l1 = 11l2
7 his equation is satisfied
11 = 11 and 12 = 7
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Ration = {tex}\frac{7}{11}{/tex}

questions.question: Six diesel engines consume 900 liters of diesel, when each one is running for 5 hrs a day. How much diesel will be required by 9 engines, each running 8 hrs a day when 5 diesel engines of former type consume as much diesel as 8 diesel engines of the later type?
questions.explanation:
Let m and n be the number of engine of former type and later type respectively
So, {tex}\frac{m}{n}=\frac{5}{8}=\frac{8 m}{5}{/tex}
So, n = {tex}\frac{8 \times 6}{5}=\frac{48}{5}=\frac{48}{5}{/tex}

No. of engines Diesel working hrs (in litres)
{tex}\frac{48}{5}{/tex} 900 5
9 x 8

Since, number of engines is in direct variation with diesel and working hours.
So, {tex}\left.\begin{array}{r}\frac{48}5:9\\5:8\end{array}\right\}::900:x{/tex}
x = {tex}900 \times \frac{9}{48 / 5} \times \frac{8}{5}{/tex} = 1350 lit.

questions.question: A can do a job in 20 days, B in 30 days and C in 60 days. If A is helped on every 3rd day by B and C, then in how many days, the job is finished?
questions.explanation:
Part of work done by A,B and C together in
3 days = {tex}\frac{1}{20} \times 3+\frac{1}{30} \times 1+\frac{1}{60} \times 1{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total time for the work = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 15 days

questions.question: P and Q can do a job in 25 days and 30 days respectively. They work together for 5 days and then p leaves. Q will finish the rest of work in how many days?
questions.explanation:
P = {tex}\frac{1}{25} ;{/tex} Q = {tex}\frac{1}{30}{/tex}
P + Q = {tex}\frac{1}{25}+\frac{1}{30}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{11}{150}{/tex}
(P + Q)’s 5 days work = {tex}\frac{11}{150} \times 5{/tex} = {tex}\frac{11}{30}{/tex}
Remaining work = {tex}1-\frac{11}{30}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{19}{30}{/tex}
Q can finish the work in 30 days.
Q can finish the {tex}\frac{19}{30} {/tex}th work in
{tex}\frac{19}{30} \times 30{/tex} = 19 days.

questions.question: An empty pipe can empty {tex}\frac{5}{6}{/tex} of cistern in 200 minutes. In 9 minutes, what part of cistern will be emptied?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{5}{6} \times{/tex} capacity is emptied in 20 minutes
so, capacity = {tex}20 \times \frac{6}{5}{/tex} = 24
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In 1 min the pipe can empty {tex}\frac{1}{24} \text { th }{/tex} part.
In 9 min the pipe can empty
{tex}9 \times \frac{1}{24} th{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{8} th{/tex} part.

questions.question: A and B undertake to do a piece of work for ₹ 200. A can do it in 6 days and B in 8 days. With the help of C they finish it in 3 days. How much is paid to C?
questions.explanation:
Reasoning: Each of three is doing work for three days.
A’s work for 3 days = {tex}3 \times \frac{1}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A gets {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times ₹ 200{/tex} = ₹ 100
B’s work for 3 days = {tex}3 \times \frac{1}{8}=\frac{3}{8}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} B gets {tex}\frac{3}{8} \times ₹ 200{/tex} = ₹ 75
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C gets the rest, i.e., ₹ 25.

questions.question: Two men and a boy can do a piece of work in 5 days. While a man and 2 boys can do it in 6 days. If a man is paid at the rate of ₹ 28 a week, what should be the wages of a boy?
questions.explanation:
Reasoning: 2 men and 1 boy can do job in 5 days means. 10 men and 5 boys can do a job in 1 day.
Again, 1 man and 2 boys can do the same job in 6 days means 6 men and 12 boys can do the same job in 1 day.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 10 men and 5 boys = 6 men and 12 boys
i.e. ₹ 16 per week.
i.e., 4 men = 7 boys
Now, 7 boys get ₹ 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 28 per week
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1 boy gets {tex}₹ \frac{4 \times 28}{7}{/tex}

questions.question: A and B can do a job in 16 and 12 days respectively. 4 days before finishing the job, A joins B. B has started the work alone. Find how many days B has worked alone?
questions.explanation:
In the last 4 days the job was done by A and B.
(A + B)’s one-day work = {tex}\frac{1}{16}+\frac{1}{12}=\frac{7}{48}{/tex}
(A + B)’s 4 days work = {tex}4 \times \frac{7}{48}=\frac{7}{12}{/tex}
Remaining work = {tex}\frac{5}{12}{/tex}
Remaining work is done by B alone.
B can finish the job in 12 days.
{tex}\frac{5}{12} \text { th }{/tex}part of job will be finished in
{tex}\frac{5}{12} \times 12{/tex} = 5 days
{tex}\therefore{/tex} B has worked alone for 5 days.

questions.question: Three pipes A, B and C can fill a cistern in 12, 15 and 20 minutes respectively. Pipes A and C work for one minute and pipes B and C work in next minute. In how many minutes the cistern will be lull if this procedure is continued?
questions.explanation:
Reasoning : Work done by pipes A and C in one minute = {tex}\frac{1}{12}+\frac{1}{20}=\frac{5+3}{60}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{8}{60}{/tex} of the cistern.
Work done by pipes B and C in the next minute = {tex}\frac{1}{15}+\frac{1}{20}=\frac{4+3}{60}=\frac{7}{60}{/tex}
Part of the cistern filled in 2 minutes = {tex}\frac{8}{60}+\frac{7}{60}=\frac{15}{60}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The cistern shall be filled in 8 minutes.

questions.question: 16 men or 24 women can do a piece of work in 20 days. The number of days needed to complete the job, if 20 men and 30 women are employed to do the same piece of work is
questions.explanation:
16 men do a work in 20 days.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 16 men do {tex}\frac{1}{20} {/tex}th work in 1 day.
1 man does {tex}\frac{1}{320} {/tex}th work in 1 day
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 20 men do {tex}\frac{20}{320}{/tex}th work in 1 day.
Similarly, 30 women do {tex}\frac{30}{480}{/tex}th work in 1 day.
Now, 20 men and 30 women together do = {tex}\frac{1}{16}+\frac{1}{16}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2}{16}=\frac{1}{8}{/tex} work in 1 day
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Together they do work in 8 days.

questions.question: A gang of labors promise to do a piece of work in 10 days, but 5 out of them become absent. If the rest of the gang do the work in 12 days, find the original number of men.
questions.explanation:
More men, less time (Indirect)
Let original number of men = x

No of Men No of days
x 10
x - 5 12

{tex}\frac{x}{x-5}=\frac{12}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 12x = 60
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 60 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 30

questions.question: If 6 men and 8 boys can do a piece of work in 10 days and 26 men and 48 boys can do the same work in 2 days, the time taken by 15 men and 20 boys to do the same type of work will be
questions.explanation:
Given(6M + 8 B ){tex}\times{/tex} 10 = (26M + 48B) {tex}\times{/tex} 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 60 M + 80 B = 52 M + 96 B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 M = 16 B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 M = 2 B
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 15 M + 20 B = 30 B + 20 B = 50 B
6 M + 8 B = 12 B + 8 B = 20 B
Now

Boys Days
20 10
50{tex}\downarrow{/tex} x (Let)

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{20 \times 10}{50}{/tex} = 4 days

questions.question: Three pipes A, B and C can fill a tank from empty to full in 30 minutes, 20 minutes and 10 minutes respectively. When the tank is empty, all the three pipes are opened. A, B and C discharge chemical solutions P, Q and R respectively. What is the proportion of solution R in the liquid in the tank after 3 minutes?
questions.explanation:
Part filled by (A + B + C) in 3 minutes
= {tex}3\left(\frac{1}{30}+\frac{1}{20}+\frac{1}{10}\right){/tex} = {tex}3 \times \frac{11}{60}=\frac{11}{20}{/tex}
Part filled by C in 3 minutes = {tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required ratio = {tex}\frac{\frac{3}{10}}{\frac{11}{20}}=-\frac{3}{10} \times \frac{20}{11}=\frac{6}{11}{/tex}

questions.question: In a camp, there is enough flour for 300 persons for 42 days. How long will the flour last if 20 more persons join the camp?
questions.explanation:
If number of persons increase, then flour consume more. So, this is the case of inverse proportion.

Number of persons 300 320
Number of days 42 x

Then,
300 {tex}\times{/tex} 42 = 320 {tex}\times{/tex} x
x = {tex}\frac{300 \times 42}{320}{/tex}
x = {tex}39 \frac{3}{8}{/tex} days.

questions.question: If 52 men can do a piece of work in 35 days, in how many days 28 men will do it?
questions.explanation:
Suppose 28 men will do the given piece of work in x days. The given information can be exibited in the following tabular form.

Number of men 52 28
Number of days 35 x

Clearly, less is the number of men, more will be the number of days to finish the work. It is therefore, the case of inverse variation.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Ratio of number of men = Inverse ratio of number of days
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 52 : 28 = x : 35
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{52}{28}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{35}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 52 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 = 28 {tex}\times{/tex} x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{52 \times 35}{28}{/tex} = 65

questions.question: If x and y vary inversely as each other, and x = 10 when y = 6. Find y when x = 15.
questions.explanation:
Since x and y vary inversely as each other, therefore the product xy always remains constant.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 15 {tex}\times{/tex} y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 60 = 15y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{60}{15}=y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 4

questions.question: If 27 men take 15 days to mow 225 hectares of grass, how long will 33 men take to mow 165 hectare?
questions.explanation:
27 men mow 225 hectares in 15 days
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1 man mow 225 hectares in (15 {tex}\times{/tex} 27) days (indirect)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1 man mow 1 hectares in {tex}\frac{15 \times 27}{225}{/tex} days (direct)
1 man mow 165 hectares in {tex}\frac{15 \times 27}{225} \times 165{/tex} days (direct)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 33 men mow 165 hectares in
{tex}\frac{15 \times 27 \times 165}{225 \times 33}{/tex} = 9 days

questions.question: If 10 masons can build a wall 50 meters long in 25 days of 8 hours each, in how many days of 6 hours each will 15 masons build a wall 36 metres long?
questions.explanation:
10 mason 8 hrs 50 m wall 25 days
1 mason 8 hrs 50 m wall 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 days (Indirect)
1 mason 1 hr 50 m wall 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 days (Indirect)
1 mason 1 hr 1 m wall {tex}\frac{25 \times 10 \times 8}{50}{/tex} days (Direct)
1 mason 1 hr 36 m wall {tex}\frac{25 \times 10 \times 8 \times 36}{50}{/tex} days (Indirect)
1 mason 6 hr 36 m wall {tex}\frac{25 \times 10 \times 8 \times 36}{50 \times 6}{/tex} days (Indirect)
15 mason 6 hr 36 m wall {tex}\frac{25 \times 10 \times 8 \times 36}{50 \times 6 \times 15}{/tex} days (Indirect)
= 16 days

questions.question: X and Y can do a piece of work in 72 days. Y and Z can do it in 120 days. X and Z can do it in 90 days. In how many days all the three together can do the work?
questions.explanation:
(X + Y)’s one day work = {tex}\frac{1}{72}{/tex}
(Y + Z)’s one day work = {tex}\frac{1}{120}{/tex}
(Z + X)’s one day work = {tex}\frac{1}{90}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2(X + Y + Z)’s one day work
= {tex}\frac{1}{72}+\frac{1}{120}+\frac{1}{90}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5+3+4}{360}=\frac{12}{360}=\frac{1}{30}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (X + Y + Z) s one day work = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{30}=\frac{1}{60}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} They will complete the work in 60 days.

questions.question: With a uniform speed a car Covers a distance in 8 hours. Had the speed been increased by 4 km/hr, the same distance could have been covered in {tex}7 \frac{1}{2} {/tex}hours. the distance covered is
questions.explanation:
Let the distance be x km.
According to question
{tex}\frac{x}{7 \frac{1}{2}}-\frac{x}{8}=4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x}{15}-\frac{x}{8}=4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{16 x-15 x}{120}=4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 480 km

questions.question: Two positive numbers x and y are inversely proportional. If x increases by 20%, then percentage decrease in y is
questions.explanation:
x {tex}\propto{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{y}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{k}{y},{/tex} k = constant
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac{k}{x}{/tex}
When x increases by 20%
x ' = {tex}x+\frac{20(x)}{100}=\frac{6 x}{5}{/tex}
y' = {tex}\frac{k}{\left(\frac{6 x}{5}\right)}=\frac{5 k}{6 x}{/tex}
Change in y = {tex}\frac{5 k}{6 x}-\frac{k}{x}=\frac{-k}{6 x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Value of y decreases
% decrease in y = {tex}\frac{\frac{k}{6 x}}{\frac{k}{x}} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{6} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{50}{3}=16 \frac{2}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: On a scale of map, 0.6 cm represents 6.6 km. If the distance between the points on the map is 80.5 cm, the actual distance between these points is:
questions.explanation:
Let the actual distance be x km. Then, more distance on the map, more is the actual distance.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 0.6 : 80.5 : : 6.6 : x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.6x = 80.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6.6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{80.5 \times 6.6}{0.6}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 885.5

questions.question: If 45 students can consume a stock of food in 2 months, then for how many days the same stock of food will last for 27 students?
questions.explanation:
More students, less days ; less students, more days

Students Days
45 60
1 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 45
27 {tex}\frac{60 \times 45}{27}{/tex} = 100 days

questions.question: A rope makes 260 rounds of a cylinder with base radius 20 cm. How many times can it go round a cylinder with base radius 26 cm?
questions.explanation:
Less Radius, More rounds; more radius, less rounds.

Radius Round
20 260
1 260 {tex}\times{/tex} 20
26 {tex}\frac{260 \times 20}{26}{/tex} = 20

questions.question: A wheel that has 6 cogs is meshed with a larger wheel of 14 cogs. When the smaller wheel has made 21 revolutions, then the number of 41. revolutions made by the larger wheel is :
questions.explanation:
More cogs, less revolutions; Less cogs, more revolution

Cogs revolutions
6 21
1 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 21
14 {tex}\frac{6 \times 21}{14}{/tex} = 9

questions.question: Seema weaves 25 baskets in 35 days. In how many days will she weave 110 baskets?
questions.explanation:
Let the number of days be x.

Number of baskets 25 110
Number of days 32 1

As the weaving of more baskets will require more days.
So, it is case of direct variation.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{25}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{35}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25 {tex}\times{/tex} x = 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 35
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{110 \times 35}{25}{/tex} = 154 days

questions.question: A car covers a distance in 40 minutes with an average speed of 60 km/hr. What should be the average speed to cover the same distance in 25 minutes?
questions.explanation:
For the same distance time will decrease the speed of vehicle will increase. This is a case of inverse proportion.

Average Speed (km/hr 60 x
Time taken 40 25

{tex}60 \times \frac{40}{60}{/tex} = {tex}x \times \frac{25}{60}{/tex}
{tex}\left[\because 40 \text { mins }=\frac{40}{60} \mathrm{~h}\right]{/tex}
x = {tex}60 \times \frac{40}{60} \times \frac{60}{25}{/tex}
x = 96 km/hr.

questions.question: 18 men can dig a ditch in 10 days. After they have worked for 4 days, how many more men shall be employed to complete the work in 4 days from then?
questions.explanation:
No. of men No, of days Finished part of work
18 4 {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex}
x 4 {tex}\frac{6}{10}{/tex}

No. of men is in inverse variation with finished part of work.
{tex}\left.\begin{array}{c} 4: 4 \\ \frac{4}{10}: \frac{6}{10} \end{array}\right\}: 18: x{/tex}
x = {tex}18 \times \frac{4}{4} \times \frac{\frac6 {10}}{\frac 4 {10}}{/tex} = 27

questions.question: A can do {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} work in 5 days. B can do {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} of same work in 9 days and C can do {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} of that work in 8 days. In how many days can three of them together do the work.
questions.explanation:
A can do 1 work in 10 days
B can do 1 work in {tex}\frac{9 \times 5}{3} \text { days }{/tex} = 15 days
C can do 1 work in {tex}\frac{8 \times 3}{2} \text { days }{/tex} = 12 days
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (A + B + C)’s one day work
{tex}=\frac{1}{10}+\frac{1}{15}+\frac{1}{12}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6+4+5}{60}=\frac{15}{60}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} They will complete the work in 4 days.

questions.question: A train does a journey without stopping in 8 hours. If it had travelled 5 km an hour faster, it would have done the journey in 6 hours 40 min, its slower speed is
questions.explanation:
Let slower speed = u km/hr
u {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = {tex}(u+5) \times \frac{20}{3}{/tex}
[ {tex}\because{/tex} 6hr 40 min = 6hr + {tex}\frac{40}{60} {/tex}hr = {tex}6 \frac{2}{3}=\frac{20}{3} {/tex}hrs]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 24 u = 20 u +100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 u = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u = 25 km/hr

questions.question: Factorise: 12a2b + 15ab2
questions.explanation:
12a2b + 15ab2
by finding commom factor
3ab(4a + 5b)

questions.question: Factorise: 6xy - 4y + 6 - 9x
questions.explanation:
6xy - 4y + 6 - 9x

questions.question: Factorised form of 23xy - 46x + 54y - 108 is
questions.explanation:

= 23x(y - 2) + 54 (y - 2) [taking common]

questions.explanation:

[by splitting the middle term, so that the product of their numerical coefficient is equal constant term]
= (r - 7)(r - 3)

questions.question: Factorised form of p2 - 17p - 38 is

[by splitting the middle term, so that the product of their numerical coefficients is equal constant term]
= p(p - 19) + 2(p - 19) 
= (p - 19)(p + 2)

questions.question: On dividing p(4p2 - 16) by 4p(p - 2), we get
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\frac{p\left(4 p^{2}\ -\ 16\right)}{4 p(p\ -\ 2)}{/tex}
 = {tex}\frac{p\left[(2 p)^{2}\ -\ 4^{2}\right]}{4 p(p\ -\ 2)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{(2 p\ -\ 4)(2 p\ +\ 4)}{4(p\ -\ 2)}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= {tex}\frac{2(p\ -\ 2) \cdot 2(p\ +\ 2)}{4(p\ -\ 2)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{4(p\ -\ 2)(p\ +\ 2)}{4(p\ -\ 2)}{/tex}
= p + 2
Therefore, On dividing p(4p2 - 16) by 4p(p - 2), we get p + 2

questions.question: The common factor of 3ab and 2cd is
questions.explanation:

2cd = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} c {tex}\times{/tex} d{tex}\times{/tex}1
Observing the monomials, we see that, there is no common factor (neither numerical nor literal) between them except 1.
Therefore, common factor = 1

questions.question: An irreducible factor of 24x2y2 is
questions.explanation:

questions.question: If (x2 + 3x + 5) (x2 - 3x + 5) = m2 - n2, then m = ________.

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x2 + 5)2 - (3x)2 = m2 - n2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m2 = (x2 + 5)2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = x2 + 5

questions.explanation:

questions.question: The value of (1002)2 is:
questions.explanation:
(1002)2 = (1000 + 2)2
= (1000)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + (2)2
= 1000000 + 4000 + 4 = 1004004

questions.question: Factors of ax + by + bx + az + ay + bz are:
questions.explanation:
ax + by + bx + az + ay + bz
= (ax + ay + az) + (bx + by + bz)
= a (x + y + z) + b (x + y + z)
= (x + y + z) (a + b)

questions.question: {tex}9 a^2-\frac{12}{5} a+\frac{4}{25}{/tex} can be written as following perfect square:
questions.explanation:
{tex}9 a^2-\frac{12}{5} a+\frac{4}{25}{/tex}
= {tex}(3 a)^2-2 \times 3 a \times \frac{2}{5}+\left(\frac{2}{5}\right)^2{/tex}
= {tex}\left(3 a-\frac{2}{5}\right)^2{/tex}

questions.question: a3 - 4a2 + 12 - 3a
Simplify the given expression 39y2(50y2 - 98) {tex}\div{/tex} 26y2(5y + 7).
questions.explanation:
39y2 (50y2 - 98) {tex}\div{/tex} 26y2(5y + 7)
= {tex}\frac{39 y^2\left(50 y^2-98\right)}{26 y^2(5 y+7)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3\left(50 y^2-98\right)}{2(5 y+7)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 \times 2\left(25 y^2-49\right)}{2(5 y+7)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3\left[(5 y)^2-(7)^2\right]}{(5 y+7)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3[(5 y-7)(5 y+7)]}{5 y+7}{/tex}
= 3(5y - 7)
= 15y - 21
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Simplified value of the given expression is 15y - 21

questions.question: The quotient when 6ab - b2 + 12ac - 2bc is divided by (6a - b) is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{6 a b-b^2+12 a c-2 b c}{(6 a-b)}{/tex}
First expression
6ab- b2 + 12ac - 2bc
= b (6 a - b) + 2c (6a - b)
= (b + 2c)(6a - b)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{6 a b-b^2+12 a c-2 b c}{(6 a-b)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(6 a-b)(b+2 c)}{(6 a-b)}{/tex}
= (b + 2c)

questions.question: The factors of x2 + xy - 2xz - 2yz are
questions.explanation:
x2 + xy - 2xz - 2yz = x(x + y) - 2z(x + y)
= (x + y)(x - 2z)

questions.question: The factors of 1 - p3 are
questions.explanation: 1 - p3 = 13 - p3 = (1 - p) (1 + p + p2)

questions.question: Divide 15(y + 3) (y2 - 16) by 5 (y2 - y - 12)
questions.explanation:
Factorising 15(y + 3) (y2 - 16),
we get 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (y + 3) (y - 4) (y + 4)
On factorising 5(y2 - y - 12), we get
5(y2 - 4y + 3y - 12)
= 5[y(y - 4) + 3(y - 4)]
= 5(y - 4)(y + 3)
Therefore, on dividing the first expression by the second expression, we get
{tex}\frac{15(y+3)\left(y^2-16\right)}{5\left(y^2-y-12\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5 \times 3 \times(y+3)(y-4)(y+4)}{5 \times(y-4)(y+3)}{/tex}
= 3(y + 4)

questions.question: What are the roots of the equation x2 - x - 6 = 0?
questions.explanation:
Given x2 - x - 6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 - 3x + 2x - 6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(x - 3) + 2(x - 3) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 3)(x + 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 3 = 0 or x + 2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3 or x = -2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Solution set is (-2, 3).

questions.question: The solution set of 5x2 - 7x - 6 = 0 is
questions.explanation:
Given 5x2 - 7x - 6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x2 - 10x + 3x - 6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x(x - 2) + 3(x - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2)(5x + 3) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 2 = 0 or 5x + 3 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 or x = {tex}\frac {-3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Solution set is {tex}\left(\frac{-3}{5}, 2\right){/tex}.

questions.explanation: Real factors do not exist for x2 + 4.

questions.question: The factors of a2 + b - ab - a are:
questions.explanation:
a2 + b - ab - a
= a2 - ab + b - a
= a(a - b) - 1 (a - b)
= (a - 1)(a - b)

questions.question: One of the factors of (a2 - b2) (c2 - d2) - 4abcd is:
questions.explanation:
(a2 - b2) (c2 - d2) - 4abcd
= (ac - bd)2 - (bc + ad)2
= (ac - bd + bc + ad) (ac - bd - bc - ad)

questions.question: Factors for a2b2 + c2d2 - a2c2 - b2d2 are:
questions.explanation:
a2b2 + c2d2 - a2c2 - b2d2
= a2b2 - a2c2 - b2d2 + c2d2
= a2(b2 - c2) - d2 (b2 - c2)
= (a2 - d2) (b2 - c2)
= (a + d) (a - d) (b + c) (b - c)

questions.question: Factorise: 27a3 - b3 - 1 - 9ab.
questions.explanation:
27a3 - b3 - 1 - 9ab
= (3a)3 + (-b)3 + (-1)3 - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3a {tex}\times{/tex} (-b) {tex}\times{/tex} (-1)
= [3a + (-b) + (-1)] [(3a)2 + (-b)2 + (-1)2
- 3a {tex}\times{/tex} (-b) - (-b) {tex}\times{/tex} (-1) - 3a {tex}\times{/tex} (-1)]
= (3a - b - 1) (9a2 + b2 + 1 + 3ab - b + 3a)
[{tex}\therefore{/tex} a3 + b3 + c3 - 3abc = (a + b + c) (a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - be - ca)]

questions.question: The value of 52xyz (xy + yz + xz + y2) (z + x) {tex}\div{/tex} 104xy(xy + xz + yz + y2) is ________.
questions.explanation:
52xyz (xy + yz + xz + y2)
(z + x) {tex}\div{/tex} 104 xy (xy + xz + yz + y2)
{tex}= \frac{52 x y z\left(x y+y z+x z+y^2\right)(z+x)}{104 x y\left(x y+x z+y z+y^2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{z\left(x y+y z+x z+y^2\right)(z+x)}{2\left(x y+y z+x z+y^2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac12{/tex} (z)(x + z)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required value is {tex}\frac{\mathrm{z}(\mathrm{z}+\mathrm{x})}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The roots of 4x2 - 20x + 25 = 0 are
questions.explanation:
Given 4x2 - 20x + 25 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x2 - 10x - 10x + 25 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x(2x - 5) - 5(2x - 5) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 5)(2x - 5) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 5 = 0 or 2x - 5 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} or {tex}x=\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The quadratic equation has equal roots
{tex}\frac 52{/tex} and {tex}\frac 52{/tex}.

questions.question: Find the roots of 3x - 2 = {tex}\frac 8x{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given 3x - 2 = {tex}\frac8x{/tex}
Multiplying by x on both sides, we get
3x2 - 2x = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 - 2x - 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 - 6x + 4x - 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x(x - 2) + 4(x - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2) (3x + 4) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 2 = 0 or 3x + 4 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 or x = - {tex}\frac 43{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The roots are 2, -{tex}\frac 43{/tex}

questions.question: Factors of: 8a3 + b3 + 12a2b + 6ab2
questions.explanation:
8a3 + b3 + 12a2b + 6ab2
= (2a)3 + (b)3 + 3{tex}\cdot{/tex}(2a)2 b + 3{tex}\cdot{/tex} (2a)(b)2
= (2a + b)3 [ {tex}\therefore{/tex} (x + y)3 = x3 + y3 + 3x2y + 3xy2]
= (2a + b)(2a + b)(2a + b)

questions.question: Factors of: x3 - {tex}\frac{1}{x^3}-2 x+\frac{2}{x}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^3-\frac{1}{x^3}-2 x+\frac{2}{x}{/tex} {tex}= x^3-\frac{1}{x^3}-2\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}= \left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(x^2+x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)-2\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}= \left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)\left[x^2+1+\frac{1}{x^2}-2\right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left(x^3-\frac{1}{x^3}-2 x+\frac{2}{x}\right){/tex} {tex}= \left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}-1\right){/tex}

questions.question: Factors of: 64m3 - 343x3
questions.explanation:
64m3 - 343x3 = (4m)3 - (7x)3
= (4m - 7x) [(4m)2 + 2(4m)(7x) - (7)2]
(64m3 - 34x3) = (4m - 7x) (16m2 + 56mx + 49x2)
using (a3 - b3) = (a - b)(a2 + b2 + ab)

questions.question: Factors of: 27x3 + y3 + z3 - 9xyz
questions.explanation:
27x3 + y3 + z3 - 9xyz
= (3x)3 + (y)3 + (z)3 - 3(3x) (y) (z)
= (3x + y + z) [(3x)2 +(y)2 +(z)2
- (3x)(y) - (y)(z) - (3x) z]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 27x3 + y3 + z3 = (3x + y + z)(9x2 + y2 + z2 - 3xy - yz - 3xz)

questions.question: Calculate: (28)3 + (-15)3 + (-13)3
questions.explanation:
Let a = 28, b = -15, c = -13
Here we see that a + b + c = 28 + (-15) + (-13) = 0
{tex} \therefore{/tex} a3 + b3 + c3 = 3abc
{tex} \therefore{/tex} (28)3 + (-15)3 + (-13)3 = 3(28)(-15)(-13)
(28)3 + (-15)3 + (-13)3 = 16380

questions.question: Factors of: (p - q)3 + (q - r)3 + (r - p)3
questions.explanation:
Let a = p - q, b = q - r, c = r - p
We see that
a + b + c = (p - q) + (q - r) + (r - p) = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a3 + b3 + c3 = 3abc
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (p - q)3 + (q - r)3 + (r - p)3
= 3(p - q) (q - r) (r - p)

questions.explanation:
16x2 - 72xy + 81y2 - 12x + 27y

({tex}\because{/tex} (a - b)2 = a2 - 2ab + b2)

questions.question: Find the value of x2 + 8y3 divided by x + 2y
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x^3+8 y^3}{x+2 y}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{(x)^3+2(y)^3}{(x+2 y)}{/tex}
Applying the identity
a3 + b3 = (a + b) (a2 - ab + b2) to (x)3 + (2y)3
Where, a = x and b = 2y, we get,
R.H.S = {tex}\frac{(x+2 y)\left[x^2-x \times 2 y+(2 y)^2\right]}{(x+2 y)}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^3+8 y^3}{x+2 y}{/tex} = x2 - 2xy + 4y2

questions.question: The value of (3x3 + 9x2 + 27x) {tex}\div{/tex} 3x is:
questions.explanation:
(3x3 + 9x2 + 27x) {tex}\div{/tex} 3x
Factorising, 3x3 + 9x2 + 27x
= 3x(x2 + 3x + 9)
Then, {tex}\frac{3 x\left(x^2+3 x+9\right)}{3 x}{/tex}
= x2 + 3x + 9.

questions.question: If {tex}a^{\frac{1}{3}}+b^{\frac{1}{3}}+c^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} = 0, then the value of (a + b + c)3 will be:
questions.explanation:
Since {tex}a^{\frac{1}{3}}+b^{\frac{1}{3}}+c^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} = 0,
{tex}\left(\frac{1}{a^3}\right)^3+\left(b^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^3+\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^3{/tex} = {tex}3 a^{\frac{1}{3}} b^{\frac{1}{3}} c^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b + c = 3{tex}(a b c)^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a + b + c)3 = 27abc.

questions.explanation:
pq2 + q(p - 1) - 1

questions.question: The factors of {tex}\frac{1}{7} y^2+y-14{/tex} are
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{y^2}{7}+y-14{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y^2+7 y-98}{7}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{y^2+14 y-7 y-98}{7}{/tex}

= {tex}\frac{(y-7)(y+14)}{7}{/tex}

questions.question: One of the factors of {tex}x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+2-2 x-\frac{2}{x}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+2-2 x-\frac{2}{x}{/tex}
{tex}= \left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+2\right)-2\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}= \left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2-2\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}= \left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(x+\frac{1}{x}-2\right){/tex}

questions.question: If the factors of a2 + b2 + 2(ab + bc + ca) are (a + b + m) and (a + b + nc), then the value of m + n is
questions.explanation:
a2 + b2 + 2ab + 2bc + 2ca + c2 - c2
= (a + b + c)2 - c2 = (a + b) (a + b + 2c)
On comparison, we get
m = 0, n = 2
m + n = 0 + 2 = 2

questions.question: The area of a rectangle is x2 + 12 xy + 27 y2 and its length is (x + 9y). Find the breadth of the rectangle.
questions.explanation:
Breadth = {tex}\frac{\text { Area }}{\text { Length }}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^2+12 x y+27 y^2}{(x+9 y)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^2+9 x y+3 x y+27 y^2}{(x+9 y)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x(x+9 y)+3 y(x+9 y)}{(x+9 y)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(x+9 y)(x+3 y)}{(x+9 y)}{/tex}
= (x + 3y)

questions.explanation:

questions.question: a12 - 1 can be factorised as:
questions.explanation:
a12 - 1 = (a6)2 - (1)2
= (a6 - 1)(a6 + 1)
= [(a3)2 - (1)2](a6 + 1)
= (a3 - 1)(a3 + 1) (a6 + 1)
= (a - 1) (a2 + a + 1) (a + 1) (a2 - a + 1) (a6 + 1)
= (a - 1)(a2 + a + 1)(a2 - a + 1) [(a2 + 1)(a4 - a2 + 1)]

questions.question: Factors of (4a - 3b)3 - (3a - b)3 - (a - 2b)3 are:
questions.explanation:
(4a - 3b)3 - (3a - b)3- (a - 2b)3
= (4a - 3b)3 + (b - 3a)3 + (2b - a)3
Let,
x = 4a - 3b; y = b - 3a; z = 2b - a
On adding, x + y + z = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x3 + y3 + z3 = 3xyz
Substituting x, y, z values, we get
(4a - 3b)3 + (b - 3a)3 + (2b - a)3
= 3(4a - 3b)(b - 3a)(2b - a)

questions.question: Factors of a4 + a2 + 1 are:
questions.explanation:
a4 + a2 + 1 = (a2 + 1)2 - a2
= (a2 + 1)2 - (a)2
= (a2 + a + 1) (a2 - a + 1)

questions.question: Factors of 2(a + b)2 - 9(a + b) - 5 are
questions.explanation:
2(a + b)2 - 9(a + b) - 5
2a2 + 2b2 + 4ab - 9a - 9b - 5
= (2a2 + 2ab) + (2b2 + 2ab) + (a + b) - (10a + 10b + 5)
2a(a + b) + 2b (a + b) + (a + b) - 5 (2a + 2b + 1)
= (a + b) (2a + 2b + 1) - 5(2a + 2b + 1)
= (2a + 2b +1) (a + b - 5).
Another way
Let a + b = x
Then,
2(a + b)2 - 9(a + b) -5 = 2x2 - 9x - 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x2 - 10x + x - 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x(x - 5) + 1(x - 5)
(2x + 1)(x - 5)
Put the value of x = a + b
[2(a + b)+ 1] (a + b - 5)
(2a + 2b + 1) (a + b - 5)

questions.question: Choose the correct option for the roots of {tex}\frac{1}{x}-\frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{2+x}{/tex} = 0
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\frac{1}{x}-\frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{2+x}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{2+x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2+x+x}{x(2+x)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac34{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x+2}{2 x+x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac34{/tex}
Cross-multiplying, we get
3(2x + x2) = 4(2x + 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x + 3x2 = 8x + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 + 6x - 8x - 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 - 6x + 4x - 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x(x - 2) + 4(x - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2) (3x + 4) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 or x = {tex}-\frac43{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The roots are {tex}-\frac43,2{/tex}

questions.question: The roots of {tex}\frac{x}{x-1}+\frac{x-1}{x}{/tex} = {tex}2\frac12{/tex} are
questions.explanation:
Given
{tex}\frac{x}{x-1}+\frac{x-1}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac52{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^2+(x-1)^2}{(x-1) x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac52{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^2+x^2-2 x+1}{x^2-x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac52{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x^2-2 x+1}{x^2-x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac52{/tex}
Cross-multiplying, we get
2(2x2 - 2x + 1) = 5 (x2 - x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x2 - 4x + 2 = 5x2 - 5x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x2 - 5x - 4x2 + 4x - 2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 - x - 2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 -2x + x - 2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(x - 2) + 1(x - 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2) (x + 1) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 2 = 0 or x + 1 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 or x = - 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The solution is -1, 2

questions.question: Factorise : x2 + (a + b + c) x + ab + bc
questions.explanation:
x2 + (a + b + c)x + ab + bc
= x2 + ax + bx + cx + ab + bc
= x2 + ax + cx + bx + ab + bc
= x(x + a + c) + b(x + a + c)
= (x + b) (x + a + c)

questions.question: Factorise: {tex}x^2+\frac{4}{x^2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Let f(x) = {tex}x^2+\frac{4}{x^2}{/tex} = {tex}(x)^2+\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) = {tex}(x)^2+2 \cdot x \cdot \frac{2}{x}+\left(\frac{2}{x}\right)^2-2 \cdot x \cdot \frac{2}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) = {tex}\left(x+\frac{2}{x}\right)^2-(2)^2{/tex}
f(x) = {tex}\left(x+\frac{2}{x}+2\right)\left(x+\frac{2}{x}-2\right){/tex}

questions.question: Factorise: x2 + 19x - 150
questions.explanation:
x2 + 19x - 150
= x2 + 25x - 6x - 150 [By splitting the middle term]
= x(x + 25) - 6(x + 25)
= (x - 6) (x + 25)

questions.question: Factorise: {tex}x^2+5 \sqrt{5} x+30{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^2+5 \sqrt{5} x+30{/tex}
= {tex}x^2+2 \sqrt{5} x+3 \sqrt{5} x+30{/tex}
= {tex}x(x+2 \sqrt{5})+3 \sqrt{5}(x+2 \sqrt{5}){/tex}
{tex}[2 \sqrt{5} \times 3 \sqrt{5}=2 \times 3 \times(\sqrt{5} \times \sqrt{5})]{/tex}
= {tex}(x+3 \sqrt{5})(x+2 \sqrt{5}){/tex}

subtopics.name: Division of Algebraic Expressions Continued (Polynomial ÷ Polynomial)

questions.question: Factors of {tex}\left(a^3-\frac{1}{a^3}-2 a+\frac{2}{a}\right){/tex} are
questions.explanation:
{tex}a^3-\frac{1}{a^3}-2 a+\frac{2}{a}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(a-\frac{1}{a}\right)\left(a^2+1+\frac{1}{a^2}\right)-2\left(a-\frac{1}{a}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a3 - b3 = (a - b)(a2 + ab + b2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(a-\frac{1}{a}\right)\left(a^2+1+\frac{1}{a^2}-2\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(a-\frac{1}{a}\right)\left(a^2+\frac{1}{a^2}-1\right){/tex}

questions.question: The rational expression {tex}\frac{x^3-3 x^2+2 x}{x^2 y-2 x y}{/tex} in its lowest form is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x^3-3 x^2+2 x}{x^2 y-2 x y}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x\left(x^2-3 x+2\right)}{x y(x-2)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^2-2 x-x+2}{y(x-2)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x(x-2)-(x-2)}{y(x-2)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(x-2)(x-1)}{y(x-2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x-1}{y}{/tex}

questions.question: Value of {tex}\frac{c^2-(a-b)^2}{(b+c)^2-a^2}+\frac{a^2-(b-c)^2}{(a+c)^2-b^2}+\frac{b^2-(a-c)^2}{(a+b)^2-c^2}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{c^2-(a-b)^2}{(b+c)^2-a^2}+\frac{a^2-(b-c)^2}{(a+c)^2-b^2}+\frac{b^2-(a-c)^2}{(a+b)^2-c^2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(c+a-b)(c-a+b)}{(b+c-a)(b+c+a)}+\frac{(a+b-c)(a-b+c)}{(a+c-b)(a+c+b)}{/tex}
{tex}+\frac{(b+a-c)(b-a+c)}{(a+b-c)(a+b+c)}\left[x^2-y^2=(x+y)(x-y)\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{c+a-b}{a+b+c}+\frac{a+b-c}{a+b+c}+\frac{b-a+c}{a+b+c}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{c+a-b+a+b-c+b-a+c}{a+b+c}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a+b+c}{a+b+c}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: Factors of 2a(x - y) + 3b(5x - 5y) + 4c(2y - 2x)
questions.explanation:
2a(x - y) + 3b(5x - 5y) + 4c(2y - 2x)
= 2a(x - y) + 3b {tex}\times{/tex} 5(x - y) + 4c(-2) (x - y)
= (x - y) (2a + 15b - 8c)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The factors of
2a (x - y) + 3b(5x - 5y) + 4c(2y - 2x)
are (x - y) and (2x + 15b - 3c)

questions.question: Factors of a(3x - 4y)2 + 2b(4y - 3x)
questions.explanation:
a(3x - 4y)2 + 2b(4y - 3x)
= (3x - 4y) [a(3x - 4y) -2b]
(3x - 4y) (3ax - 4ay - 2b)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The factors of a (3x - 4y)2 + 2b(4y - 3x) are (3x - 4y) and (3ax - 4ay - 2b).

questions.question: Factors of {tex}x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}-3{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}-3{/tex} {tex}= \left[x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}-2-1\right]{/tex}
As 2 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}, thus replacing 2, we get
R.H.S = {tex}\left[x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}-2 x \frac{1}{x}\right]-1^2{/tex} {tex}=\left[x-\frac{1}{x}\right]^{-2}-1^2{/tex}
This is of the form a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)
where a = x - {tex}\frac 1x{/tex} and b = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + {tex}\frac 1{x^2}{/tex} - 3 = {tex}\left[x-\frac{1}{x}+1\right]\left[x-\frac{1}{x}-1\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The factors of {tex}x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}-3{/tex} are {tex}\left[x \frac{1}{x}+1\right]{/tex} and {tex}\left[x-\frac{1}{x}-1\right]{/tex}

questions.question: Value of a3 + a2 + 2a + 8 by a + 2
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\frac{a^3+a^2+2 a+8}{a+2}{/tex}
Rearranging the terms and simplifying by applying the identity
{tex}\frac{a^3+a^2+2 a+8}{a+2}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{\left(a^3+8\right)+\left(a^2+2 a\right)}{a+2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(a^3+2^3\right)+a(a+2)}{a+2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(a+2)\left(a^2-a \times 2+2^2\right)+a(a+2)}{a+2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(a+2)\left[\left(a^2-2 a+2^2\right)+a\right]}{a+2}{/tex}
= a2 - 2a + 4 + a = a2 - a + 4

questions.question: Value of x7y - xy7by (x + y) (x2 - xy + y2)
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x^7 y-x y^7}{(x+y)\left(x^2-x y+y^2\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x y\left(x^6-y^6\right)}{(x+y)\left(x^2-x y+y^2\right)}{/tex}
Adjusting x6 - y6 as (x3)2 - (y3)2 and applying
a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b) where a = x3 and b = y3,
we get,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{x y\left(x^6-y^6\right)}{(x+y)\left(x^2-x y+y^2\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x y\left[\left(x^3\right)^2-\left(y^3\right)^2\right]}{(x+y)\left(x^2-x y+y^2\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x y\left(x^3+y^3\right)\left(x^3-y^3\right)}{(x+y)\left(x^2-x y+y^2\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x y\left(x^3+y^3\right)\left(x^3-y^3\right)}{\left(x^3+y^3\right)}{/tex}
[since, (a + b)(a2 - ab + b2) = a3 + b3] = xy(x3 - y3)

questions.question: If x {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 and when a polynomial is divided by 2x, the quotient is 3x4 - 9x2 + 4. Find the polynomial.
questions.explanation:
Let the polynomial be p(x)
{tex}\frac{p(x)}{2 x}{/tex} = 3x4 - 9x2 + 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p(x) = (3x4 - 9x2 + 4) {tex}\times{/tex} 2x
= 6x5 - 18x3 + 8x

questions.question: Which of the following is a factor of x2 - 2xy + y2 - z2?
questions.explanation:
x2 - 2xy + y2 - z2
= (x - y)2 - z2
= (x - y - z) (x - y + z)

questions.question: The fractorisation of 1 + p + q + r + pq + qr + pr + pqr is
If the polynomials (px3 + 4x2 + 8x - 4) and (x3 - 4x + p) are divided by (x - 3) then the remainder in each case is the same. Then the value of p is
questions.explanation:
Let f(x) = px3 + 4x2 + 3x - 4
and g(x) = x3 - 4x + p
when f(x) is divided by (x - 3), the remainder
f(3) = p(3)3 + 4(3)2 + 3(3) - 4
= 27p + 36 + 9 - 4
f(3) = 27p + 41 ...(i)
also when g(x) is divided by (x - 3), the remainder
g(3) = (3)3 - 4(3) + p
= 27 - 12 + p
g(3) = 15 + p ...(ii)
given that f(3) = g(3)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 27p + 41 = 15 + p
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 26p = 15 - 41 = -26
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = -1

questions.question: Simplified from of {tex}\frac{\left(-6 a^3 b^5\right)\left(2 a^2 b^3\right)}{-18 a^4 b^8 c^3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\left(-6 a^3 b^5\right)\left(2 a^2 b^3\right)}{-18 a^4 b^8 c^3}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{12}{18} \frac{a^{3+2} b^{5+3}}{a^4 b^8 c^3}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{2}{3} \frac{a^5 b^8}{a^4 b^8 c^3}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{2}{3} \frac{a}{c^3}{/tex}

questions.question:
One of the factors of x7 + xy6 is:

  1. x2 + y2
  2. x
  3. either i or ii
  4. Neither i nor ii
questions.explanation:
x7 + xy6 = x(x6 + y6)
= x [(x2)3 + (y2)3]
= x(x2 + y2)(x4 - x2y2 + y4)

questions.question: Factorise: {tex}\left(5 x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2+5\left(5 x-\frac{1}{x}\right)+6{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Just for the sake of simplification,
let 5x {tex}-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = y
So, the expression becomes as follows:
y2 + 5y + 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y2 + 2y + 3y + 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y(y + 2) + 3(y + 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y(y + 2) + 3(y + 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (y + 3)(y + 2)
Putting y = {tex}5 x-\frac{1}{x}{/tex}, the final expression is
{tex}\left(5 x-\frac{1}{x}+3\right)\left(5 x-\frac{1}{x}+2\right){/tex}

questions.question: The LCM of polynomials 2x2 - 3x - 2 and x3 - 4x2 + 4x is
questions.explanation:
Let f(x) = 2x2 - 3x - 2 &
q(x) = x3 - 4x2 + 4x
f(x) = 2x2 - 4x + x - 2
q(x) = x(x2 - 4x + 4)
= 2x (x - 2) + (x - 2) q(x) = x(x - 2)2
f(x) = (x - 2) (2x + 1)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} LCM of f(x) & q(x) = x(x - 2)2 (2x + 1)

questions.question: Factorize, a3 + 8b3 + 6a2b + 12ab2.
questions.explanation: a3 + 8b3 + 6a2b + 12ab2 = a3 + 8b3 + 6ab(a + 2b) = (a + 2b)3

questions.question: Where does the point (0, -6) lie?
questions.explanation: Here x = 0 (origin) and y = -6. So point (0, -6) lies on y-axis.

questions.question: Plot a line graph for the following points (1, 2), (2, 1), (3,2), (4,3).
questions.explanation: It is a line with a dip as the 2nd point has lower Y value than others. X Value is gradually increasing as 1, 2, 3, 4.

questions.question: A graph that displays data that changes continuously over periods of time is
questions.explanation: Line graph is an important way to represent and compare the data which varies continuously. A line graph displays the relation between two varying quantities. In a line graph, we connect all the points by a line segment while in bar graph and histogram, we use rectangles of uniform width.

questions.question:
The given graph shows the progress of a cyclist during a ride.

Which of the following describe the rider’s progress over the period of time.

options.option: As time passes the speed of cyclist decreases steadily.
As time passes speed of cyclist increases.
Speed of cyclist increases for a short time period and then increases very slowly.

questions.explanation: As time passes, the speed of cyclist decreases steadily.

questions.question:
Study the graph and answer the questions that follow.

Which was the hottest day?
questions.explanation: On Friday temperature was high. So it was the hottest day.

questions.question: The line graph shows the sale of dolls by Suhas from Monday to Saturday on a particular week. Given that cost of one doll is ₹ 35, how much did Suhas receive from the sale of dolls on Saturday?
questions.explanation:
Number of dolls sold on Saturday = 40
Cost of 1 doll = 35
Total cost of 35 dolls = 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 = 1400

questions.question: The line graph shows the monthly expenditure of the the Vasu family. The difference between their highest and lowest monthly expenditure is:
questions.explanation:
Month with highest expenditure= February
Month with lowest expenditure = April
Difference in expenditure = 600 - 200 = 400

questions.explanation:
x-axis shows time (in hours) and
y-axis shows Temperature (in oF)

questions.question: The line graph shows that price of chickens in towns M and N. On which days are the price of chickens the same in bothe the towns?
questions.explanation: Price of chickens in towns M and N are same on Tuesday.

questions.question: The line graph shows the sale of watches in a company. How many watches were sold in those 5 months?
questions.explanation: Total watches sold = (20 + 50 + 20 + 60 + 30) = 180

questions.question: The line graph shows the monthly expenditure of Vasu family. The total expenditure over the first 3 months is:
questions.explanation: Total expenditure over the first three months = (500 + 600 + 300) = 1400.

Changes in y when x changes from 0 to 2.

questions.explanation: There is no change in Y

questions.question: The number 2 8 2 2 1 is divisible by which of the following:
If the 4-digit number 2X Y7 is exactly divisible by 3, then which of the following is the least value of (X + Y)?
If M is a number such that M ÷ 5 gives a remainder of 1, then which of the following is the one’s digit of M?
Substitute digits for the letter N to make the following Addition problem true.
questions.explanation: On adding 7 and 5 you get 12. One gets carried over to tens place. 8 and 1 make 9. Answer shows 5. Only 6 added to 9 would give 15.

questions.question: Substituting digits for the letter A to make the following Addition problem true.
questions.explanation: On adding 5 8 7 we get 20. 2 gets carried over. 2 3 9 8 added gives 22.

questions.question: Substitute digits for the letter A to make the following Multiplication problem true.
questions.explanation: 4 times A is 8 means A can be 2 or 7. A times A in second step is 4. Thus A is 2.

questions.question: Substitute digits for the letters A and B to make the following Multiplication problem true.
questions.explanation:
In the solution when we add 7+7+2 we get A which is equal to 16 so A= 6 and 1 will be the carry over.  Thus A is 6.
Now in first step the multiplication of B and  A which is equal to 6 is giving a number whose ones digit is B again. This is possible when
B = 2, 4, 6 or 8.
In case B = 4, the product {tex}B\times6=\;4\times6\;=\;24,{/tex}now 2 will be carry over for the next step 
we have {tex}4\times7+2=30{/tex} , hence the zero in between 7 and B and 3 will be carry over for the next step
now we have {tex}4\times1+3=7{/tex}
so value of B= 4

questions.question: Substitute digits for the letters P and Q to make the following Multiplication problem true.
questions.explanation:
In the solution when we add P + Q = P that means  Q = 0
now {tex}3\times P{/tex} gives the number whose one's digit is also P this is possible only when P = 5
Therefore, the value of P = 5 and Q = 0

questions.question: By which of the following number 225 is divisible? 2, 3, 4, and 6
questions.explanation: It's digit sum = 9. So, it is divisible by 3

questions.question: The number 477 is divisible by each of the following numbers except: 3, 6, 9
questions.explanation: 4 + 7 + 7 = 18. Divisible by 9 and 3. Number ending in 7 cannot be divisible by 6.

questions.question: If a number is divisible by 9, then it is also divisible by which number?
questions.explanation: 3 is a factor of 9.

questions.question: Which of the numbers is divisible by 10?
questions.explanation: Because it ends in digit zero.

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 3, what might be the ones digit of N?
questions.explanation: Any digit is completely divided by 5 if it's unit digit is 5 or 0 If remainder is 3 then the unit digit must be either 0 + 3 = 3 or 5 + 3 = 8

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following :-
questions.explanation: 1 + B is 8 so B = 7. B + A gives 1 in units digit. Thus A has to be 4.

questions.question: If the division N {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 1, what might be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation:
A number is divisible by 5 when its ones digit is 0 or 5.
so if a division N {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 1 then the digit on ones place might be 5 + 1 = 6

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following:-
questions.explanation:
When A is added to B, it gives 9 that is a number whose ones place is 9.Sum can be 9 only as summation of two single digits cannot be 19. So no carry generated.
In the next step, A + 2 = 0
It is possible if A = 8.
Therefore, 2 + 8 = 10 and 1 will be carried forward for the next step.
1 + 1 + 6 = 8. Therefore, value of A = 8.
When A is added to B, it gives 9.
A + B = 9
8 + B = 9
Therefore, value of B = 1
{tex}\begin{gathered} \underline {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\;128} \\ { + 681} \end{array}} \hfill \\ 809 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} {/tex}

Thus value of A and B is 8 and 1 respectively.

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 4, what might be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation: We know for a number to be divisible by 5 should have 0 or 5 at ones place. If the remainder is 4 than the ones digit of N must be either 0 + 4 = 4 or 5 + 4 = 9. Therefore, the answer is either 4 or 9.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following :
questions.explanation:
When 3is multiplied with B it gives a number whose onesplace is B again. So, B must be 5 or 0.
Let B = 5
First step: 5 × 3 = 15
1 will be carried forward. Therefore, (A × 3) + 1 = CA. This is not possible for any number.
Therefore, value of B has to be 0 only.
If B = 0, then there will be no carry. So we get A × 3 = CA. 
When a number is multiplied with 3, its ones place should be the number itself. That is possible only for A = 0 or 5. But A cannot be 0 as it has to be two digit numbers. Therefore, the value of A is 5. Thus we get the following
{tex}\begin{gathered} \underline {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {50} \\ { \times 3} \end{array}} \hfill \\ 150 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} {/tex}
The value of A,  B and C is 5, 0 and 1 respectively.

questions.question: Find Q in the addition.
questions.explanation: The addition of Q and 3 is giving 1i.e, a number who's ones  digit is 1 which is possible if Q = 8. Now 8 + 3 = 11. Therefore,  1 + 1 + Q = 0 i. e, a number whose ones digit is 0 whicheans the number should be 10. So, 1 + 1 + Q = 10. Therefore, the value of Q = 8.

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 0, what might be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation: If a number is completely divisible by 5, it should have either 0 or 5 at its ones digit.

questions.question: If 21y5 is a multiple of 9, where y is a digit, what is the value of y?
questions.explanation:
If a number is a multiple of 9, the sum of its digits must be divisible by 9. 
Sum of digits of 21y5 = 2 + 1 + y + 5 = 8 + y. 
Hence, 8 + y should be a multiple of 9. 
Next multiple of 9 is 9. So, 8 + y = 9
So, the value of y = 1.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following:
questions.explanation:
When 5 is multiplied with B it gives a number whose ones place is B again. So, B must be 5 or 0.
Let B = 5
First step: 5 × B = 5 × 5 = 25
2 will be carried forward. Therefore, (A × 5) + 2 = CA. This is possible for number A = 2 or 7.
The multiplication is as given below.
25 × 5 =125
75 × 5 = 375
Let B = 0
First step: 5 × B = 5
5 × 0 = 0
There will not be any carry in this case.
In the next step, 5 × A = CA
This can happen only when the value of A is 5 or 0.
However, A cannot be 0 as AB is two digit number. Therefore, the value of A is 5.
50 ×   5 = 250
Therefore, the value of A,  B and C are 5, 0 and 2 respectively.

questions.question: Find A and B in the addition. A + A + A = BA
questions.explanation:
Here,  A + A + A = BA as the sum of 3 ones digit numbers is a two-digit number so the value of A will be greater than 3. 
Putting the value of A = 4,
4 + 4 + 4 = 12 which do not satisfy the equation. 
Putting the value of A = 5, 
5 + 5 + 5 = 15, which satisfies the equation. 
Therefore, A= 5 and B = 1.

questions.question: If 31z5 is a multiple of 9, where z is a digit, what is the value of z?
questions.explanation: For a number to be multiple of 9, the sum of its digit must be divisible by 9. Here,  3 + 1 + 5 = 9. So,  to make 31z5 divisible by 9 we will add either 0 or 9. Therefore, the value of z is either 0 or 9.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in the following:
questions.explanation:
When 6 is multiplied with B, it gives a number whose ones place is B. It is possible only if B = 0, 2, 4, 6 or 8.
B = 0;
The product will be 0 in this case so it is not possible.
B = 2;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 12 and 1 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 1 = BB = 22. Then integer value of A is not possible.
B = 6;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 36 and 3 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 3 = BB = 66. Then integer value of A is not possible.
B = 8;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 48 and 4 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 4 = BB = 88.
6A = 84.
A = 14
But A is single digit number.
Then value of A is not possible.
B = 4;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24 and 2 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 2 = BB = 44.
6A = 42.
A = 7
The multiplication is given below
74 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 444
Thus integer value of A and B is 7 and 4 respectively.

questions.question: Find the value of A & B from the following? 3A + 25 = B2
questions.explanation:
Here,  A = 5 + 2 = 7
As 7 + 5 = 12. So, 1 will be carried over. 
Now,  1 + 3 + 2 = B
B = 6
So, A = 7 and B = 6

questions.question: If 21z5 is a multiple of 9, where z is a digit, find the value of z?
questions.explanation:
For a number to be divisible by 9, the sum of its digits must be divisible by 9
Here, 2 + 1 + 5 = 8
Next multiple of 9 is 9. So, we will add 9 - 8 = 1. Therefore, the value of z = 1.

questions.question: Generalised form of a two-digit number xy is
questions.explanation: {tex}10 x + y{/tex}

questions.question: The usual form of 1000a + 10b + c is
questions.explanation:
Given expanded (or generalised) form of a number is 1000a + 10b + c. Then, we have to find its usual form.
We can write it as 1000 {tex}\times{/tex} a + 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 + 10 {tex}\times{/tex} b + c
i.e. a0bc, which is the usual form.

questions.question: Generalised form of a four-digit number abdc is
questions.explanation:
In generalised form, we express a number as the sum of the products of its digits with their respective place values.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} abdc is written in generalised form as 1000a + 100b + 10d + c.
i.e. abdc = 1000a + 100b + 10d + c

questions.question: The sum of all the numbers formed by the digits x, y and z of the number xyz is divisible by
questions.explanation:
We have, xyz + yzx + zxy
= (100x + 10y + z) + (100y + 10z + x) + (100z+ 10x + y) ...(i)
= 100x + 10x + x + 10y + 100y + y + z + 100z + 10Z
111x + 111y+ 111z = 111(x + y + z)
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 37 {tex}\times{/tex} (x + y + z)
Hence, Eq. (i) is divisible by 37, but not divisible by 11, 33 and 74.

questions.question: A four-digit number aabb is dividisble by 55. Then, possible value(s) of b is /are
questions.explanation: It is given that, aabb is divisible by 55. Then, it is also divisible by 5. Now, if a number is divisible by 5, then its unit digit is either 0 or 5. Hence, the possible values of b are 0 and 5.

questions.question: Let abc be a three-digit number. Then, abc + bca + cab is not divisible by
questions.explanation:
We know that, the sum of three-digit numbers taken in cyclic order can be written as 111 (a + b + c).
I.e. abc + bca + cab = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 37 {tex}\times{/tex} (a + b + c)
Hence, the sum is divisible by 3, 37 and (a + b + c) but not divisible by 9

questions.question: A four-digit number 4ab5 is divisible by 55. Then, the value of b - a is
questions.explanation:
Given, a four-digit number 4ab5 is divisible by 55. Then, it is also divisible by 11.
The difference of sum of its digits in odd places and sum of its digits in even places is either 0 or multiple of 11.
i.e. (4 + b) - (a + 5) is 0 or a multiple of 11 , if 4 + b - a - 5 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b - a = 1

questions.question: If abc is a three-digit number, then number abc - a - b - c is divisible by
questions.explanation:
We can write a three digit number in the form: abc =  100a + 10b + c
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  abc - a - 5 - c = (100a + 10b + c ) - a - b - c
= 100a - a + 10b - b
= 99a + 9b = 9(11a + b)
Hence, the given number abc - a - b - c is divisible by 9.

questions.question: If the sum of digits of a number is divisible by three, then the number is always divisible by
questions.explanation: We know that, if sum of digits of a number is divisible by three, then the number must be divisible by 3, i.e. the remainder obtained by dividing the number by 3 is same as the remainder obtained by dividing the sum of its digits by 3.

questions.question: If x +y + z = 6 and z is an odd digit, then the three-digit number xyz is
questions.explanation: We have, x + y + z= 6 and z is an odd digit. Since sum of the digits is divisible by 3, it will also be divisible by 2 and 3 but unit digit is odd, so it is divisible by 3 only, Hence, the number is an odd multiple of 3.

questions.question: If 5A + B3 = 65, then the values of A and B is
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\frac{5 A+B 3}{65}{/tex} 
Evidently, A + 3 is a number taking values from 3 to 12. So, either A + 3 is 5 or it is a two-digit number whose unit digit is 5. But, A + 3 is less than or equal to 12.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A + 3 = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 2
In the tens column, we have 5 + B = 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 1
Hence, A = 2 and B = 1

questions.question: If 5A {tex}\times{/tex} A = 399, then the value of A is
questions.explanation:
We have, 5A {tex}\times{/tex} A = 399
Here, 5A {tex}\times{/tex} A = 9 i.e. A {tex}\times{/tex} A is the number 9 or a number whose unit’s digit is 9. Thus, the number whose product with itself produces a two-digit number having its unit’s digit as 9 is 7.
i.e. A {tex}\times{/tex} A = 49 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A=7
Now, 5 {tex}\times{/tex} A + 4 = 39
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 + 4 = 39
39 = 39
So, A satisfies the product.
Hence, the value of A is 7

questions.question: If A3 + 8B = 150, then the value of A + B is
questions.explanation:
We have, A3 + 8B = 150
Here, 3 + B = 0, so 3 + B is a two-digit number whose unit’s digit is zero.
3 + B = 10 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 7 and 1 carry
Now, considering ten’s column, A + 8 + 1 = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A + 9 = 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 6
Hence, A + B = 6 + 7
= 13

questions.question: Generalised form of a three-digit number xyz is
questions.explanation:
In general, any three-digit number xyz can be written as,
xyz = 100 {tex}\times{/tex} x + 10 {tex}\times{/tex} y + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} z
= 100x + 10y + z
where x is a hundredth place digit, y is a ten’s place digit and z is a unit’s place digit. Hence, if it’s a three-digit number, the places will be ones, tens, and hundreds from right to left.

questions.question: The usual form of 100a + b + 10c is
questions.explanation:
In general, the usual form of 100a + b + 10c is
acb = 100 {tex}\times{/tex} a + 10 {tex}\times{/tex} c + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} b = 100a + 10c + b
or after rearranging 100a + b + 10c = acb

questions.question: If 5 {tex}\times{/tex} A = CA then the values of A and C are
questions.explanation:
5 {tex}\times{/tex} A = CA
A = 5, C = 2
5{tex}\times{/tex}5 = 25

questions.question: If 5A + 25 is equal to B2, then the value of A + B is
questions.explanation:
If 5A + 25 = B2
here A + 5 = 2 i.e. two digit number. so, A = 7 and 1 carrya
57 + 25 = 82 so, B = 8
hence A + B = 7 + 8
= 15

questions.question: If 6A {tex}\times{/tex} B = A8B, then the value of A – B is
questions.explanation:
6A {tex}\times{/tex} B = A8B
A{tex}\times{/tex}B = B and 6{tex}\times{/tex}B = A8
Therefore, A = 1 and B = 3
61{tex}\times{/tex}3 = 183
Hence, A - B = 1 - 3 = - 2

questions.question: The sum of all three digit numbers, which when divided by 5, gives a remainder of 2, is
questions.explanation:
The nos. 102, 107,... 197 are in A.P
a = 102
d = 5
l = 997
997 = 102 + (n - 1) {tex}\times{/tex} 5
997 = 102 + 5n - 5
5n = 991 - 97
5n = 900
n = {tex}\frac{900}{8}{/tex}
n = 180
Sum required = {tex}\frac{180}{2}{/tex}[102 + 997]
= 98910

questions.question: Kaushal gets 3 marks for every correct solution and losses 2 marks for every wrong solution. He solves 30 questions and gets 40 marks. How many correct question did Kaushal solve?
questions.explanation:
Total marks of 30 questions of Kaushal = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 90 Marks
Missing marks = 90 - 40
= 50
For 1 wrong answer = 3 + 2
i.e., 5 marks are deducted
No. of wrong answers = {tex}\frac{50}{5}{/tex}
= 10
No. of correct answers = 30 - 10
= 20

questions.question: On three different crossing the traffic lights change their colours after 24 second, 36 second and 54 seconds respectively. If they change their colour all together at 10 : 15 : 00 am, then at what time further they will change their colour all together?
questions.explanation:
Time taken = LCM of 24, 36 and 54

2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 = 216 sec.
= 3 min 36 seconds
Required time
= 10 : 15 : 00 + 3 : 36
= 10 : 18 : 36 a.m.

questions.question: The length of a largest square shaped tile which is used in 5 m 44 cm long and 3 m 74 cm broad floor of a room, is-
questions.explanation:
Length of floor = 5 m 44 cm
= 544 cm
width of floor = 3 m 74 cm
= 374 cm
Their HCF = (16 {tex}\times{/tex} 34, 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 34)
= 34 cm
Required side of tile = 34 cm

questions.question: The traffic light of three different routes changes at 48, 72 & 108 seconds respectively. If it changes at 8 : 20 : 00 together, then at what time it will change together?
questions.explanation:
LCM of 48, 72 & 108

= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= 432 sec.
= 7 : 12 minutes

questions.question: What is the smallest number, in which when 2 is added, then the total is divided completely by 24, 36, 45 and 60?
questions.explanation:
LCM of 24, 36, 45 & 60

= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
= 360

questions.question: If 53x-1 {tex}\div{/tex} 25 = 125, then the value of x, is
questions.explanation:
We have, 53x-1 {tex}\div{/tex} 25 = 125
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 53x -1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{25}{/tex} = 125 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{5^{11}}{5} \times \frac{1}{5 \times 5}{/tex} = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}5^{3 x} \times \frac{1}{5^3}{/tex} = 53
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 53x = 53 {tex}\times{/tex} 53 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 53x = 53+1 = 5k
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 6 [{tex}\because{/tex} bases are same]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}6 \times \frac{1}{3}{/tex} = 2

questions.question: The average of 9 numbers is 30. Average of first five numbers is 25, and last 3 numbers, average is 35. What will be the 6th number?
questions.explanation:
Average of 9 nos. = 30
sum of 9 nos. = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
= 270
Sum of first 5 nos. = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 25
= 125
Sum of last 3 nos. = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 35
= 105
Sixth no. = 270 - (125 + 105)
= 270 - 230
= 40

questions.question: Out of the following rational numbers, which is the smallest?
questions.explanation:
Here, the denominators of all rational numbers are equal. The number is the smallest, which the number has the greatest negative integer numerator.
Hence, the smallest rational number is {tex}\frac{-5}{7}{/tex}.

questions.question: Six bells begin to toll together at the interval of 10 s, 15 sec, 20 sec, 25 sec, 30 sec, 35 sec respectively, then how many times will they toll together in the span of 7 h?
questions.explanation:
We have to find the LCM of 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35

LCM = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 2100
In 7 hour (7 {tex}\times{/tex} 3600 sec ), then the ring will toll together = {tex}\frac{7 \times 3600}{2100}{/tex} = 12 times

questions.question: What will be the sum of all the numbers between 200 and 600, which will be divisible by 16?
questions.explanation:
Least no. to be divisible by 16 = 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 208
Largest no. to be divisible by 16
= 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 37 ... 593
Sum of the nos. = 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 + 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 14 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 + ... + 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 37
= 16(13 + 14 + 15 + ... + 37)
= 16[11 + 2 + ... + 37) - (1 + 2 + ... 12)]
= {tex}16(\frac{37 \times 28}{2}-\frac{1 . \times 13}{2}){/tex}
= 16(703 - 78)
= 10000

questions.question:
What value will come at the below place of question mark?
0.08 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5 + 0.9 =?
questions.explanation:
0.08 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5 + 0.9 = ?
? = 0.04 + 0.9
? = 0.94

questions.question:

The 94.5% of 550 = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}550 \times \frac{94.5}{100}{/tex} = ?

? = 519.75

questions.question: If a number 732*5 is divisible by 9 then the digit at * will be:
A number is divisible by 3 if:

options.option: the sum of digits of the number is divisible by 3
the sum of digits of the number is divisible by 1
the sum of digits of the number is divisible by 5
the sum of digits of the number is divisible by 2

questions.explanation: the sum of digits of the number is divisible by 3

questions.question: The number (10n - 1) is divisible by 11 for ________.
questions.explanation: (10n - 1) is divisible by 11 for even values of n as 102 - 1 = 99, 104 - 1 = 9999, 106 - 1 = 999999, etc. are divisible by 11.

questions.question: The values of A and B in the given addition respectively are ________.
The greatest value that must be given to x so that the number 7713 x 8 is divisible by 4 is ________.
questions.explanation: 7713 x 8 is divisible by 4 if its last two digits is divisible by 4. So, we have x8 to be divisible by 4. Therefore, greatest value of x is 8.

questions.question: If  where A and B are single digit numbers, then A + B = ________.
questions.explanation:
Step 1 : A {tex}\times{/tex} A = 9
Two cases arises
Case 1: 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 9 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 3
Case 2: 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 49 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 7
Step 2: Case 1 : If A = 3, then the product will be 343 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 1029
Hence, B = 0
Case 2: If A = 7, then the product will be 747 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 5229 (not possible)
Hence, A = 3 and B = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A + B = 3

questions.question: Which of the following numbers is divisible by 8?
Given that the number 1771*61 is divisible by 11, where * is single digit number, what are the possible values of *?
questions.explanation:
If 1771*61 is divisible by 11, then (7 + 1 + 6) - (1 + 7 + * + 1) is either 0 or a multiple of 11
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14 - (9 + *) is either 0 or a multiple of 11
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15 - * is either 0 or a multiple of 11
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 5 - * = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} * = 5

questions.question: The largest natural number by which the product of three consecutive even natural numbers is always divisible, is ________.
questions.explanation: The required number would be the product of three smallest even natural numbers i.e., 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 48.

questions.question: If in a number, difference between the sum of digits at its odd places and that of digits at the even places is given 0, then the number is divisible by ________.
A 5-digit number xy235 is divisible by 3 such that x + y < 5, where x and y are single digits, then possible values of (x, y) are ________.
questions.explanation:
xy235 is divisible by 3 so sum of its digits is also divisible by 3.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y + 2 + 3 + 5 = x + y + 10 is divisible by 3 and x + y < 5.
So possible values of (x, y) are (1, 1) or (2, 0).

questions.question: If  and there is no carry on addition, then the value of B is ________.
If N divided by 5 leaves a remainder 0, then one’s digit of N must be ________.
questions.explanation:
N leaves remainder 0 when divided by 5.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} N is divisible by 5.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} One's digit of N is either 0 or 5.

questions.question: Given that the number 67y19 is divisible by 9, where y is a single digit, what is the least possible value of y?
questions.explanation:
67y19 is divisible by 9 so sum of its digits is also divisible by 9.
6 + 7 + y + 1 + 9 = 23 + y is divisible by 9.
So, least possible value of y = 4.

questions.question: A 3-digit number cba is divisible by 3 if ________.
questions.explanation: a + b + c is divisible by 3

questions.question: If , then the value of B is ________.
In a division, the divisor is 12 times the quotient and 5 times the remainder. If the remainder is 48, then dividend is ________.
questions.explanation:
Remainder = 48
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Divisor = 48 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 240
Quotient = {tex}\frac{240}{12}{/tex} = 20
{tex}\because{/tex} Dividend = Divisor {tex}\times{/tex} Quotient + Remainder
= 240 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 + 48 = 4800 + 48 = 4848.

questions.question: Taking today as zero on the number line, if the day before yesterday is 11 May, what is the date 2 days after day after tomorrow?
questions.explanation: 16th May

questions.question: How many 5-digit numbers of the form AABAA is divisible by 33?
questions.explanation:
We have AABAA is divisible by 33.
So, it is divisible by both 3 and 11.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A + B + A - (A + A) = B is divisible by 11.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 0
Also, A + A + B + A + A = 4A + B is divisible by 3.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4A is divisible by 3 ({tex}\because{/tex} B = 0)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A is divisible by 3
Hence, possible values of A are 0, 3, 6, 9
But A can’t be equal to zero.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of possible 5-digit numbers are 3.

questions.question: A 3-digit number cba is divisible by 9 if ________.
questions.explanation: A number is divisible by 9 if the sum of its digits is divisible by 9.

questions.question: {tex}\overline{31 x}{/tex} is divisible by 2 if x =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\overline{31 x}{/tex} is divisible by 2 if the unite digit of
number is 0, 2, 4, 6 or 8.

questions.question: If a 3-digit number abc is divisible by 11, then ________.

a - b - c is a multiple of 11
a + b + c is a multiple of 11
a - b + c is a multiple of 11

questions.explanation:
A number is divisible by 11 if the difference of the sum of the digits
at odd places and the sum of the digits at even places is 0 or a multiple of 11.
Here the number is abc so, we check the difference (c + a) - (b) for divisibility by 11.

questions.question: The digits number {tex}\overline{a b}{/tex} when reversed can be expressed as
questions.explanation:
{tex}\overline{a b}{/tex} when digits reversed can be writen as
{tex}\overline{b a}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\overline{b a}{/tex} = 10b + a.

questions.question: {tex}A \times A = {3 A}{/tex} when A
questions.explanation: 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 36 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A = 6.

questions.question: A three - digit number 42x is divisible by 9. Find the value of x.
questions.explanation:
Since 42x is divisible by 9, the sum of its
digits, i.e. 4 + 2 + x must be divisible by 9.
i.e. 6 + ;x is divisible by 9
i.e. 6 + x = 9 or 18, ________.

x= 3.

questions.question: Find the value of A and B if
questions.explanation:
Form ones column A + 4 gives a number whose ones digit is 2.
So, A = 8. The value of B can be obtained by solving 2 + B is a number whose ones digit is 1. So, B = 9

questions.question: Iy3y6 is divisible by 11, then the value of y is:
questions.explanation:
A number is divisible by 11,
if the difference of the sum of its digits in odd
places and the sum of its digits in even
places is either 0 or a multiple of 11.
Given number = Iy3y6
Sum of the digits in odd places - Sum of
the digits in even places
0 = (y + y) - (1 + 3 + 6)
0 = 2y - 10
y = {tex}\frac{10}{2}{/tex}
y = 5.

questions.question: The number 348 is divisible by each of the following numbers except ________
questions.explanation:
In the number 348 the digit at the unit place is 8.
So, it is not divisible by 5.

questions.question: Suppose that the division x {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder 4 and the division x {tex}\div{/tex} 2 leaves a remainder 1. Find the ones digit of x.
questions.explanation:
Since x {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder 4, so ones
digit of x can be 4 or 9. Also, since x {tex}\div{/tex} 2
leaves a remainder 1, so ones digit must be 9 only.

questions.question: 1x3y6 is a five digit number where x, y are digits and y exceeds x by 6. If this number is divisible by 18, then the value of {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
1x3y6 is divisible by 18 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}& divisible by 9
1 + x + 3+ y + 6 = 10 + x + y(x + y = 8)
y - x = 6 ...(1)
[ Given in the problem ]
y + x = 8 ...(2)
Subtracting (1) from (2)
y + x = 8
y- x = 6
2y = 14
y = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1
{tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} = 7

questions.question: Unit’s digit in the number (12357)655 is
questions.explanation:
(12357)
unit digit of (12357)655

= unit digit is (1 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) = unit digit is 3.

questions.question: If aabb is a four digit number and also a perfect square then the value of a + b is

= 1000a + 100a + 10b+ b
= 1100a + 11b
= 11 (100a + b)

100a + b should be of the type
11 x k2 where k is natural number
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The possible values of 11 x k2 can be:
aabb are 121 x 16
121 {tex}\times{/tex} 25
121 {tex}\times{/tex} 36
121 {tex}\times{/tex} 49 neglected
121 {tex}\times{/tex} 64
So, 7744 is the given four digit number
and 7 + 4 = 11.

questions.question: 72 - 27 is divisible by
questions.explanation:
Since the difference of the two digit number
i .e., ab and number obtained by reversing its digit
